observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the CGSO:

Prepare the IIRUP and initiate the disposal procedures for the unserviceable PPE items following the procedures prescribed by the abovementioned law, rules and regulations and coordinate with the CAACO for the proper derecognition in the books; and

Ensure that all government properties which are no longer serviceable are disposed of at the earliest opportunity to prevent accumulation of such properties, avoid further deterioration and reduction of their economic value.
observations.managementAction:
Issuance of Executive Order No. 31. Series of 2024 re: An Order Reconstituting the for the Disposition of Unserviceable or Obsolescent Supply or Property of the Local Government Unit of the Island Garden City of Samal Disposal.

An orientation of COA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 re: Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government properties was conducted last June 11, 2024.

The committee agreed to conduct a disposal of unserviceable properties through public auction that will be fully realized on or before the last quarter of this year.
observations.reason: Although a Disposal Committee has been duly constituted, no disposal activities were undertaken during the current year. As a result, unserviceable properties continue to accumulate, and their prolonged retention may lead to further deterioration, potentially diminishing their salvage value.

observations.recommendation:
a)	Avail of the remedies provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, the one-time cleansing of PPE. This proactive step will facilitate the reconciliation of discrepancies, resulting in reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability. Moreover, it will establish the validity and accuracy of the recorded properties, thereby ensuring the fairness of presentation of the PPE accounts in the Financial Statements (FSs). 

b)	All Departments/ Offices to support and coordinate with the CAO, CGSO and the Inventory Committee in facilitating the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation of the property accounts to establish actual existence, ownership, and condition of the PPEs assigned to their respective department /office.

Avoid giving additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not yet settled their previous cash advances. Please strictly follow the guidelines provided under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid recurring noncompliance.

Take appropriate action against accountable officers who have long outstanding cash advances, especially those who are still connected with the agency. Encourage them to immediately settle their accounts according to the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory accounts all purchases of materials and supplies upon acceptance of the delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or distribution; 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer (in the absence of a designated General Services Officer) to ensure that all issuances are supported with Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) which shall be summarized in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and shall then be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the corresponding expense; and

Observe proper recording of transactions by referring to the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to provide a fair financial statement presentation.
observations.reason:
Not all purchases of supplies and materials were recorded under the inventory account. Most were recorded as outright expense.

The Municipal Treasurer had not prepared SSMI for the issuance of supplies.

Proper recording on the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials was inconsistently observed by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
3.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the:
3.9.1	City Treasurer submit the Schedule of Unreleased Checks to the City Accountant especially at year end;
3.9.2	City Accountant do the remedial measures to attain fairness of presentation of the Cash and Cash Equivalent, in conformity with Paragraph 76 of IPSAS 1; and
3.9.3	City Accountant discontinue the inclusion of unreleased checks among its outstanding checks in preparing its Bank Reconciliation Statements.

Recommendations:
4.10	We recommended to Management the following remedial measures:
4.10.1	To require the City General Service Officer to prepare an updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 379 of RA No. 7160 to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal;
4.10.2	To direct the City Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to derecognize the unserviceable equipment as prescribed under PPSAS No. 17 after IIRUP is duly approved and evaluated by COA.

Recommendations:
5.13	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the CAccO:
5.13.1	rectify the misclassification of expenses by reclassifying the identified transactions amounting to ₱823,276.00 to their appropriate accounts in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.; and
5.13.2	institute a system of periodic review and validation of all recorded expenses to ensure proper classification based on the actual nature of transactions. City Accounting Office (CAccO) to implement a dual-verification process, where accounting personnel cross-check expense classifications against supporting documents (e.g., disbursement vouchers, purchase orders) before recording.
Mandate the regular reconciliation of the Obligation Request (OBR) expense account with the general ledger to detect and correct misclassifications promptly.
Provide training to accounting personnel on the correct application of IPSAS 1 and the Revised Chart of Accounts under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to reinforce compliance and prevent future errors.
Enforce the proper accounting treatment for the procurement of welfare goods by initially recognizing them as inventory under the "Welfare Goods for Distribution" account instead of recording them as outright expenses.

Recommendation:
6.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to prepare and record the appropriate Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to correct the balances of the Due to NGAs and Trust Liability accounts by reclassifying the amount of ₱2,487,960.87, with the adjustment to be completed and reported to the Local Chief Executive within 30 days from receipt of this recommendation to ensure the accuracy of financial records and compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-09.

Recommendations:
7.9	We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
7.9.1	direct the City Accounting’s Office (CAccO), in coordination with the concerned implementing offices, shall conduct a full reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account on or before April 17, 2025, to determine the exact amounts payable to each source barangay, ensuring accuracy and transparency through supporting documentation; and
7.9.2	upon completion of the reconciliation, the CAccO facilitate the remittance of all unexpended funds within 15 days to the respective source barangays, in strict compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and other applicable financial regulations. A report confirming the remittance, including official receipts and acknowledgment from recipients, shall be submitted to the audit team within 10 days after completion of the disbursement.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
ensure that the target completion date for the inspection of all PPEs which is initially set on June 2025 will be met; and
avail of the remedies provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, the one-time cleansing of PPE. This proactive step will facilitate the reconciliation of discrepancies, resulting in reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability. Moreover, it will establish the validity and accuracy of the recorded properties, thereby ensuring the fairness of presentation of the PPE accounts in the Financial Statements (FSs).

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the CAO and CTO to issue a certification stating that Trust Funds amounting to P1,611,929.68 have indeterminate purposes and to recommend to the LFC for the transfer of these funds to the General Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
to formulate a remediation plan with established deadlines for each part of the procurement process in order to create accountability within and among the Offices and fully implement all prevention and mitigation PPAs included in the LDRRMF for CY 2024;
direct Implementing Offices to identify prevention and mitigation PPAs with realistic timelines and resource allocation by conducting thorough planning and scheduling of future projects and activities; and
address challenges encountered all throughout the procurement process, before submitting the LDRRMFIP to ensure that future projects are executed effectively and within the specified period.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
formulate a catch-up plan which is aimed to establish deadlines for each Office to prepare the necessary documents/reports in order to create accountability within and among the Offices;
strategically formulate project priorities, taking into account their feasibility and the capacity of the implementing offices to effectively manage the implementation of all budgeted PPAs within the year; and
promptly reassess unimplemented PPAs to determine their continued relevance and alignment with the current needs of local constituents. Revert the funds of projects if deemed unattainable and unfeasible as early as possible.

The PEO shall either immediately submit to the Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) the lacking supporting documents of the projects cited above. PEO and PACCO to reconcile their reports and records on the updated status of implement projects for the latter to affect the necessary journal entries for the reclassification in the accounting books. PEO and PACCO to coordinate, in writing, on the timely submission of the required attachments such as but not limited to the certificate of completion, acceptance and turnover of completed projects to avoid recurrence of this issue in the future. 1. PEO and PACCO to coordinate in writing for timely submission of required documents (certificate of completion, acceptance, and turnover). 2. Assign specific staff from PEO and PACCO to handle document exchange. 3. Create a checklist of required attachments for completed projects.

The implementing offices of programs and projects funded through transfers from NGAs reassess their strategies and establish a written guideline to ensure the prompt implementation of identified programs and projects. Additionally, determine in a timely manner the viability of executing these initiatives which projects are no longer feasible for these reasons the implementation and to confirm the balances of completed projects. Strengthening Controls - To avoid recurrence, we are instituting closer coordination between PTO, PACCO and the implementing departments to ensure that project funds are monitored, and unutilized balances are identified for timely return. Provincial Treasurer return to the respective source agencies those funds intended for programs and projects that are determined to be unimplementable as well as those remaining balances for the completed projects.

The implementing offices or end-users promptly prepare the RIS for the utilized inventory items and submit the same to the PGSO. The PGSO to prepare the RSMI for submission to PACCO every time the PGSO receives RIS from end-user offices. To issue Memo to all department heads to designate responsible personnel as Supply/Property Custodian. The PACCO to prepare JV to record the issuances based on the RSMI from the PGSO and record utilization. The Inventory Team to conduct a physical count of construction materials inventory to confirm its existence and to report the accurate balance of the account.

The Management demand from the respective contractors to speed up the accomplishment of the mentioned unfinished projects. If meeting the target date is not possible due to justifiable reasons, an appropriate request for an extension should be filed by them in accordance with the provision under RA 9184. Require contractors to file request for extension if the target date cannot be met. The Provincial Engineer regularly evaluate the status of all projects undertaken by the Province. PPMC conduct regular monitoring and evaluation on the status of implementation of major infrastructure projects, and report to the Honorable Governor the results thereof. Submit monitoring and evaluation reports to the governor for appropriate action.

The PDRRMO ensure monitoring of the allocation and utilization of DRRM fund in the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRMF, and improve in terms of percentage and amount of utilization of DRRM funds for CY 2025 and the ensuing years. The PDRRMO coordinate, in writing, with the appropriate offices/departments in the province to avoid delay in the processing of documents such as purchase requests.

We recommended to the Provincial Governor to direct the Provincial Assessor, in coordination with the other concerned provincial offices/departments, hasten the preparation and completion of the SMV and Data gathering and Issuance of Field Appraisal and Assessment Sheets and follow the calendar of activities (see attached list from the Manual) to ensure early effectivity of the long overdue new general revision of real property assessments of the province. The Provincial Assessor coordinate with her counterpart municipal assessors, in writing, to ensure that the municipalities are in line with provincial government's goal of full implementation of the new general revision within the period provided by the guidelines.

PEO - Planning Division submit immediately to the BAC the POW, ABC and Approved Plans/Drawing of the abovementioned projects. The PEO-Planning Division shall: (1) As part of the purchase request, provide the POW, ABC and Approved Plans/Drawings and other required/necessary attachments before posting/advertising. (2) Implement a monitoring system to track projects undertaken in relation to the submission of corresponding contracts and supporting documents to the BAC. This system should be formally documented and submitted to the Local Chief Executive (LCE), and copy furnished to COA. 1. Submission of all approved contracts and the complete supporting documents of all projects undertaken within five (5) days from the execution of those contracts, as prescribed by COA Circular Nos.2012-001 and 2009-001. (2) submit a quarterly reporting to ensure proper audit trail and accountability.

PACCO to effect the necessary accounting journal entries to reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate PPE account. PEO and PACCO to coordinate in writing for timely submission of required documents (certificate of completion, acceptance, and turnover). Assign specific staff from PEO and PACCO to handle document exchange. Create a checklist of required attachments for completed projects.

Provincial Engineer to evaluate the status of each project herein by conducting site visits and progress reviews to identify the best course of action to hasten the full implementation of the projects, including the imposition of liquidated damages to contractors for delays incurred. PEO and PACCO immediate actions to expedite project completion, including the imposition of liquidated damages on contractors responsible for unjustified delays, ensuring accountability and adherence to project timelines.

Implementing Offices (PEO and PAGRO) to conduct strategic planning for the fast implementation of the identified projects/programs and jointly conduct strategic planning to assess and fast track project implementation. Provincial Treasurer to return to the respective grantor those funds intended for projects deemed unimplementable and unimplementable and those balances of completed projects before preparation of all DV.

We recommended to the Provincial Governor to direct the Provincial Assessor, in coordination with the offices concerned, to hasten the preparation and completion of the Field Appraisal and Assessment Sheets and follow the calendar of activities (see attached list from the Manual) to ensure early effectivity of the long overdue general revision. Continuous coordination and monitoring to sustain implementation and compliance of RA 12001 (Real Property Valuation and Assessment Reform Act).

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to review and account for all tangible items below P50,000.00, acquired and issued prior to CY 2024, which have been classified as PPE and charge/expense their carrying amounts to Prior Period Adjustment pursuant to Paragraph 3.7 of COA Circular 2024-006;
Municipal Accountant to close the accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment losses of these properties in the books of accounts; and
Municipal Accountant to facilitate the prompt update of the accounting software to incorporate the new semi-expendable accounts to be used in recording semi-expendable properties and reclassify those recorded under the Other Supplies Inventory account accordingly.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations and the Management agreed:
for the OIC-Municipal Accountant to promptly verify the nature/ purpose of the funds to determine the status of the projects covered by such funds and to coordinate with the source agencies in reconciling the balances with their records for ongoing projects with remaining balances in the books, and prepare the necessary correcting entries, if any, to arrive at the accurate balance of the Due to NGAs account as at reporting date;
for the Heads of Implementing Offices to determine if the remaining amounts pertain to unutilized portion of the funds received after project completion and advise the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to cause the remittance/return of such to the source agencies or to prepare a letter request allowing the transfer of the unutilized amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund for the LGU’s utilization subject to the approval of the donor agency; and
for the Municipal Treasurer to cause the remittance of the dormant accounts to the source agencies or transfer to the General Fund after verification by the Municipal Accountant and the Heads of Implementing Offices.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDO, MACCO and MTO to coordinate and re-evaluate the status of the projects and the non-moving accounts, and revert the balances to the General Fund or the National Treasury, whichever is applicable, in order to provide the government of additional resources to support its various programs and activities.

Recommendations:
1.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and GASO agreed to:
1.8.1 undertake collaborative efforts in reconciling the variance between the
balances of the PPE accounts recorded in the books and those reported
in the RPCPPE; and effect the necessary adjustment/s to come up with
the correct balances; and
1.8.2 ensure maintenance of complete and updated PPELC and PCs for all
PPE and reconcile regularly both records to come up with a reliable
balance of the PPE as at year end.

Recommendations:
2.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
2.10.1 Municipal Mayor instructs the implementing offices concerned to
immediately submit the documents evidencing the completion of the
aforementioned projects to the Accounting Office and to regularly
furnish the latter with the said documents whenever projects are 100%
completed;
2.10.2 Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify
the completed projects from the Construction-in-Progress accounts to
their appropriate PPE account if the necessary documents are already at
hand, provide the related depreciation expenses and capitalize the total
amount recognized as expense in CY 2023 and 2024; and
2.10.3 implementing office support the disbursement vouchers with complete
documentation, including the entire Program of Works of the project if
the project is implemented by phase, to guide the accounting personnel
on its proper recording.

Recommendations:
3.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
3.10.1 Municipal Treasurer
3.10.1.1 negotiate with the DBP Manager for the possible restoration
of cash deducted as payment for the penalties;
3.10.1.2 assess the necessity of maintaining multiple bank accounts,
particularly those with minimal balances, especially the
account that continuously incurs penalties, and to facilitate
the immediate closure of such account/s; and
3.10.1.3 record the unrecorded deposits of P14,147.88 in the cashbook.
3.10.2 Municipal Accountant submit the bank credit memos for the
unrecorded deposits totaling P14,147.88 and to prepare adjusting
entries to reflect the correct cash balance in the books of accounts once
verified; and
3.10.3 responsible official refund the penalties incurred that were
inappropriately charged against the Municipality’s fund, if
negotiation fails.

Recommendations:
4.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
4.10.1 Municipal Accountant and GASO maintain updated and complete
Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) and Stock Cards (SC), respectively, for
each commodity/stock;
4.10.2 Inventory Committee conducts periodic physical count of inventories and
submits RCPI, regardless of the balance, to the Accounting Office and
Office of the Auditor; and
4.10.3 Municipal Accountant ensure timely recording of the issuance of supplies
and materials based on RSMI submitted by the GASO and to reconcile
regularly the balance of SLC with SC and RCPI.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee responsible for the periodic physical inventory of all the supplies and materials of the Municipality to ascertain their completeness;
for the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate weekly the Requisition and    Issue Slip using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit to the Accounting Office together with the original Requisition and Issue Slip for recording; and
for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the books.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
fast track the full validation of the identified reconciling items for preparation of necessary adjustments and journal entries in the books;
make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the cash in bank balance and recognize the corresponding liability for the stale checks;and
trace the nature of the balances reflected in the closed bank accounts and submit to this Office the results thereof together with certifications from the bank on the existence of the balances such that an evaluation may be made on whether losses were incurred and who shall be responsible for such losses. After verification, record the adjusting entries in the books to reflect the correct ending balance of Cash in Bank account.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations:
for the Municipal Accountant to hasten the verification on the nature/ purpose of these funds to determine the status of the projects covered by such funds;
for the Heads of Implementing Offices to determine if the remaining amounts pertain to unutilized portion of the funds received after project completion and advise the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to cause the remittance/return of such to the source agencies or to prepare a letter request allowing the transfer of the unutilized amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund for the LGU’s utilization subject to the approval of the donor agency; and
for ongoing projects with remaining balances in the books, for the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the source agencies to reconcile the balances with their records and prepare the necessary correcting entries (if any) to arrive at the accurate balance of the Due to NGAs account as at reporting date.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Mayor initiate and propose legislative measures to the Sangguniang Bayan in authorizing the former to represent the Municipality in executing a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement to transfer the identified PPE items;
Office of the Municipal Mayor ensure proper documentation for the transfer of PPE items to the respective barangays/offices upon securing authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the PPE items from the books of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Accountant to record all consummated procurement of supplies and materials thru the inventory account based on the Acceptance and Inspection Report together with the supporting documents and all issuances through the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued with the original Requisition and Issue Slip; and to properly follow the steps in recording the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method;
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, as required in Sections 119 and 120 of NGAS Manual Volume I for local government units; and
the Municipal Treasurer to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials every end of the semester and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the records and to the Audit Team.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant should:
enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability; and
request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant closely coordinate in updating the utilization of LDRRMF to determine the unexpended balance of the fund and transfer the said balance immediately from General Fund to the Trust Fund on or before the last banking day of the year. The same should be recorded before closing the books of accounts as at year end. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant shall submit the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct  the:
Municipal Accountant to review and account for all tangible items below P50,000.00, acquired and issued prior to CY 2024, which have been classified as PPE and charge/expense their carrying amounts to Prior Period Adjustment pursuant to Paragraph 3.7 of COA Circular 2024-006;
Municipal Accountant to close the accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment losses of these properties in the books of accounts; and
Municipal Accountant to facilitate the prompt update of the accounting software to incorporate the new semi-expendable accounts to be used in recording semi-expendable properties and reclassify those recorded under the Other Supplies Inventory account accordingly.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the incorporation of the appropriate inventory and expense account titles for semi-expendable properties as provided in the Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2024-006 into the accounting software, and subsequently reclassify the inventory in its proper account titles and make necessary adjustments recognizing the corresponding expenses.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
for the MPDC and the MEO to coordinate, strictly monitor and evaluate the implementation of all priority development projects within the budget year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF;
for the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that development projects arising from debt servicing can be paid without opting to reverse the appropriated funds of other development fund projects; and to prioritize the allocation of funds for the implementation of proposed development projects in the LDIP; and
for the MPDC to provide a copy of the Municipal Development Council Execom Resolutions to highlight the realignment of the reverted projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
for the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) in coordination with the MPDO and MBO to conduct careful planning and identification of activities to be included in the GPB;
for the MPDO to refrain from the practice of reverting the GAD Budget and allocating it to other programs, and submit a written justification of the reversion of GAD budget; and
for the GAD Focal Person to observe prompt submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRM Officer should judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRMMF, incorporating feasible time schedules, availability of resources, and other contingencies to avoid delays in the implementation of PPAs. Further, the MDRRMO is also enjoined to improve the utilization rate of LDRRMF and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs in order to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for, responding to, and recovering from the impact of disasters.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
for the Municipal Accountant to record all procurement of supplies and materials thru the inventory account based on the Acceptance and Inspection Report together with the supporting documents and all issuances through Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued with the original Requisition and Issue Slip;
for the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, as required in Sections 119 and 120 of NGAS Manual Volume I for local government units; and
for the Municipal Treasurer to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials every end of the semester and submit a Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the records and to the Audit Team.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Mayor initiate and propose legislative measures to the Sangguniang Bayan in authorizing the former to represent the Municipality in executing a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement to transfer the identified PPE items;
Office of the Municipal Mayor ensure proper documentation for the transfer of PPE items to the respective barangays/offices upon securing authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the PPE items from the books of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to review and account for all tangible items below P50,000.00, acquired and issued prior to CY 2024, which have been classified as PPE and charge/expense their carrying amounts to Prior Period Adjustment pursuant to Paragraph 3.7 of COA Circular 2024-006;
Municipal Accountant to close the accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment losses of these properties in the books of accounts; and
Municipal Accountant to facilitate the prompt update of the accounting software to incorporate the new semi-expendable accounts to be used in recording semi-expendable properties.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDO, MACCO and MTO to coordinate and re-evaluate the status of the projects and the non-moving accounts, and revert the balances to the General Fund or the National Treasurer, whichever is applicable, in order to provide the government of additional resources to support its various programs and activities.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman and TWG Head meticulously verify the eligibility requirements and ensure all supporting documents are prepare and submitted were properly validated, verify whether the winning bidder’s line of business as stated in its permit aligns with the nature of procurement, and review and validate the contents in the Post-Qualification Report and Resolution before submission to the Head of the Procuring Entity.
Likewise, submit a written justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies for further evaluation by the audit team.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant  establish a payable account for the deduction of GSIS contributions pending municipal employees’ inclusion in the GSIS system to ensure that employee’s deductions are properly accounted for, thereby guaranteeing the accuracy of their net take-home pay; and
Human Resource Office properly monitor and facilitate the process of including municipal employees in the GSIS system.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the:
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer  have better coordination in planning and implementing the 20% development fund projects, making sure that these are aligned based on the project timelines; and
Municipal Engineer collaborate in ensuring sufficient budget is allocated to the identified development projects to prevent non-implementation caused by budget constraints.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the OIC-MBO review the budget allocation for proposed programs and projects in the AIP and ensure that the funding requirements are classified under the proper expenditure categories (i.e. PS, MOOE, Capital Outlay).

Recommendations:
We recommended that the MDRRMC, MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer strictly follow the guidelines in the utilization of LDRRM Fund to ensure attainment of the Fund’s objectives.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the improper capitalization of expense account and to submit the corresponding Journal Voucher/s to the COA Office;
MACCO coordinate with the MEO to verify whether a project involves capitalizable infrastructure or maintenance activities to prevent future misclassification; and
Municipal Planning Development Office ensure that project titles accurately reflect the specific nature of the work to avoid confusion in financial recording and reporting.

We recommended that the:
OIC-GSO ensure the preparation of monthly SSMI as basis of the Accounting Office to record the issuance of inventories timely or as they take place; and
Municipal Accountant ensure the maintenance of updated and complete Supplies Ledger Cards, as required in Sections 114 and 120 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs and timely recording of supplies issued.

1.12 We reiterated our prior year's recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed 		  to:
ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE account in the financial statements; and.
direct the Municipal GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE stock cards and ledger cards, respectively, and perform reconciliation between their respective PPE records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
secure copies of Bank Statements of the above-cited bank accounts and submit immediately to the Audit Team for verification; and
identify the sources and nature of these dormant accounts and if the funds no longer serve their purpose, return the same to the source agencies or to the General Fund if the source agencies will approve.

We recommended and the management agreed that the :
GSO Designate conduct an inventory count of the materials and supplies to determine the unconsumed/unissued/undistributed portion of the expenses which will be the basis for the Municipal Accountant to record the related inventory accounts; and
Municipal Accountant record all inventories procured to their appropriate inventory accounts upon purchase and expensed only if the corresponding Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) are submitted.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
MPD Coordinator review all the projects undertaken and to be undertaken by the Municipality including the projects noted in our observation and come up with a concrete action to address the deficiencies noted;
Municipal Engineer address the root cause of the non-implementation; and
MPDC and ME coordinate with other offices concerned to fast track the implementation of the said projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
OIC-MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer coordinate and ensure that all expenditures charged against the LDRRMF are included in the approved LDRRMFIP of the current year and to strictly abide by the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.  2013-1 for the proper utilization of the LDRRMF; and.
Municipal Accountant ensure the proper charging and documentation of MDRRMF disbursements in compliance with the applicable regulations.
We recommended and the GFPS agreed to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant with the cooperation of the Municipal Treasurer agreed to submit immediately the reported lacking vouchers and the supporting documents to preclude the issuance of Notices of Suspension. Also, personnel concerned shall strictly observe and submit the required reports within the prescribed deadlines to effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their validity, propriety, completeness and accuracy.

We reiterated our prior year's recommendations and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor: create an Inventory Committee tasked to regularly conduct the annual physical count of the Municipality’s PPE items and to prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Office of Auditor; ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements; and direct the Municipal GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE stock cards and ledger cards, respectively, and perform reconciliation between their respective PPE records.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant record cash advance correctly under Advances to SDO to facilitate proper monitoring and ensure accountability, and; Acting Municipal Treasurer submit the liquidation reports of the above-cited cash advances for the Audit Team to verify the actual utilization.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant: 3.9.1 write the Branch Manager of DBP to inquire action taken by the Bank on the errors; 3.9.2 carefully review the bank reconciliation statement and promptly identify and address any discrepancies between the balance in the bank and in the books; and 3.9.3 make necessary adjustments on the noted deficiencies to arrive at accurate Cash-in-Bank balances to be presented on the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere to rules and regulations by ensuring that funds with a specific purpose are properly classified and transferred to the appropriate Trust Fund account.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRMC, headed by the Municipal Mayor, to promptly submit the Supplemental LDRRMFIP with complete details of projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness using the Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed of the following: for the LDRRMC to carefully plan all the necessary activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP; for the OIC-MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate and ensure that all expenditures charged against the LDRRMF are within the approved appropriation for the current year and to strictly abide by the stipulations of the Joint Memorandum Circular of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.  2013-1 for the proper utilization of the LDRRMF; and for the Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper charging and documentation of MDRRMF disbursements in compliance with the applicable regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: - MPD Coordinator review all the projects undertaken and to be undertaken by the Municipality including the projects noted in our observation and come up with a concrete action to address the deficiencies noted; and - MPDC and the Municipal Engineer coordinate with other offices concerned to fast track the implementation of the said projects.
We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Inventory and Appraisal Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, identify all land properties owned and/or controlled by the Municipality and ensure their proper valuation;
Municipal Assessor  in coordination with the General Service Officer secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCTs), Deeds of Sale/Donation, and other valid proof of ownership for all land properties acquired by the LGU, regardless of the mode of acquisition and submit these documents to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the proper recording of the acquisition, including  adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements, where appropriate pending the complete submission of requirements for the release of TCT or full acceptance of donation as stipulated in the Deed of Donation, where applicable copy of which must be furnished to the General Services Officer on the following:
(a) unrecorded land units identified as listed in the MASSO Records as properties acquired and utilized by the LGU in various projects with a fair market value of P27,152,692.00;
(b)  land properties with a fair market value of P63,787,645.00 acquired through donation but not recorded in the books; and
(3) Three (3) Units of land properties of the Municipality recorded in the books in aggregate costs of P7,358,000.00 acquired through donation; and
require the General Service Officer (a) to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor to update property records with MASSO list of Municipal Properties and Schedule of Land Account as reported in the Financial Statements affecting land properties for reconciliation purposes and (b) to facilitate safekeeping and retrieval of documents deemed necessary to establish absolute ownership of land assets acquired by the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
provide adequate disclosure or a narrative explanation in the Notes to Financial Statements as required under Paragraphs 122-125 of IPSAS No. 1 in the succeeding reporting period regarding the contractual commitments for both parties with respect to the donation of housing units and lot being utilized under the “Level-Up Housing Village” Program of the Municipality for completed projects totaling P19,819,780.35 and the lot being utilized for the purpose; and
take up the JEV for the recording of donated housing units and land, provided that all conditions stipulated in the deed of conditional donation are fully met or satisfactorily complied upon full acceptance of donation by the beneficiaries. Below is a suggested entry in taking up the transactions once the requirements of conditional donation are fully satisfied, to wit:
To record the value of individual housing units awarded to recipients

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Engineering Office submit the required Certificate of Completion and Acceptance for projects reported as completed in the QPPA and other required documents necessary in facilitating the reclassification of Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts to PPE accounts, taking into consideration the settlement of any liquidated damages, where appropriate;
Focal person assigned exercise prudence in monitoring the status of Infrastructure projects based on the documents submitted such as Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, fuel consumption report and other pertinent documents on completed projects, including the physical accomplishment reports as to the projects implemented; and
Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset account and compute for the depreciation expenses on the completed projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the requirements of fidelity bonding of all personnel handling public funds, including job order personnel designated as sub-collectors of the Municipality, in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019, in order mitigate financial risks and uphold accountability in revenue collection.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant  send demand letters compelling the concerned contractors to comply with the required posting of Surety Bond that is equivalent to 30% of the Contract Price as mandated under Section 62.2.3.3 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184   to cover any structural defects that may be found on the two (2) completed and fully paid infrastructure projects.
Also, we recommended and the Management concurred that the  Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure strict compliance with the posting of the required amount of warranty security by the contractor prior to the release of full payment for every completed/accepted infrastructure project to assure the Municipality of contractor’s commitment to fulfill their responsibility as required under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor:
to require the Project Management Committee to submit to the COA office the following documentary requirements to ascertain if beneficiaries are qualified to receive the assistance:
a. Barangay Certification on Residency for at least one (1) year and in case of
single (in status) applicants must be head of a family
b. Affidavit containing the following:
b.1 Non-ownership of any real property either acquired through inheritance or by purchase;
b.2 Non-beneficiary of any government housing projects.
c. Marriage Certificate (if applicable)
d. Any valid government-issued IDe. Deed of Condition executed by the Municipal Mayor on behalf of the Municipality as “Donor” and duly accepted by the ISF-recipient as “Donee”
f. Other pertinent documents deemed essential to prove the validity and propriety of donation of these assets to the target beneficiaries;
to direct the Municipal Housing Board with the able of assistance of Project Management Committee to revisit the existing guidelines regarding the appropriate timeline in processing the documentary requirements and other areas needing improvement to ensure proper implementation of the project; and
to direct the Project Management Committee to provide a Status Report as to the extent of compliance by parties concerned with the implementation of the project.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality’s Development Council adopt doable strategies that provides linkage to efforts exerted in calculating the resources needed during project identification stage with the assistance of Municipal and Planning Development Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only viable development projects are included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP).

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office conformed to set realistic estimates for appropriate time and schedule based on allotted funds in the implementation of viable disaster response and management-related undertakings.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person:
to strengthen the Gender and Development Inter-Agency Committee by capacitating its Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System to accomplish projects within the approved GAD objectives; and
to ensure that GAD ARs are duly supported with the required reports/documents as required under item 2.2 and furnish it to the COA office to facilitate audit of GAD fund utilization.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Human Resource Management Office assign personnel to conduct verification/reconciliation and confirmation of the reported unreconciled balance of the accounts Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG, and Due to PhilHealth as of December 31, 2024 and coordinate with the concerned agencies to confirm the balance of these accounts and address any outstanding contributions; and
Human Resource Management Office with the assistance of Municipal Accounting Office see to it that all deductions/contributions for the GSIS, Pag-IBIG Fund, and PhilHealth for the month are remitted intact and on time to the said agencies.

1.10 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the:
a. Inventory and Appraisal Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor,
to identify all land properties owned and/or controlled by the Municipality and
ensure their proper valuation;
b. Municipal Accountant (a) to reconcile their records with the Municipal Assessor as
to the land assets in aggregate cost of ₱585,932.00 which are no longer classified as
Municipal properties without complete details and information but still recorded in
the books and to provide adequate disclosure of details of the reasons for
derecognition of the land properties from the books in the Notes to Financial
Statement, where applicable for fair presentation of the Land Account in the
financial statements; (b) to obtain a copy of SB resolution authorizing the
donation/sale of these land properties to the actual recipients/vendee, if any;
c. Municipal Assessor to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCTs), Deeds of
Sale/Donation, and other valid proof of ownership for all land properties acquired
by the LGU and submit these documents to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the
proper recording of the Eighty-Eight (88) land units with a Fair Market Value of
₱364,449,684.00, including adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements, where appropriate pending the complete submission of requirements for
the release of TCT, copy of which must be furnished to the General Services Officer;
and
d. General Service Office (a) to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Assessor to update property records with Schedule of Land Account as
reported in the Financial Statements and MASSO list of Municipal Properties
affecting land properties for reconciliation purposes and (b) to facilitate safekeeping
and retrieval of documents deemed necessary to establish absolute ownership of land
assets acquired by the Municipality.

2.10 We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Municipal Engineering Office to submit the required Certificate of Completion and
Acceptance for projects reported as completed in the QPPA and other required
documents necessary in facilitating the reclassification of Construction in Progress
(CIP) accounts to PPE accounts, taking into consideration the settlement of any
liquidated damages, where appropriate;
2. Focal person assigned to exercise prudence in monitoring the status of Infrastructure
projects based on the documents submitted such as Certificate of Completion and
Acceptance on completed projects, including the physical accomplishment reports as
to the projects implemented; and
3. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP
account to its appropriate asset account and compute for the depreciation expenses
on the completed projects.

an Journal Entry Voucher (using the suggested entry below) to rectify the entries
made and ensure that transactions are recorded in the books of accounts in
compliance with Section 27 of the International Public-Sector Accounting Standards
(IPSAS) 1 for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements and

4.12 We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Inventory Committee composed of the Accounting and General Services Office or
their duly authorized representatives, to be responsible for scheduling of physical
count of inventories, including the subsequent reconciliation of accounts;
2. Inventory committee to provide a written justification in the event of any failure to
conduct the physical count, reconciliation of records, or the timely submission of
the RPCI and RPCSP and other required documents deemed necessary to the Audit
Team for evaluation in audit;
3. GSO to ensure timely and accurate preparation of the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI), supported by duly accomplished Requisition and Issue
Slip; and
4. GSO and Accounting Office to facilitate continuous reconciliation of accounting
and property records and to adjust the books of accounts and property

5.7 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant expedite
the filing of the request for write-off, together with the required supporting documents
for receivables where settlement/collectability can no longer be ascertained in
accordance with COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

6.4 We recommended and the Management concurred to require the Accountable Officer
concerned to strictly abide by the guidelines embodied COA Circular No. 97-002 by
ensuring that only duly designated officers are allowed to perform disbursing functions,
and to formally issue an official designation to the Disbursing Officer, a copy of which
shall be furnished to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.

7.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to expedite the processing
of requirements in availing the ATM Payroll System in releasing to Job Order the

Recommendations:

Supplies Expense account to fairly present the financial statement, and;
b. establish a proper inventory tracking system, including the maintenance of
Stock Cards, Inventory Ledgers, and Requisition and Issuance Slips (RIS) to
properly monitor supplies.

Recommendations:

depreciation and depreciation expense using the straight-line method, in
compliance with PPSAS 17;
b. implement a systematic tracking mechanism for all PPE to ensure timely
provision of depreciation and its accurate valuation; and
c. conduct periodic reconciliation between the Municipal Accounting and General
Services Offices to ensure consistency in asset records and depreciation
schedules.

balances are accurately reported in the financial statements.

land assets in the financial statements, ensuring that all properties owned by the
Municipality are properly accounted for in compliance with government
accounting standards and regulations.

Recommendations:
5.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s
Offices:
5.7.1. reconcile the discrepancies noted between their records and to regularly
reconcile the same so that errors and discrepancies in balances could be
traced and accordingly adjusted; and
5.7.2. draw adjusting entries to correct the balances of affected Cash and cash
equivalent accounts and to adjust balances in the Cash Books.
Management’s Comments:
5.8. According to the Municipal Treasurer, the variance in the Cash in Bank - LCCA
account resulted from a payment received from CHED for MonCAST in
December. While this payment had already been recognized as income in the general ledger (GL), it had not yet been recorded in the cashbook, leading to a
discrepancy between the two records.
5.9. On the other hand, the variance in the Cash - Local Treasury account was caused
by a clerical error, which may have involved incorrect data entry,
misclassification of transactions, or omission of records. The Treasurer's and
Accountant’s offices commit to taking the necessary steps to reconcile these
discrepancies and ensure the accuracy of financial records.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
5.10. While management has provided an explanation for the discrepancy in the Cash
in Bank - LCCA account, timely recording of transactions remains essential to
ensure accurate financial reporting. We encourage management to establish
procedures to prevent similar timing issues in the future.
62
5.11. Similarly, while the variance in the Cash - Local Treasury account was attributed
to clerical errors, proper controls—such as regular reconciliations and reviews—
are necessary to minimize such mistakes and maintain accurate records.

Also, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure strict compliance with the posting of the required amount of warranty security by the contractor prior to the release of full payment for every completed/accepted infrastructure project to assure the Municipality of contractor’s commitment to fulfill their responsibility as required under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Recommendations:

to strengthen the Gender and Development Inter-Agency Committee by capacitating its Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System to accomplish projects within the approved GAD objectives; and
to ensure that GAD ARs are duly supported with the required reports/documents as required under item 2.2 and furnish it to the COA office to facilitate audit of GAD fund utilization.

a. make the necessary adjusting entry to Supplies Inventory Account and Supplies
Expense account to fairly present the financial statement, and;
b. prioritize the proper recording of all purchases relative to inventories, which should
be recognized as an asset when purchased and expensed only when used, sold, or
distributed.

2.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Engineering Office submit to the General Services Office the Certificates
of Project Completion and other supporting documents of the completed infrastructure
projects for recording;
b. Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal adjusting entries to close the CIP
accounts to its appropriate Property Plant and Equipment accounts and provide the
corresponding depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation; and
c. Municipal Accountant submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the JEV taking up
the reclassification of asset accounts and the provision of the depreciation expense and
the corresponding accumulated depreciation.

3.11 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant adjust
and restore the total amount of all unclaimed checks every end of the year in order
to reflect Cash in Bank balances and Accounts Payable.

4.16 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Inventory and Appraisal Committee together with the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Assessor identify all land items owned or controlled by the Municipality
and provide proper valuation thereof;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor reconcile accounting and assessor’s
records, and adjust all discrepancies if there are any, and;
c. Municipal Accountant take the necessary steps to record unrecorded/accurate land
assets in the financial statements, ensuring that all properties owned by the
Municipality are properly accounted for in compliance with government accounting
standards and regulations.

Recommendations:
1.8	We recommended and Management agreed to:
direct the Municipal Engineer to fast-track the retrieval of relevant certificates of project completion and submit copies to the Municipal Accounting Office for the proper reclassification; and
require the Municipal Accountant:
b.1.	 to re-classify the completed projects from the CIP accounts to its appropriate PPE accounts duly supported with certificates of project completion; and
b.2.	to compute for the unrecorded depreciation starting on the year the projects were completed.

Recommendations:
2.10	We recommended and Management agreed to:
create a separate Inventory Committee for goods and supplies responsible for the planning, the conduct of physical count, and the preparation of report thereon;
ensure compliance on the conduct of semestral physical inventory count on inventories, including the preparation and submission of RPCI to MAO and COA on or before July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
instruct the Accounting Office to make all necessary adjustments for all unrecorded issuances of inventories and the reconciliation of the RPCI and the accounting records.

Recommendations:
4.5	We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the immediate disposal of obsolete checks in accordance with governing guidelines to prevent the risk of possible misuse.

Recommendations:
5.7	We recommended and Management agreed to:
expedite the thorough review and analysis of the Due to NGAs account, and make the necessary adjustments, where appropriate; and
return the excess amount from the fully completed projects to the source agencies.

Recommendation:

Recommendations:
7.11	We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the Municipal Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the other offices in the Municipality, to conduct initial re-assessment as to the viability of the planned projects prior years projects under the 20% Development Fund and provide recommendation to the Municipal Development Office if whether it is feasible to continue the project;
subsequently, coordinate with the Municipal Development Council to determine the appropriate action to be undertaken in relation to the recommendation of the MPDO as to the viability of the prior years; projects, particularly as to whether it is feasible to continue the projects or identify new programs, projects and activities to be implemented; and
consider the opening of additional plantilla for the Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the implementation of development projects.

Recommendations:
8.6	We recommended and Management agreed to:
improve planning by setting clear timelines and properly allocating resources, while streamlining the procurement process to speed up project execution. Prioritize key LDRRMF-related projects, enhance coordination between offices; and
conduct a regular monitoring of the Plan and ensure the implementation of PPAs included therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office to deny collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste and strictly implement the use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers for specific types of wastes. CENRO should also strive to ensure that only residual wastes shall be disposed in the Sanitary Landfill.
observations.managementAction:
1.) Conducted regular Information, Education Campaign Activities such as Orientation on RA 9003 and establishing of BSWMC, reminding barangays of their roles and responsibilities mandated under the law. Per RA 9003 and City Ordinance 0361-10, segregation and collection of biodegradable and recyclable wastes are responsibilities of the barangays, while residuals and special wastes are with the City.         
                                               
2.) Enforced RA9003 and C.O.0361-10 through issuance of citation tickets to violators. From January to December 2023, a total of 4,183 violators were apprehended.       
                             
3.) Requested Barangays to utilize Brgy-owned trucks to undertake segregated collection of garbage and transport residual wastes to the landfill. As of December 2023, a total of 40 barangays have barangay owned trucks and regularly disposing garbage to the landfill.

4.) Submitted Project Design for a Proposed Davao City Central Materials Recovery and Composting Facility. The proposed facility will cater recyclable and biodegradable wastes from barangay sources which lacks these crucial solid waste diversion facilities and ensure segregation will be successful. Facility will be constructed once the new sanitary landfill is completed.

observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
1. the Municipal Accountant to record all consummated procurement of supplies and materials thru the inventory account based on the Acceptance and Inspection Report together with the supporting documents and all issuances through the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued with the original Requisition and Issue Slip; and to properly follow the steps in recording the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method;
2. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, as required in Sections 119 and 120 of NGAS Manual Volume I for local government units; and
3. the Municipal Treasurer to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials every end of the semester and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the records and to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has assured the auditors that her office is fully capable of addressing the issues related to the AOM. Furthermore, starting in 2025, the office will be able to provide comprehensive reports on this account, as the necessary updates have been incorporated into their system. This integration is expected to enhance the accuracy and timeliness of the reports, ensuring better compliance and accountability in the management of the Municipality's financial affairs.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant should:
enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability; and
request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.
observations.managementAction: The management is in the process of securing the necessary supporting documents for the request for authority to write off dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant closely coordinate in updating the utilization of LDRRMF to determine the unexpended balance of the fund and transfer the said balance immediately from General Fund to the Trust Fund on or before the last banking day of the year. The same should be recorded before closing the books of accounts as at year end. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant shall submit the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant closely coordinate in updating the utilization of LDRRMF to determine the unexpended balance of the fund and transfer the said balance immediately from General Fund to the Trust Fund on or before the last banking day of the year. The same should be recorded before closing the books of accounts as at year end. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant shall submit the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to review and account for all tangible items below P50,000.00, acquired and issued prior to CY 2024, which have been classified as PPE and charge/expense their carrying amounts to Prior Period Adjustment pursuant to Paragraph 3.7 of COA Circular 2024-006;
2. Municipal Accountant to close the accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment losses of these properties in the books of accounts; and
3. Municipal Accountant to facilitate the prompt update of the accounting software to incorporate the new semi-expendable accounts to be used in recording semi-expendable properties and reclassify those recorded under the Other Supplies Inventory account accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Since the software for the accounting system was just recently upgraded, reclassification of previously deemed PPE items is currently in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the incorporation of the appropriate inventory and expense account titles for semi-expendable properties as provided in the Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2024-006 into the accounting software, and subsequently reclassify the inventory in its proper account titles and make necessary adjustments recognizing the corresponding expenses.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to facilitate the incorporation of the appropriate inventory and expense account titles for semi-expendable properties as provided in the Annex B of COA Circular No. 2024-006 into the accounting software, and subsequently reclassify the inventory in its proper account titles and make necessary adjustments recognizing the corresponding expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
1. for the MPDC and the MEO to coordinate, strictly monitor and evaluate the implementation of all priority development projects within the budget year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF;
2. for the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that development projects arising from debt servicing can be paid without opting to reverse the appropriated funds of other development fund projects; and to prioritize the allocation of funds for the implementation of proposed development projects in the LDIP; and
3. for the MPDC to provide a copy of the Municipal Development Council Execom Resolutions to highlight the realignment of the reverted projects.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and MEO to coordinate, strictly monitor and evaluate the implementation of all priority development projects within the budget year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF; for the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that development projects arising from debt servicing can be paid without opting to reverse the appropriated funds of other development fund projects; and to prioritize the allocation of funds for the implementation of proposed development projects in the LDIP; and for the MPDC to provide a copy of the Municipal Development Council Execom Resolutions to highlight the realignment of the reverted projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
1. for the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) in coordination with the MPDO and MBO to conduct careful planning and identification of activities to be included in the GPB;
2. for the MPDO to refrain from the practice of reverting the GAD Budget and allocating it to other programs, and submit a written justification of the reversion of GAD budget; and
3. for the GAD Focal Person to observe prompt submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Due to the unexpected implementation of the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) KALAHI-CIDSS program, some of the planned projects under the Gender and Development Fund had to be reverted. Additionally, certain projects were realigned to prioritize the construction of two multipurpose buildings. The Municipality also chose to prioritize the implementation of the LGU counterpart projects funded by the National Government. However, the Municipality assured that, in 2025, they will continue with the planned Gender and Development Fund projects. These projects remain a priority, and the Municipality is committed to ensuring their timely and effective implementation moving forward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRM Officer should judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRMMF, incorporating feasible time schedules, availability of resources, and other contingencies to avoid delays in the implementation of PPAs. Further, the MDRRMO is also enjoined to improve the utilization rate of LDRRMF and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs in order to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for, responding to, and recovering from the impact of disasters.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRM Officer should judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRMMF, incorporating feasible time schedules, availability of resources, and other contingencies to avoid delays in the implementation of PPAs. Further, the MDRRMO is also enjoined to improve the utilization rate of LDRRMF and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs in order to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for, responding to, and recovering from the impact of disasters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

Stop the granting of cash advances to non-permanent officials designated as Special Disbursing Officers.

observations.recommendation:
14. We recommended that management enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the recipient barangays stating all the obligations of both parties in the implementation of 20% Development Projects of the Municipality. It is emphasized that the fund transfer will be subject to liquidation and, hence be accounted as Due from Local Government Units on the part of Municipality and Due to Local Government Units on the part of barangay and the record the project into appropriate asset account. 

15. Also, in the project implementation, since the barangay personnel has no technical capacity to implement the same, include in the MOA the extent of help or assistance to be rendered by the MEO to ensure that infrastructure projects will meet the quality standards for safety and efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Management had stopped the transfer of funds to Barangays. Projects for Barangays under 20% Development Fund were implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
16. We recommended that the Management, particularly the MDC EXECOM, before deciding what projects are to be undertaken, especially under the 20% Development Fund, proper evaluation, as well as consultation with all offices and stakeholders, must be made first, seeking their comments and concerns, so that the project to be included in the plan, is implementation-ready. Also, timely monitoring and assessment of the planned projects must be made, so that any changes, or realignment, that will be made due to demand of the condition, will also be made earlier to make the project implementable within the budget year.  

17. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) closely monitor and evaluate the status of the implementation of projects.

We recommended the following to the MDRRMO:

18. Ensure strict compliance with the guidelines provided in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, dated 25 March 2013 on the charging of expenses against the Municipality’s current year and unexpended LDRRMF.

19. For fuel expenditures, ensure that the Driver’s Trip Ticket is properly and completely accomplished, and the prescribed format of Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels (Appendices G and F of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles) are prepared and submitted every month-end.

20. We also recommended that the OIC - Municipal Budget Officer ensure that only expenses related to disaster risk management activities and in line with the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under the LDRRMF are charged from this fund.

21. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that all supporting documents are complete and appropriate before making certification of the DV.

22. Finally, we recommended that the agency officials concerned restore the amount of P1,633,902.29 (174,304.54+1,459,597.75) in the Municipality’s LDRRMF or provide justification as to the propriety of its charging thereof to preclude disallowance in audit.

23. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning Office, GAD Focal person and other offices concerned to continue updating, completing and maintaining a functional GAD Database which shall aid in gender analysis for the identification of gender issues. and the cause/causes, to be addressed by the agency; the agency’s GAD database.

24. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit timely the paid DVs on a daily or weekly basis, whichever is appropriate, to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely processing thereof and submission to the COA Office. 	

25. Also, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the mentioned lacking DVs.
observations.reason: One hundred sixty-five (165) DVs in CY 2023 with a total amount of P28,316,711.91 are still unsubmitted

Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description of the road, including Road ID/PIN, Road Name, Components, Description, and Cost, and supply the information to other responsible departments concerned, including the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant, who in turn will facilitate the preparation and maintenance of the necessary Local Road Network (LRN) Property and LRN Ledger Cards, respectively;

observations.recommendation:

4.Inventory Committee to conduct an investigation to determine the last known location/office where the missing PPE items were issued/installed, the person accountable, circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for the loss, among others;

observations.managementAction:
