observations.recommendation: 13.       i.          Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the creation and personnel complement of the Internal Audit Services (IAS) Office and consider converting existing vacant positions and/or parallel positions of incumbents in the agency for the installation and strengthening of the IAS, if warranted; and

observations.recommendation: 16.    We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to ensure that LDRRMF should be utilized only for disaster-related activities and programs to maximize the intended benefits among constituents in the event any disaster might occur.

It may seem that those activities are for security reasons, effects of man-made disaster could possibly happen, it may be natural or man- made. Based on your observation, this office will do its best to maximize and align the resources properly and taking heed of your recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 17.    Officials/personnel concerned in the Engineering Office and GSO to provide a written explanation citing the reasons for not holding them liable for whatever damages may have been incurred in the disbursements of these   contracts if disallowed in audit, where applicable;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted a written explanation to COA and recommended the termination of two project contracts. The office ensured thorough project assessment before approval to minimize abandonment risks and enhance early identification and management of project risks, including contractor performance, resource allocation, and delays.

observations.recommendation: 18.    Municipal Planning and Development Council, with the assistance of implementing departments concerned to adopt coherent strategies and synchronized planning in formulating future development projects with detailed feasibility/preliminary engineering study prior to its implementation;
observations.managementAction: A budget was allocated under the Project Pre-Implementation Support Program to fund preliminary activities such as geo-resistivity surveys and securing taxes, duties, and licenses.

observations.recommendation: 19.    Department heads concerned, primarily assisted by the BAC Secretariat, to produce and submit the documents covering the two (2) contracts for abandoned infrastructure projects, through the Office of the Audit Team to the Regional Technical Audit Group (RTAG) of the COA Regional XI for an in-depth evaluation, subject to the applicable guidelines found in RA No. 9184 as a basis to render an appropriate decision in audit; and
observations.managementAction: All program coordinators and focal persons prepared Project Briefs when proposing PPAs in development plans and investment programs.

observations.recommendation: 20.    Municipal Engineering Office to conduct an inspection on the status of both projects as of date and determine any liquidated damages, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer's Office inspected the project and found the actual accomplishment exceeded the contractor's claimed percentage.

observations.recommendation: 21.  Require the BAC, TWG, and Municipal Engineer to submit an explanation in writing as to the causes of the deficiencies noted, specifically on awarding of contracts with multiple projects and simultaneous/overlapping contract periods without proper evaluation as to the technical capability to undertake the project, and delays incurred in the project implementation;
observations.managementAction: The BAC enforced stricter oversight to ensure contractors adhered to timelines. Clear guidelines were established on awarding multiple contracts, limiting projects per contractor, and assessing their capacity.

observations.recommendation: 22.  Direct the BAC-TWG to evaluate thoroughly the responsiveness of the lowest calculated bidder based on the reasonableness of the amount of time and other inputs it can handle to undertake the project during post-qualification using a comparative evaluation sheet  indicating the number of projects with the same equipment and key personnel pledged as available for different projects with simultaneous or overlapping implementation period within the vicinity, and decide thereon as the basis to disqualify future bids during the post qualification stage, where appropriate;
observations.managementAction: The BAC and TWG conducted a thorough evaluation of contractors' technical capabilities, including their capacity to manage multiple contracts. The process was enhanced to assess resources, personnel, and track record.

observations.recommendation: 23.  Require the BAC to set the parameters as to the limitation of multiple projects to be awarded to a single contractor based on the sufficiency and availability of the equipment pledged for the project and adequacy of key personnel assigned for the contract vis-à-vis project scheduling, and set a comparison with other LGUs in the province before as benchmark for declaring the bids as responsive;
observations.managementAction: The BAC and TWG drafted parameters to assess a contractor’s capacity for multiple projects, considering equipment, key personnel, project scheduling, and benchmarks from other LGUs.

observations.recommendation: 24.  Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and supervise the simultaneous implementation of the project to safeguard from unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer's Office strengthened its monitoring system to ensure effective oversight of simultaneous project implementations, focusing on progress tracking, resource allocation, and compliance with timelines.

observations.recommendation: 25.  Municipal Accountant to compute for the liquidated damages based on the extent of delays incurred in number of days and the contract amount and ensure completed documents are attached to support the disbursements, where warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant computes liquidated damages, if applicable, based on supporting documents for contractor payments.

observations.recommendation: 26.            scrutinize the sufficiency of the contractor’s key personnel and equipment to ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of contract implementation; and
observations.managementAction: The TWG verified personnel credentials and equipment availability, ensuring contracts were awarded only to capable contractors.

observations.recommendation: 27.            strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in the evaluation of bids submitted by the bidders to ensure fair and competitive bidding; and ensure that all approved contracts for projects implemented are supported with a DOLE-approved Construction Safety and Health Program pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the IRR.
observations.managementAction: Contractors were required to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP within 10 days of the Notice of Award. The BAC, TWG, and Secretariat strictly enforced compliance to ensure worker and public safety.

observations.recommendation: 29.     We recommended and Management agreed for the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 90 under Chapter 3 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to prepare two separate cashbooks for the Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts under the Trust Fund- SALINTUBIG and Trust Fund- DA BUB.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer's Office maintains separate cashbooks for Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank under the Trust Fund-Salintubig and Trust Fund-DA BUB.

observations.recommendation: 31.    exercise prudence in expediting the process of the selection, recruitment, and promotion from the pool of applicants to deserving individuals whose competence and skills are commensurate to the minimum qualification standards, subject to the requirements of the CSC laws and regulations; and

Meanwhile, the OS Committee has reviewed the OSSP, which is now ready for submission to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval. Additionally, relevant offices are conducting a comprehensive review of the municipality’s organizational structure.
observations.reason: A review of the 4th-quarter manpower report confirmed that some CY 2023 vacancies have been filled, with hiring ongoing to address the remaining 57 vacant positions as of December 31, 2024. The Municipal Human Resource Officer affirmed compliance with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: 32.    We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality are properly labeled with “For Official Use Only,” under which should be written the corresponding name of the Agency operating or using the same. The mark should appear on each side of the motor vehicle. When there is no sufficient space on each side, the same should appear at the back and on the front just below the windshield of the motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Stickers have been affixed to all LGU vehicles except those assigned to the Municipal Mayor and COA Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
a. Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant a complete description of road components for road projects as the basis for its recognition in the books of accounts.
b. General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components and secure a copy of the Road Map from the MPDO.
c. Inventory Committee to comply with the prescribed template on the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as illustrated in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008.
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare the ledger card of PPE accounts, including road networks, and observe the proper accounting and reporting of road networks and the appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the total road network system in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network to the Municipal General Services Officer.
The General Services Officer prepared the Local Road Inventory and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components.
The inventory committee used the prescribed template on the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System illustrated in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008.
The Municipal Accountant prepared the ledger card of PPE accounts, including road networks, and observed the proper accounting and reporting of road networks and the appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the total road network system in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management agreed to instruct the:
a. Property Officer to update the records based on the results of the physical inventory and reconcile with the accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of the PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE; and
b. MACCO and GSO (i) coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership, and condition of the properties, and (ii) prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Property Officer update their records based on the results on their physical count and reconcile it to the accounting records.
The Municipal Accountant and The Municipal General Services Officer coordinate each other and prepared the supporting schedules and reconcile the property as to the actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties. Also, they already prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and management is already agreeable to our audit recommendation through the Municipal Accountant who committed to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statement of MDOC account to facilitate its review.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the Bank Reconciliation Statement of MDOC account to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management already reclassifies the receivables that belong to non-current assets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassify the receivables that belong to non-current assets manually.

The Municipal Accountant made an adjustment to ensure the reconciliation of the amounts and accounts presented in the report which is in conformity with Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards.
The Municipal Accountant already disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide relevant, reliable, and timely information about the LGU’s financial performance, position, and cash flows to assist management in making proper decisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to promptly establish the comprehensive guidelines and implementing rules and procedures for the storage, reporting, and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies to ensure transparency, accountability, and optimal utilization of donated resources, thereby enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of healthcare services provided to the public.

observations.recommendation:
6. We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer to:

observations.recommendation: 2.     consider an in-depth evaluation of the existing organizational structure and staffing requirements based on the current work demands in individual offices needing significant improvement, subject to the availability of funds in the next budget period to achieve optimal benefits in terms of fostering job opportunities and set of qualified personnel as a channel in attaining envisioned programs and policies designed to pave the way for improved quality of life among the constituents.

8. We recommended and the Management agreed for the: 

1.     Municipal Social Welfare Development Office to ensure that a well-defined policies and guidelines should be established in writing on the grant of financial assistance containing but not limited to the following:

a.      Types of financial assistance (medical, burial, etc.) together with the prescribed rate for a specific case, including the frequency and mode of release of similar grants to an individual,

b.     Documentary requirements to be used in screening the eligibility of the recipient for a specific type of financial assistance, and

c.      Duties and responsibilities of personnel to be assigned in conducting a thorough evaluation or a case study prior to the grant of financial aid.
observations.managementAction: There is already an executive order issued (Executive Order No. 33 series of 2024, issued on 8th of April, 2024.

observations.recommendation: 2.     OIC- MSWDO to furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader a copy of the promulgated policies and guidelines intended for the approval, granting and mode of release of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The Office of Audit Team Leader was furnished the copy of the said executive order on March 3, 2025.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Human Resource Officer, and the management concurred to submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies and expedite the hiring of individuals from a pool of applicants who are qualified for the position in accordance with the requirements of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted a written justification for the LDRRMO vacancies and initiated the hiring process. One of the two mandatory positions has been filled, while the other remains vacant and has been published for applicants.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile its records on the reported 25% Barangay Share in RPT proceeds in the periods with unremitted balance and to submit the required reports as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in processing the share of each component barangay in the RPT proceeds that should have been remitted/distributed on a quarterly basis in accordance with Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure that the barangay can avail of the immediate use of the fund for its viable programs and projects.
observations.managementAction:
The remittance of the share of RPT to the barangays are remitted monthly together with the share of RPT to the province.

Barangay shares from the LGU are already remitted timely to the barangays as evidenced by the monthly journal entries.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommended and the Management agreed for the MHRMO to issue an internal written policy/memorandum for proper delineation of the functions and the recruitment of job order personnel and other considerations, including the basis for their entitlement to higher and lawful rates with due consideration on the minimum wage rate, specifically under applicable Wage Order No. RB XI-2I effective January 1, 2023, or as may be prescribed by the DOLE- RTWPB-Region XI in succeeding wage order.
observations.managementAction: The LGU coordinated with the Local Finance Committee to plan a gradual wage increase for job order workers and is finalizing policies and memoranda for contract of service wage adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts held under the name of the LGU; b. provide the necessary supporting documents upon submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to facilitate verification of the amounts presented in the report; c. draw journal entries to record the debit/credit memos; and d. for subsequent years, ensure that necessary adjustments are made before closing of books to revert to the cash in bank account the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks to fairly present the account in the financial statement as at yearend.
observations.managementAction: All accounts of the LGU are prepared with BRS. Hard copies of bank statements were provided by the LBP as the supporting documents for verification. Journal entries were drawn and copy was submitted to the audit team. A report of unreleased checks was provided by MTO for reversion to the cash in bank at yearend by the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor file the necessary request for authority to write off the dormant Other Receivables account to COA following the procedures as prescribed under COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, duly supported by the necessary documents. Furthermore, the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the impairment loss of the remaining dormant other receivable accounts to ensure its fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Following the procedures as prescribed under COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, duly supported by the necessary documents. Impairment loss was recognized in the books of accounts by the management.
observations.reason: Still gathering the necessary documents for the request for authority to write off dormant accounts. The team will monitor the management’s progress.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office to implement the Moving Average Method in costing the Supplies and Material Inventories and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock recorded under the account as provided under COA rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management is on the initial stage of assessing the feasibility of procuring an inventory system and hiring additional personnel for its implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as at year-end in accordance with Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001.
observations.managementAction: The tax liability account was recognized at year-end for the corresponding payables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to facilitate the posting on the LGU’s website and in three (3) conspicuous places the quarterly reports on the fund utilization and status of implementation of the projects funded under the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC in accordance with the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 151 for the purpose of enhancing transparency and promoting public accountability.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO is now consistent with the posting of the fund utilization reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to develop a plan to strategize and consider potential wage adjustments for job order personnel, aligning them with the compensation levels of equivalent positions in the government.
observations.managementAction: Increase was made and gradually adhered to the current minimum wage of the region.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management, through the Human Resource and Management Officer, instruct the newly hired and promoted employees for immediate compliance with the documentary requirements to facilitate the timely processing and updating of their GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC records.
observations.managementAction: Prior to the first payment for the salary of the newly hired personnel, the MHRMO ensured the essential compliance of these requirements consequently the updating of records with other government agencies. Remittance Advice (ARA) will be promptly updated, ensuring the timely and accurate monthly remittance of premiums, loan repayments, and contributions to GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Person, establish a feasible timeline for the offices concerned for the submission of GAD ARs by the implementing offices concerned to facilitate the timely consolidation and prompt submission to oversight agencies in accordance with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001. Accordingly, posting requirements should be observed in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2019-149.
observations.managementAction: The submission of accomplishment reports by the implementing offices concerned are strictly complied and monitored by the GAD Focal Person. Non-compliance are issued with memorandum.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office to:
a. prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts held under the name of the LGU; 
b. provide the necessary supporting documents upon submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to facilitate verification of the reliability and accuracy of the amounts presented in the report; and 
c. for subsequent years, ensure that necessary adjustments are made before closing of books to revert to the cash in bank account the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks to fairly present the account in the financial statement as at yearend.
observations.managementAction:
Three out of six bank accounts were already closed and adjusted from the books with JEV no. OthJ-04680 dated June 30, 2024 and the verification of BRS; reactivated and adjusted last May 10, 2024 with JEV No. ColJ 04649;04653;04680. All accounts of the LGU are prepared with BRS.
Monthly bank reconciliation statements submitted to COA.
Noted by the MTO that all checks will be released before year-end, otherwise MAO will draw adjusting entries for unreleased checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as at year-end in accordance with Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001.
observations.managementAction: The tax liability account was recognized at year-end for the corresponding identified account payables.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. strictly enforce liquidation of outstanding cash advances within the prescribed period through the issuance of written notices to the erring Accountable Officer and furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the same; 
b. stop the granting of additional cash advances to officials and staff with existing unliquidated cash advances; and 
c. exhaust all efforts for the settlement of outstanding cash advances of AOs who were no longer connected with the LGU and those who were already deceased. Thereafter, consider the filing of the request for authority to write off accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: All cash advances were fully liquidated as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior recommendation that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accounting Office and other concerned offices to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the accounts as prescribed under Section 7.1 COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023. Finally, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write off the dormant Due from NGOs/POs account to COA for the write-off of dormant accounts, duly supported by the documents as prescribed under the same circular.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has conducted regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation to ensure the accuracy and existence of the accounts.
Additionally, management has formally submitted a request to the audit team for the write-off of the dormant accounts, in accordance with applicable guidelines and procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to facilitate the posting on the LGU’s website and in three (3) conspicuous places the quarterly reports on the fund utilization and status of implementation of the projects funded under the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC in accordance with the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 151 for the purpose of enhancing transparency and promoting accountability.
observations.managementAction: Posted in three (3) conspicuous places, with photo documentation submitted as proof. However, the LGU website is not yet available for online posting.
observations.reason: However, the LGU website is not yet available for online posting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to develop a plan to strategize and consider potential wage adjustments for job order personnel, aligning them with the compensation levels of equivalent positions in the government.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO aligned the compensation of the job orders according to their respective position in accordance with the Local Budget Circular no. 137 dated July 13, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, through the Human Resource and Management Officer, instructs the newly hired and promoted employees for the immediate compliance of the essential documentary requirements to facilitate the timely processing and updating of their GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC records. Consequently, the Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) will be promptly updated, ensuring the timely and accurate monthly remittance of premiums, loan repayments, and contributions to GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC.
observations.managementAction:
Management has initiated the early preparation of the ARA to ensure timely submission.
Consequently, the December 2024 loan repayments, employee contributions, and LGU counterparts to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth were fully remitted in January 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Person, establishes a feasible timeline for the submission of GAD ARs by the implementing offices concerned to facilitate the timely consolidation and prompt submission to oversight agencies in accordance with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001. Accordingly, posting requirements should be observed in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2019-149.
observations.managementAction: The submission of accomplishment reports by the implementing offices concerned are strictly complied and monitored by the GAD Focal Person. Non-compliance are issued with memorandum.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management includes in their budget discussion the long unexpended continuing appropriation balances, declaring them as savings, revert the balances to the unappropriated surplus of the 20% Development Fund for it to be reprogrammed to other vital developmental projects/ programs/activities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balances were declared as savings, included in Supplemental Budget No. 1 for CY 2024, and reprogrammed for other activities (PPAs).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Engineer to conduct an inspection to assess the current status of the project and determine the remaining work. Furthermore, the Mayor should consider initiating the process for terminating the contract if deemed necessary. Correspondingly, require the Municipal Accountant to impose the Liquidated Damages (LD) upon payment to the contractor, which is equivalent to one tenth (1/10) of one percent (1%) of the unfinished portion of the projects for every day of delay.
observations.managementAction: The project is 100% completed. The final billing is subject to liquidated damages upon processing of the Disbursement Voucher (DV).

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended the AAR Municipal Mayor to: 1. Require the General Services Officer to undertake the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 162 of COA Circular No. 92-386; and 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher for the derecognition of the unserviceable properties to ensure fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The management undertake the disposal of various unserviceable properties on September 18, 2024.
The MACCO made a Journal Entry Voucher in reference to the actual disposal of the unserviceable properties (JEV# 09-07844.)

observations.recommendation:
3. We recommended the AAR Municipal Mayor to file the request for authority to write off the dormant unliquidated cash advances account to write off as COA duly supported by the documents as prescribed under COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.
4. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the impairment loss of dormant unliquidated cash advances to ensure fair presentation of receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The management will gradually gather the necessary documents for the request to write off as prescribed in the new COA circular. The team will monitor the management’s progress.
Impairment loss was recognized in the books of accounts by the management.
observations.reason: The management will gradually gather the necessary documents for the request to write off as prescribed in the new COA circular. The team will monitor the management’s progress.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as at year-end in accordance with Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001 and to present fairly the true financial obligations of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Due to BIR account was recognized at year-end for the corresponding accounts identified.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 6. carefully review the information provided in the financial statements including its schedules and, if necessary, make adjustments to ensure the reconciliation of the amounts and accounts presented in the report. Failure to do so could result in misrepresentation of their financial statements, which is not in conformity with Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards; and 7. disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide relevant, reliable, and timely information to assist management make proper decisions.
observations.managementAction:
The relevant information has been reconciled and linked for each Financial Statement as of December 31, 2023.
All other significant information reflected in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: 9. observations.managementAction: Posting of quarterly reports on the fund utilization and status of implementation of the projects funded under the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC in accordance with the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 151 for the purpose of enhancing transparency and promoting public accountability was done religiously by the LGU. The report was posted on the Bulletin Board of the municipal building, terminal, and market areas. It was also posted on the LGU website and in the Subaybayan portal. Evidence of posting was submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that the Management direct the Human Resource Management Officer to develop a plan to strategize and consider potential adjustments for job order personnel, aligning them with the compensation levels of equivalent positions in the government.
observations.managementAction: Due to financial constraints, the LGU has not provided the 394 JO wages equivalent/comparable rate as provided in item 9 of the COA-DBM circular. However, the LGU is considered to be responsive to the situation and is continuously filling the vacant Plantilla positions to potentially benefit the Job Order personnel. Also, there is a proposed Job Order salary increase for CY 2025.

observations.recommendation: 14. observations.managementAction: The LGU has a project monitoring team composed of Civil Society Organizations that will monitor and evaluate the different projects in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to: 16. evaluate the viability of unimplemented PPAs under the LDRRM Fund- Continuing Appropriations and endorse these projects for immediate and reprogramming, whichever is deemed most appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO already realigned the 1.3M from purchasing a lot for the evacuation center and relocation site for the displaced families struck by the earthquake through SB Resolution No. 2003-422. The 1.3M was used for the fuel, oil, and lubricants for the site development of the relocation site, while the 400k was used for the construction materials for the temporary shelter. Realignment of Php 1,586,172.40 from the unutilized MDRRM Fund’s Capital Outlay CY 2015, 2018-2022, 2022 to purchase of rescue dog (300,000.00), purchase of motor vehicle (250,000.00), Fuel and Oil (486,172.00), capability building (350,000.00), repair and maintenance of vehicle (200,000.00) through SB Resolution No. 2024-186 and 2024-212.

observations.recommendation: 17. We recommended that the MDRRMO enhance monitoring efforts to ensure the optimal utilization and implementation of PPAs under the LDRRM fund, particularly the unimplemented continuing appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO is closely monitoring the utilization and the implementation of PPAs most especially the unimplemented continuing appropriations.

Management complied with the requirements for accurate reporting of local road network assets. The Municipal Engineer obtained data on each road (ID/PIN, name, components, condition, and costs) and shared it with the General Service Officer (GSO) and Municipal Accountant. The GSO adopted this data to create Local Road Network (LRN) Property Ledger Cards, while the Municipal Accountant acted on the preparation of corresponding LRN Accounting Ledger Cards in the ECPAC System. The Municipal Accountant  recorded the Road Networks in the books, stating the required information for proper disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to include only viable undertakings to be sourced from LDRRM Fund-Continuing Appropriations amounting to P875,953.85 that are deemed most appropriate under the circumstances for prompt implementation after thorough evaluation, subject to approval of the Municipal DRRM Council; and

11. 	Enhance monitoring efforts and strategies to ensure the optimal utilization and implementation of PPAs under the LDRRM fund, particularly the unimplemented continuing appropriations.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer, and the management agreed to submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies and expedite the hiring of individuals from a pool of applicants who are qualified for the position in accordance with the requirements of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The mandatory positions were already published. This shall be followed  recruitment process, including  screening of  applicants, if any, scheduling interviews, and conducting evaluations of application based on qualification standards to ensure that only qualified applicants are selected. Efforts were focused on filling the vacancies as quickly as possible, in compliance with the LGU’s recruitment policies, to ensure that the positions applied are in accordance with the desired staffing pattern established by CSC guidelines and other applicable laws and regulations for recruitment of additional personnel.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile its records on the reported 25% Barangay Share in RPT proceeds in the periods with unremitted balances to submit the required reports as basis of the Municipal Accountant in processing the amount that should have been remitted/distributed as share of each component barangay in the RPT proceeds in accordance with Section 271 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that the barangay can avail of the immediate use of the fund for its viable programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: Real Property Tax (RPT) records were reconciled monthly, with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO). Once reconciled, the barangay share will be promptly transferred to the respective barangays monthly.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommended and the Management agreed for the BAC Chairman, Members, Technical Working Group, and Secretariat to scrutinize the veracity of the documents submitted, particularly a copy of the Construction Safety and Health Program (CoSH)Program duly approved by the DOLE, forming an integral part of the contract for infrastructure projects, pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 prior to issuance of Notice to proceed or where feasible, upon evaluation of bids or during the post-qualification stage.
observations.managementAction: The management integrated in their procurement equire the winning bidder to submit Construction Safety and Health (CoSH) Program duly approved by the DOLE prior to the issuance of Notice to Proceed.

observations.recommendation:
15. 	We recommend, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer to exercise prudence in expediting the process of the selection, recruitment, and promotion from the pool of applicants to deserving individuals whose competence and skills commensurate to the minimum qualification standards, subject to the requirements of the CSC laws and regulations; and

16.	consider an in-depth evaluation of the existing organizational structure and staffing requirements based on the current work demands in individual offices needing significant improvement, subject to availability of funds in the next budget period to achieve optimal benefits in terms of fostering job opportunities and set of qualified personnel as channel in attaining envisioned programs and policies designed to pave the way for improved quality of life among the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Human Resource Office expedited the recruitment and selection process in filling up the 46 vacant positions. This action  involves publishing the remaining vacancies immediately and facilitated the prompt screening and interviewing of applicants  to ensure  that a competent personnel is hired for a position in the selection process within a specified timeline.

observations.managementAction:
An In-house system was developed by CAO to ease the process on Grant of Loan Repayment, as well as reconciliation.

Initially, on October 17, 2022, the CAO has presented the system to CSWDO for comments and suggestions.
 
On August 25, 223, we have communicated to CSWDO our findings on its submitted list of SEA beneficiaries as of Dec. 31, 2022, as well as requesting the same to set a meeting as to the proper orientation of the system to the identified user/s of its office. 

Once it is implemented, we will do the same for CPDO, COOP and Agri.  


CSWDO: 
The CSWDO adheres to the recommendations made by COA and commits on the following to adopt its next program and service implementation:

a. A regular reconciliation on accounting rules and records to stakeholders and City Accountant’s Office is conducted to resolve accounting auditing rules and processes; 
b. A reconciliation reports with the list of active and inactive beneficiaries were submitted to the City Accountant’s Office and requested the latter to write-off the bad debts in accordance to the existing rules and regulations.
 
CDRRMO:
LDRRMO and Central 911 does not have programs/project related to loan grants or risk financing.


CAO has already evaluated the status of the outstanding receivables and eventually requested for write-off of the unpaid loans as implemented by City Coop and City Agri. Some requests were granted, some were denied and some were pending due to lack of necessary documents to be submitted by the concerned offices to COA. 

On November 9, 2023, we have communicated with City Coop and CPDO on Schedule of Loans Receivables-Others for reconciliation.

On November 9, 2023, we have communicated with City Agri requesting them to submit the required and lacking documents to COA to facilitate the request for write-off.

Monthly Status Reports on Cooperative Financial Assistance (CFAP) and Magnegosyo ta Day (MTD) under Davo City Sustainable Livelihood and Entrepreneurship Program (DCSLEP) of the City Cooperative Development Office (CCDO) from January 2019 to October 2023. 



No loan assistance program/project has been submitted and approved for funding for CY 2021 to date. The CAGRO has recommended to disqualify any group with record of not 
paying on-time their loan obligations with the City Government of Davao 
to avail of any future loan assistance, should there be any.

c.A comprehensive guideline on Sustainable livelihood grant program in lieu of the existing livelihood assistance has been proposed and documented for processing and execution by the city mayor to institutionalized the City’s livelihood program, to include the requirement of a Sworn Affidavit of the qualified beneficiaries and provisionally sanction the erring borrowers.  


d.Presently, under existing livelihood assistance, defaulting borrowers were no longer recommended to Livelihood Assiatnce Grant under the program of DSWD and DTI inoredr to sanction them from availing the additional government livelihood programs. 


The CSWDO is still reviewing the existing policies in imposing sanctions. Currently, it is a practice that the defaulting borrowers were not recommended for LAG or DTI fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office to deny collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste and strictly implement the use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers for specific types of wastes. observations.managementAction:
On-going compliance.

1.Conducted regular information, education campaign activities such as Orientation on RA 9003 and establishing BSWMC, reminding barangays of their roles and responsibilities mandated under law. Per RA 9003 and City Ordinance 0361-10, segregation and collection of biodegradable and recyclable wastes are responsibilities of barangays, while residuals and specials wastes are with the City.  

2.Enforced RA 9003 and C.O.0361-10 through issuance of citation tickets to violators. From January to October 2023, a total of 1673 violators were apprehended. 

3.Requested barangays to utilized barangay-owned trucks to undertake collection of garbage and transport residual wastes to the landfill. As of October 2023, a total of 34 barangays have barangay-owned trucks and regularly disposing garbage to the landfill.

4.Submitted Project design for a proposed davao City Central Materials Recovery and Composting Facility. The proposed facility will cater recyclable and biodegradable wastes from barangay sources which lacks these crucial solid waste diversion facilities and ensure segregation will be successful.

observations.recommendation:
7. Reconstitute or reactivate the Inventory Committee through the 
issuance of an executive order. Subsequently, direct them to 
prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for approval of the 
Mayor and submit the same to the Audit Team at least ten (10) 
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking 
activities; and direct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic 
physical count of all PPE to establish its existence. The management 
should establish policy guidelines in the conduct of physical count 
of PPE particularly in coming up with a Physical Inventory Plan 
(PIP). In coordination with the MGSO, the Inventory Committee 
shall plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical 
inventory within the prescribed period; Direct the MGSO to ensure 
timely completion of the required Report on Physical Count of 
Property, Plant, and Equipment and submit the report to the Audit 
Team not later than January 31st of the following year. The RPCPPE 
should indicate the details as to property description, property 
number, unit of measure, acquisition date, cost, balance per card, 
balance on hand and status/condition at the time of inspection of 
each inventoried item; Direct the Municipal Accountant and the 
Municipal GSO to reconcile their respective PPE records to 
establish the correctness of PPE account in the financial 
statements. Accurate accounting and property records should be 
maintained by periodically reconciling both balances and effecting 
adjustments on discrepancies noted. The MGSO should adhere to 
the Chart of Accounts used by the MACCO, their schedules and 
breakdowns. The MGSO should maintain the Property Cards 
indicating therein the critical information necessary for effective 
management, tracking and accounting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management

a. Refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers who have not settled their previous cash advances in compliance with Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445.

b. Enforce appropriate action against accountable officers with long-overdue outstanding cash advances to immediately liquidate or settle their accounts. For cash advances that are identified and qualified to be written off, expedite the request supported with the required documents as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

c. Implement an efficient and effective control procedure in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in conformity with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.reason:
Reiterated.
Refer to Part II - audit findings #17  of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid giving additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not yet settled their previous cash advances. Please strictly follow the guidelines provided under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid recurring noncompliance.

Take appropriate action against accountable officers who have long outstanding cash advances, especially those who are still connected with the agency. Encourage them to immediately settle their accounts according to the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002.

Implement an efficient and effective control procedure in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances. Make sure that the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, are followed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures proper documentation is maintained as per the guidelines mentioned in COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023, to ensure transparency and accountability.
observations.reason:
Reiterated.
Refer to Part II - audit findings #17  of this report.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the accounting and property section should exert extra effort in observing procedures in the accounting and recording of PPEs as required in Section 41 and 56 of the NGAS Manual for a reliable and informative financial statement.

The following steps have to be undertaken:

Accounting Section:

Maintain PPELC for each ledger accounts under the PPE. Start from the current year and backwards.

Determine cut-off period on transaction where data is no longer available for possible requests for authority to write-off as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-001.

Monitor submission of the Physical Count of PPEs and periodically conduct reconciliation for immediate correction in the books of accounts.

Recording unserviceable properties reported in the IIRUP in Other Assets and effect adjustment in the PPE.

Property Section:

Prepare timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory and coordinate with the Inventory Committee members for a cohesive planning.

Update recording of Property Cards to facilitate preparation of the Inventory report to be presented per category of PPEs.

Submit the Inventory report required in Section 56 of the NGAS Manual.

The submitted RCPPE were incomplete and were not reconciled with the book balance.

The Property Section still did not maintain Property Cards

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officers concerned to apply the guidelines and procedures explicitly provided in COA Circular 2020-006 by adopting the following measures: · In coordination with the Property Division/Unit, the Inventory Committee shall plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least, the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory-taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; · Reinforce the Inventory Committee which shall be composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less; · The members of the Inventory Committee shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed; and · Property records shall be updated based on the results of the physical inventory and reconciled with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation, and the management committed to direct the:

a.	Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as the basis for its recognition in the books of accounts.

b. General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components, and secure a copy of the Road Map from the MPDO.

c. Inventory Committee to comply with the prescribed template on the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as illustrated in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008.

d. Municipal Accountant to prepare the ledger card of PPE accounts, including road networks, and henceforth, observe the proper accounting and reporting of road networks and the appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the total road network system in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer apprised the audit team of their action plan to request appropriation for the purchase of the survey equipment to be utilized upon ocular inspection of roads and their components. Moreover, he mentioned that the preparation of the Local Road Network property card would be undertaken, provided all the data needed are complete.
observations.reason: Reiterated through AOM No. 24-001 dated Jan. 15, 2024, and presented in Observation No. 01 in Part II of this Report.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and Municipal General Services Officer to lay down plans and policies in the monitoring of supplies and improvement of the current supply management system through employment of the following: (a) maintenance of supply cards and list of food supplies indicating therein the expiration date; and (b) inter-office monitoring scheme wherein the MGSO have records of supplies and property located and distributed on each office for parallel monitoring; or, other means that Management may find appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has transferred its MDRRM Supply Room in an unused stall in the Municipal Public Market
observations.reason: Control mechanisms were not yet installed since the MGSO has difficulty in monitoring its supplies

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

35. Coordinate with the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committee (BSWMC) to facilitate for the collection of wastes near the national highway and to aid the barangay for the repair of the material recovery facility (MRF) to provide a proper storage facility for the wastes collected;

36. Require the Head, OMM-ENRMD to ensure that collection of garbage from the Barangay is undertaken by the Municipality within the scheduled time; and

37. Facilitate in the establishment of collection points for garbage disposal and strictly monitor that wastes are not piled on undesignated areas through establishing monitoring schemes or through other means that Management may find appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The garbage collection efficiency of the MENRO was not yet properly disseminated to the barangays. MRF were still not properly used and established.

observations.recommendation: 41.  We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Committee to devise an annual LDRRM Plan that has determinable parameters which are quantifiable, qualitative or time-bounded and by which accomplishments or results of PPAs shall be assessed.
observations.managementAction: Performance indicators are being incorporated in the 2024 Budget
observations.reason: Budget preparation of CY 2023 was already undertaken in CY 2022 thereby not incorporating the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

42. Request the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the LDRRM Officer, to plan programs, projects and activities that are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound through undertaking a bottom-up planning approach or through other means that Management may find appropriate;

43. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to expeditiously undertake procurement activities by following the number of calendar days indicated in Republic Act No. 9184; and

44. Require the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to provide mechanisms in the reconciliation of their reports.
observations.managementAction: Performance indicators are being incorporated in the 2024 Budget
observations.reason: Budget preparation of CY 2023 was already undertaken in CY 2022 thereby not incorporating the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

45. Establish a database that will provide interlocking information between the Municipal Assessor’s Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to provide easy access, management and updating of information pertaining to the basic taxes due; and 

46. Evaluate the existing procedures in the assessment and billing of basic taxes and penalties and improve breakdowns in recording through adoption of work-automation processes or any other means that Management may find appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has started the reconciliation of the database of the Municipal Assessor’s Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office
observations.reason: The reconciliation is still on-going due to the volume of documents to be verified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

47. Institute administrative and/ or judicial action against delinquent taxpayers to avoid prescription of the right of the Municipality to collect such taxes; and

48. Evaluate the procedures in the collection of delinquent taxes and introduce improvements in the process to improve collection efficiency on delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Treasurer’s Office is set to undertake the institution of administrative/ judicial action and the improvement of the collection efficiencies of Municipalities in 2024.
observations.reason: The implementation of administrative/ judicial actions on delinquent tax payers is under the supervision of the Provincial Treasurer

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

51. Require the GAD Focal Point Personnel to observe the usage of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tools in GAD planning and to determine the HGDG scores in each activity as basis in the attribution of expenses for GAD-related activities; and

52. Provide corroborative details on reported amounts in the GAD Accomplishment Report to enhance the reliability of reported accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has directed the GAD Office to use of the HGDG Tool in attribution of GAD expenses.
observations.reason: The Municipal GAD Office has an impression that HGDG cannot apply due to the fixed appropriation for GAD activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

53.  Require the GAD Focal Point Personnel to devise a systematic and holistic approach in the identification of GAD-related PPAs to be undertaken in the period through following the guidelines set forth in the PCW– DILG- NEDA- DBM Joint Circular 2013-01 or through other means that may be deemed appropriate; and

54. Instruct the offices in-charge of the implementation of GAD-related PPAs to devise a periodic monitoring and review mechanism that would facilitate in the determination of the status of implementation of each PPA and to provide a feedback mechanism by which constraints found in the implementation could be addressed expeditiously.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has yet to provide procedures on the identification of GAD PPAs.
observations.reason: The Municipal GAD Office has difficulty in the implementation of PPAs due to the lack of review mechanisms in the verification as to the achievability of the proposed PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

55. Request the accounting software provider to facilitate the modification of the inventory valuation to the moving average method; and

56. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the usage of the moving average method in the valuation of inventories and to facilitate in identifying interventions, like manual computation of proper inventory valuation, to accurately present the inventory balance in the operational and financial reports pending the modification of the accounting software.
observations.managementAction: The software provider has not yet modified the program on inventory valuation.
observations.reason: Management has requested the software provider but programming thereof is still on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Municipal Mayor the following:

69. 	Instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to observe proper recordkeeping of inventories through establishing and documenting procedures and policies of inventory management; and,

70. 	Require the Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the semi-annual physical count of inventories by coordinating with the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office;

71. 	Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to periodically reconcile their records to detect and correct any errors in the financial and operational reports.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal General Services Officer has yet to establish procedures and policies of inventory management.



The Municipal General Services Officer has not facilitated for the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories
observations.reason: The Municipal General Services Officer cited the lack of manpower as the reason for the non-implementation.
Management is still formulating mechanisms for the impairment of receivables

The Municipal General Services Office has not provided copies of land titles and other proof of ownership.




The Municipal General Services Office is conducting intra-department reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: 88. We recommended to the management to require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF for year 2020 and thereafter, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: The Municipality has undertaken changes in the officers in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:  
	
95. Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due from LGUs account with that of the reciprocal account Due to LGUs in the books of the Barangays to determine if receivables are still existing and demand liquidation thereof to settle the account.   

96. For dormant receivables and/or fund transfers to the barangays, file appropriate request for the write-off of the dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to: 

97. Exhaust all means to locate the NGOs/POs and the supporting documents of the long outstanding account. Once located, send demand letters for them to settle their accounts; and


98. If the result of preceding recommendation for dormant account renders futile, follow the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2016-005 to request for the write-off of these accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: NGOs and POs not exiting were still being tracked.
observations.reason: Actions are being undertaken by the Municipal Legal office by sending demand letters.

The Market Supervisor and Municipal Treasurer coordinate with each other, update and reconcile their records to determine the accurate amount of uncollected market stall fees and submit report to the Municipal Accountant on the accumulated uncollected amounts for the proper taking up in the books of accounts; and

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned Department Heads to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and provide the detailed description of every item in the Road Network account, on or before June 30, 2024, for proper accounting and inventory.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the CGSO to undertake a comprehensive physical inventory check of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) held by partner National Government Agencies (NGAs) for the purpose of verifying the presence and state of the said PPEs on or prior to June 30, 2024.
Direct the CGSO to issue Demand Letters to responsible officials who have failed to present or produce the assets that have been assigned to their custody.
Direct the CGSO to initiate the formal procedure of requesting the return of unserviceable properties that are currently held by partner NGAs, with the purpose of ensuring their proper disposition.
Require the CGSO to submit a Request for Agenda for Property Transfer to the Sangguniang Panlungsod covering all properties issued to other government agencies.
Require the City Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to derecognize from the books of the City the assets with approved Property Transfer and duly received by the recipient agencies.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to:
Avail of the remedies provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, the one-time cleansing of PPE. This proactive step will facilitate the reconciliation of discrepancies, resulting in reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability. Moreover, it will establish the validity and accuracy of the recorded properties, thereby ensuring the fairness of presentation of the PPE accounts in the Financial Statements (FSs).
Instruct all departments/offices to support and coordinate with the CAO, CGSO and the Inventory Committee in facilitating the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation of the property accounts to establish actual existence, ownership, and condition of the PPEs assigned to their respective department /office.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to:
Instruct CTO to exert additional effort to locate, examine, and provide the necessary documentation for the unrecognized transactions on deposits and unspecified withdrawals;
Instruct CAO to ensure that the dates in the outstanding checks schedule pertains to the check issuance date; and
Direct CAO to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the necessary adjusting entries to present correctly the balances of the affected accounts in the books.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accounting Office (CAO) together with at least two (2) other Offices which management deemed appropriate to partner with the CAO, to coordinate and craft an internal accounting policy on the provision of impairment on receivables that are long overdue and dormant and whose collectability is doubtful in accordance with IPSAS 29.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to:
Require the CGSO thru the created Inventory Committee to conduct a semi-annual physical inventory count with the assistance of the supplies custodians from the different Offices; and
Instruct the Inventory Committee, together with the CGSO and the CAO, to conduct reconciliation of records to come up with a corrected balance of the inventory account and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, as provided in Section 124, Vol. I on the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the BAC and CHO to continue strict adherence to the provisions of the Executive Order No. 49 s. 1993, DOH Administrative Order No. 2012-0023 on the procurement of essential medicines that will be used as reference, and COA Circular 2023-004 regarding the submission of a Certification by the Medical Officer indicating that the requisitioned medicine and drugs are included in the latest edition of the PNF, as an additional documentary requirement.
Instruct CHO to establish a database utilizing the latest edition of the PNF's list of essential medicines as a reference for budgeting the items to be procured. Additionally, employ this database as a meticulous checking mechanism to facilitate thorough review processes for procurement.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Establish a policy and procedure that ensures the information of CTO and CTTMO regarding the collection on Pay Parking matches/reconciles and supported with the order of payments for easy accounting, recording and monitoring purposes.
Review the monthly collections regularly of parking fees whether it agrees to the recorded collections with that of the CTO together with the issued order of payment to avoid the accumulation of variance of up to P2,629,568.00.
Strengthen the internal control compliance/implementation in order to prevent under/over collection, misuse, theft and malversation of funds.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed, for the:
Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all property, plant, and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06;
GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements; and
Municipal Mayor to appoint members of the Inventory and Appraisal Committee who could diligently and willingly devote considerable time to conduct and appraise all properties whose cost cannot be reliably determined due to the unavailability of financial records.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed, for the:
Municipal Agriculturist to conduct assessment on both the physical and the price   changes of the biological asset and submit a report to the Municipal Accountant; and
Municipal Accountant to re-measure biological asset based on the assessment of the Municipal Agriculturist and record the derived measurement in the books.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed, for the:
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a list of cash in bank accounts that remained dormant for so many years with the request to close and transfer these to the General Fund;
Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances from the Trust Fund to the General Fund bank accounts; and
Municipal Budget Officer to consider the balances as source in the preparation of the supplemental or annual budget in the current or ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed, for the:
Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to submit explanation on the incurrence of delays in completing the projects and subsequently determine the appropriate action to be taken whether to issue a Warning Letter or to directly employ punitive actions prescribed by existing guidelines; and
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the balances of those fully implemented projects to the respective National Government Agencies where the funds came from.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal DRRMO to:
Conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects/activities can be identified accordingly using the LDRRM funds- current and prior years and implemented the same thus, optimum utilization of the available funds be achieved; and
Re-program/re-appropriate in the ensuing year the unexpended balance of MOOE from 70% mitigation fund of the current year and the planned PPAs be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and revised AIP.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal HR Officer to:
Clearly define the functions and duties of the CoS/Job Order personnel in the contract/job order and be known and accepted by them prior to rendition of services to facilitate proper evaluation of work performed by these personnel; and
Evaluate the accomplishment reports vis-a-vis the terms/ services agreed upon in the contract.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management  agreed for the GAD Focal Point Person to:
Facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to help the officials concerned in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs and the evaluation of the effectiveness of the same;  and
Properly mainstream GAD concerns in the local development plans, especially in the CDP.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed for the BAC to:
Stop the practice of purchasing supplies and materials thru reimbursement basis. The provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) must be adhered strictly; and
Determine and recommend to the LCE the proper mode of procurement to be done in every procurement activity.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all property, plant, and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06;
2. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements; and
3. Municipal Mayor to appoint members of the Inventory and Appraisal Committee who could diligently and willingly devote considerable time to conduct and appraise all properties whose cost cannot be reliably determined due to the unavailability of financial records.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to::
1.  Strictly observe the prescribed frequency of deposit of collections/receipts (once a week or upon reaching P10,000.00). If impracticable, at least 3 to 4 times a month. Assignment of regular and bonded staff to deposit the collections frequently could be considered;
2. Deposit  all collections intact and avoid retaining part of the collections as cash reserve for cash disbursements. This is in compliance with Section 8 of COA Circular 92-382 to safeguard public funds from any improper/illegal use; and
Set up a petty cash fund to cover petty disbursements. Any expenses not considered petty should be paid through check and not from collections. This is to implement sound cash management in the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed  for the:
1. Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to submit explanation on the incurrence of delays in completing the projects and subsequently determine the appropriate action to be taken whether to issue a Warning Letter or to directly employ punitive actions prescribed by existing guidelines; and
2. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the balances of those fully implemented projects to the respective National Government Agencies where the funds came from.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management  agreed for the:
1.  Members   of the Municipal Development Council to (a) establish parameters to be used in the appraisal and prioritization of programs and projects; and, (b) maintain an effective line of communication with the proponents and implementing offices to ensure that the planned programs and projects are implemented;
2. Implementing Office concerned to strictly observe the timelines set and to religiously coordinate with the Municipal Development Council in the execution of planned activities; and,
3. Personnel concerned in the Office of the Municipal Mayor to (a) closely monitor the implementation of the projects; and, (b) devise a mechanism by which status of project implementation is reflected as a parameter in measuring the individual performance of the implementing offices and employees.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal DRRM Officer to:
1. Conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects/activities under LDRRM funds be reasonably attained in the current year thus, optimum utilization of the available funds be achieved; and
2. Ensure that unexpended balance of MOOE from the 70% mitigation fund of the current year be reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing year and the planned PPAs be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and the revised AIP.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Implement a system for tracking and monitoring cash advances granted to employees, ensuring adherence to rules and regulation;
2. Cause the deduction from the employee’s salary the long outstanding unliquidated cash advances; and
3. Refrain from granting new cash advances unless the previous ones have been fully liquidated.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the GAD Focal Point Person  to:
1. Facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to help the officials concerned in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs and the evaluation of the effectiveness of the same, and
2.  Properly mainstream GAD concerns in the local development plans, especially in the CDP.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the::
1. Municipal General Services Officer to fill out completely and properly the AIR;
2. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply strictly all the requirements stipulated in the New Procurement Law or R.A 9184 and its implementing guidelines particularly on the completeness of supporting documents to be attached to the DV prior to submission to the Municipal Accountant; and .
3. Municipal Accountant to see to it that all payments for the purchase of goods are supported with supporting documents that are filled out properly and completely before affixing his signature in Box A.  Due diligence is requested particularly in reviewing the dates of the transactions which should be in chronological order to avoid audit suspension and foster transparency of government transactions without creating any malice or doubt.

Recommendations:
