We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly journals, reports, financial statements with the supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed legal due dates. The attached Annex A will serve as guide in achieving completeness and timely submission in compliance with each specific legal basis; and
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain appropriate Records/logbooks for transmitted documents for proper identification of responsible persons who caused the delay, and to establish effective internal control system and sound fiscal administration.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Municipal Accountant to reclassify all tangible items acquired prior to CY 2022 and
below the new capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 from the PPE account to the
appropriate semi-expendable property accounts, or expense accounts/prior period
adjustment in case of issued semi-expendable property pursuant to Paragraphs 4.3 to
4.6 of COA Circular 2022-004;
2. OIC-Municipal General Services Officer to issue Annex A.3 - Inventory Custodian
Slip (ICS) for issued semi-expendable property to establish accountability over them
pursuant to Paragraph 4.7.3 of COA Circular 2022-004 and the procedures in Part II of
Annex B thereof, particularly:
• Step No. 5 - For the issue of semi-expendable property, the Property and/or
Supply Custodian shall prepare ICS. Separate ICS control number shall be
assigned for low-valued and high-valued semi-expendable property;
3. OIC-MGSO to maintain Annex A.4 - Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued
(RegSPI) pursuant to Paragraph 4.7.4. This registry shall be maintained in the Property
and/or Supply Division/Unit for each class of issued semi-expendable property. The
Property and/or Supply Custodian shall record promptly the issue, return, reissue,
disposal, and other information about the property if returned and the
description/information about the asset. It shall be maintained by fund. ICS shall be
maintained continuously and recorded in sequential manner to keep track of any
missing ICS; (Note: This functions similarly to SPC, however only a registry is
recommended to be maintained since issued semi-expendable property are already
derecognized in the books of accounts.);
4. OIC-MGSO to segregate the reporting of unserviceable semi-expendable property
from the unserviceable PPE. The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Semi-Expendable Property (IIRUSP) should be used to report unserviceable semi-
expendable property and IIRUP to report PPE for disposal and submit the same to the
Audit Team for inspection by COA TAS;
5. OIC-MGSO to prepare annually a separate Report on the Physical Count of Semi-
Expendable Property (RPCSP) to report the physical count of semi-expendable
property which are owned by the agency/entity, by type of property still in the custody
of the Property and/or Supply Division/Unit as at a given date;
6. OIC-MGSO to segregate and exclude semi-expendable property from the list of non-
existing/missing PPE before availing/applying for the one-time cleansing of PPE. The
list should only reflect PPE items costing P50,000.00 and above to efficiently facilitate
the request for authority for derecognition of PPE without available record of
accountability;
7. Municipal Accountant to clean the books of accounts first or take up the necessary
written off from the books of accounts the accounting entries to write-off/drop from the
books of accounts the remaining balances as of December 31, 2023 of any amounts
lumped under the “Unreconciled SL”, which were created to facilitate the conversion
of accounts from the Old GAS to the NGAS and/or from the manual accounting
system to the eNGAS, that cannot anymore be reconciled with the property records
before applying for the one-time cleansing of PPE; and
8. OIC-MGSO, (before applying for the disposal and one-time cleansing of PPE), to
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant the final list of properties to be disposed
and/or derecognized and see to it that they have matching SLs to ensure the accuracy
of data applied for disposal or derecognition.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the OIC-MGSO to prepare/issue
Property Return Slip (PRS) to the person and/or office returning the
obsolete/unserviceable property immediately upon receipt thereof, indicating therein
details such as property description and property number, duly signed by the person
returning the property and the personnel receiving the returned property. Afterward,
cancel the corresponding ARE, if any.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Inventory Committee to
immediately tag with Property Stickers all PPE items counted following the suggested
information provided under Paragraphs 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal CBMS Focal Person or
the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to submit to the Audit Team a
copy of the notarized Tripartite MOA pursuant to Section 3.2.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG
JMC No. 01 dated April 27, 2023 and Section A. 1(c) of PSA Office Memorandum No.
2022-219 dated September 22, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Engineer to:
1. Enforce strict monitoring on the status of infrastructure projects through calibrated
responses to delays in the performance of the contractor pursuant to GPPB rules to
ensure the timely implementation of infrastructure projects; and
2. Perform a careful review of the correctness of the data/documents submitted by the
Contractor on every progress/final billing before recommending the approval of
payment.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal CBMS Focal Person /
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
1. Secure and submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Oath of Data Privacy sworn to by
the Third-Party Service Provider LGUSUITE, INC., pursuant to the Data Privacy Act
of 2012, DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 01 dated April 27, 2023 and PSA Office
Memorandum No. 2022-219 dated September 22, 2022; and
2. Require as part of final billing/payment requirement the submission of a certificate or
document issued by LGUSUITE, INC. to the fieldworkers as proof of their turn-over
of all devices, storage media, and files, whether paper-based or electronic, and all
access thereto at the end of the project.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Budget Officer to
allocate funds for the titling of lots and the hiring of a Legal Retainer, in coordination
with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, to fast-track the processing of Certificate of
Land Title to establish ownership of the land to avoid possible disputes over
ownership claims by third persons and protect the legal interest of the LGU pursuant
to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify all tangible items on the new capitalization threshold of below P50,000.00 from
the PPE account to the appropriate semi-expendable property accounts, or expense
accounts/prior period adjustment in case of issued semi-expendable property pursuant to
COA Circular 2022-004.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Municipal Accountant to follow strictly the perpetual inventory method, that is, to
record to an Inventory account the supplies and materials purchased or received by the
agency; and
2. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare a Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) on every
issuance and summarize these RISs in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI). Further, submit separately the SSMI with the RIS to the Municipal
Accountant for proper recording of the issuances.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to observe
the drawing of cash advance for local travel instead of direct charges to travelling
expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-004. Consequently, observe the
procedures for liquidation thereof pursuant to the Circular and regularly prepare the
necessary quarterly and semestral reports on unliquidated cash advances to monitor these
balances.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended and the Management agreed to issue an Executive Order (EO)
reconstituting the membership of the Inventory Committee, taking into account the
issue at hand and, specifying in the said EO the functions and responsibilities of the
Committee.
2. Further, it was recommended and the Municipal Officials concerned agreed, for the:
A. Inventory Committee to:
a. Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) incorporating the procedural
guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020, for approval by the Municipal Mayor;
b. Submit the approved PIP to the Office of the Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
c. Conduct the actual physical count of PPE, which should be completed
within the time indicated in the PIP;
d. Prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team within the
prescribed reglementary period.
B. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to reconcile their respective
PPE records to establish the correctness of PPE account in the financial
statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Municipal Mayor to issue an Executive Order to create an Inventory Committee to
conduct a complete and periodic physical count of all Supplies and Materials
Inventory account in compliance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
2. MGSO to prepare the Stock Cards of all Inventory accounts as a preparatory activity
in the conduct of physical count;
3. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Supplies Ledger Cards of all Inventory accounts
for reconciliation with the records of the Municipal General Services Office; and
4. Inventory Committee to submit on time the required Report of the Physical Count of
Inventory to the MGSO so that necessary action can be effected if the items are
already utilized or consumed. The Report shall likewise be submitted to the
Accounting Office for subsequent reconciliation with the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the BAC Chairman to submit a copy
of the approved contracts and POs to COA within five (5) working days from issuance
thereof, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal OIC-MDRRMO to
prepare the Municipality’s DRRM Implementing Plan and thereafter, furnish the Audit
Team with a copy thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to establish a M&E System and issue an
Executive Order creating the M&E Team so that proper monitoring on the
implementation and the evaluation of the effectiveness of GAD policies and PPAs will be
achieved.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Members of the Municipal Development Council to (a) establish parameters to be
used in the appraisal and prioritization of programs and projects; and (b) maintain an
effective line of communication with the proponents and implementing offices to
ensure that the planned programs and projects are implemented;
2. Implementing Office concerned to strictly observe the timelines set and to religiously
coordinate with the Municipal Development Council in the execution of planned
activities; and
3. Personnel concerned in the Office of the Municipal Mayor to (a) closely monitor the
implementation of the projects; and, (b) devise a mechanism by which status of project
84
implementation is reflected as a parameter in measuring the individual performance of
the implementing offices and employees.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Municipal DRRM Officer to conduct adequate planning so that programmed
projects/ activities under LDRRM funds be reasonably attained in the current year,
thus optimum utilization of the available funds may be achieved; and
2. Municipal Accountant to transfer the current year’s unobligated/unexpended QRF
and MOOE to the Special Trust Fund “Trust Liability-DRRMF account.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
1. Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries, if deemed necessary, after
earnest effort to reconcile the beginning balance of the account. Otherwise, the
payable should be evaluated for write-off following the guidelines under COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
2. Municipal Treasurer to remit regularly future taxes withheld from compensation and
from suppliers concerned within the reglementary period following Revenue
Regulation No. 2-98 and other pertinent laws.

2. Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical count of all the Property, Plant, and Equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06; and simultaneously the Appraisal and Disposal Committee shall appraise unserviceable equipments.
3. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively, and to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements.

4. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant computes for the correct depreciation of the PPE items through the preparation of a lapsing schedule, and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry so that the correct value of the PPE will be presented in the financial statements.

5) Municipal Accountant to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a list of cash in bank accounts that remained dormant for so many years with the request to close and transfer these to the General Fund; 6) Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances from the Trust Fund to the General Fund proper bank accounts; and 7) Municipal Budget Officer to consider the balances as source in the preparation of the budget.

8. We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to return the unutilized funds granted by national government agency so that this could be used by other government agencies for noble purposes.

9. Municipal Engineer to hasten the implementation and completion of the subject development projects thru efficient monitoring and inspection; 10. BAC to properly plan the procurement process of all identified priority development projects under the 20% DF to guarantee the prompt awarding of Contracts to winning bidder and to facilitate timely awarding of Contracts to same; and 11. Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to compute the penalties or liquidating damages for the delayed projects so that the contractor may expedite the completion of the projects.

12. Conduct adequate planning so that projects/activities under LDRRM funds be reasonably attained in the current year; and 13. Ensure that unexpended balance of MOOE from the 70% mitigation fund of the current year be reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing year and the planned PPAs be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and the revised AIP.

14. Facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to help the officials concerned in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs, and in the evaluation of the effectiveness of the same; and 15. Properly mainstream GAD concerns in the local development plans, especially in the CDP.

16. Municipal Accountant to submit the journals, reports, financial statements with the supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed legal due dates. The attached Annex A will serve as guide in achieving completeness and timely submission in compliance with each specific legal basis; and 17. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain appropriate Records/logbooks for transmitted documents for proper identification of responsible persons who caused the delay, and to establish effective internal control system and sound fiscal administration.

18. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a list of cash in bank accounts that remained dormant for so many years with the request to close them and transfer to Trust Fund Proper and for MHO bank account request for re-activate if it is still usable.
19. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances to the Trust Fund’s Proper bank accounts.

20. Direct the Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all the property, plant, and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06; and 21. Direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements.

22. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the proper depreciation of the PPE items through the preparation of a lapsing schedule and subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry so that the correct value of the PPE will be presented in the financial statements.

23. The Municipal Engineer to hasten the implementation and completion of the subject development projects thru efficient monitoring so that the interest expenses incurred could be recouped thru the earnings that will be generated when these projects will be put into use.

24. The Planning Office to make representation with the source agency and request for the retention of the fund for the immediate implementation of the intended project/program.
25. The Implementing Offices to fast track the implementation of the program/ project intended for the Municipality’s benefit to avoid the fund’s return to its source, thus optimally utilizing government’s resources.

26. Facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to help the officials concerned in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs, and to be implemented for coming year.
27. Direct the Officials concerned to ensure proper mainstreaming of GAD concerns in the local development plans, especially in the CDP.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers to ensure the accuracy of the reported balances of the cash. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant to fast track the full validation of the identified reconciling items for preparation of necessary adjustments and journal entries to account for the same in the books, make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the cash in bank balance and recognize the corresponding liability for the stale checks, and submit the bank reconciliation statements for the four (4) dormant accounts as discussed above.

As a reiteration of prior year’s audit recommendations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Inventory/Property Committee to conduct an effective and complete physical inventory of all PPE items of the Municipality and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE with remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.
Reconcile the results thereof with the available accounting records maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office to determine unrecorded properties and other differences for further validation. Thereafter, submit a report on the results of the reconciliation made for review and verification.
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for approval by the Municipal Mayor, and submit the approved PIP to the Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking activities.
Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability.
Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that qualify under peace and order.
Management’s Comments:
As justified by the Municipal Accountant (formerly the OIC of the Municipal Budget Office), the PPAs for peace and order were duly approved by the MPOC and subsequently adopted by the SB. The MPOC has the necessary tools to categorize the PPAs for peace and order and public safety, recommending strategic actions or activities aimed at promoting and improving peace and order within the Municipality. However, following the audit team's observation regarding the inappropriate inclusion of non-peace and order PPAs in the computation of the Confidential Fund (CF), management will notify the MPOC to rectify these inaccuracies in the current Peace and Order Public Safety (POPS) Plan and future transactions.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
While the audit team recognized the significant impact of these projects and programs on the peace and order and public safety of the community, it was underscored that the LGU is not precluded from implementing the same programs provided that these shall not be included in the total amount appropriated for peace and order for the purpose of computing the confidential fund as these activities do not align with the definition of peace and order activities as provided in the DILG Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor’s Office submit to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documents and all other requirements pertaining to the land acquisition as prescribed in the COA Circular 2012-001, in order to preclude disallowances or suspensions in the audit.
We recommended that the Local Development Council promptly prepare and submit the necessary AIP or supplemental investment program for the purpose and to be approved by the local sanggunian.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
Conduct a thorough verification on the nature/ purpose of these funds to determine the status of the projects covered by such funds;
Determine if the remaining amounts pertain to unutilized portion of the funds received after project completion and cause the remittance/ return of such to the source agencies, or direct the personnel in-charge of the implementation of the above projects to prepare a letter request allowing the transfer of the unutilized amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund for the LGU’s utilization subject to the approval of the donor agency; and
For ongoing projects with remaining balances in the books, coordinate with the source agencies to reconcile the balances with their records and prepare the necessary correcting entries (if any) to arrive at the accurate balances of the Due to NGAs account as at reporting date; and
4.   Coordinate with those offices that have received the funds from the source agencies to reconcile and verify the accuracy of the unexpended balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to specifically describe or itemize the programs, projects and activities for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and in the Annual Budget. Otherwise, secure the approval or authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects not specifically identified in the Appropriation Ordinance. Furthermore, during planning, the MDC shall ensure that PPAs for inclusion in the AIP are based on actual proposals from different stakeholders with cost estimates that are based on approved program of works. Lastly, provide justifications for the above-mentioned deficiencies.

We recommended the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to review the provisions outlined in R.A. 9679 and secure a written agreement and policy regarding upgrading monthly Pag-IBIG contributions;
2. For the Human Resource Management Officer to secure a “Request to Update Membership Savings” form for those employees who want to contribute more than the mandatory contributions; and
3. For the Municipal Accounting Office to apply the latest provisions for Pag-IBIG contributions for CY 2024 onwards since there is a mandatory upgrade of monthly contributions from P100.00 to P200.00 starting February 2024.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the computation of income tax due and to make the necessary adjustments in the amount of taxes withheld, as warranted. Furthermore, enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office to be updated with the latest BIR issuances to ensure accuracy in the computation of taxes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing R.A. No. 11313, and ensure compliance with the posting requirements for proper dissemination of the ordinance. Furthermore, ensure the inclusion of the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Require the department heads/designated supervisors to prepare and/or revise the statement of duties and responsibilities. This document should outline the specific tasks to 	be completed within a month, ensuring that actual accomplishments align with the office's requirements.
Require job orders to prepare and submit accomplishment reports that are specific in terms of actual duties performed such that proper evaluation may be made on the necessity of hiring/re-hiring.
We recommended to the Management to adopt agency guidelines on the use of reimbursement as a mode of payment, outlining the conditions and thresholds for resorting to the same.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the offices concerned to review the status of implementation of unimplemented projects, determine the cause of delay and provide an action plan to immediately address the issues.
Moreover, direct the MPDO and MEO to exercise diligently their monitoring function such that issues and concerns that cause delays in the project implementation could be immediately identified and addressed.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record immediately and appropriately all the identified unrecorded PPE items.
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct an effective and complete physical inventory of all PPE items of the Municipality, and require the GSO to update the RPCPPE with details on the date of acquisition of PPE, accumulated depreciation, and remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.
Reconcile the results thereof with the available accounting records maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office to determine unrecorded properties and other differences for further validation and subsequent correction in records maintained. Thereafter, submit a report on the results of the reconciliation made for review and verification.
Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations and the Management agreed:
For the management to secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan through a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement to transfer the above-mentioned PPAs;
For the General Service Officer to facilitate the transfer of the subject PPE items or structures to the end-user agencies through a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement and forward a copy of the document to the Municipal Accountant as the basis for the derecognition; and
For the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize these PPE items from the books of the agency.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed:
For the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card for roads and road components for every road network as well as working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components;
Facilitate reconciliation between the records of MACCO and GASO on local road networks and effect necessary adjustments; and
Provide accurate depreciation for each road component and includes disclosure on the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
For the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network with detailed information on road components for CY 2023 and in the succeeding years.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjusting entries and ensure the correct recording of financial transactions using the prescribed charts of accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to fast track the full validation of the identified reconciling items for preparation of necessary adjustments and journal entries to account for the same in the books;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the cash in bank balance and recognize the corresponding liability for the stale checks; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the nature of the balances reflected in the closed bank accounts and to submit to this office the results thereof together with certifications from the bank on the existence of the balances such that an evaluation may be made on whether losses were incurred and who shall be responsible for such losses. After verification, record the adjusting entries in the books to reflect the correct ending balance of Cash in Bank account.

Recommendation:

We reiterated the following recommendations:
Stop the practice of incurring expenses and making payments therefore despite the lack of a budget for the purpose;
Exercise prudence in the use of government funds;
Intensify the collections and other revenue-generating activities  to increase the current cash balance; and
For the Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence in monitoring the availability of cash prior to the issuance of checks, and for the Municipal Budget Officer, to be more circumspect in the conduct of her duties by ensuring that all claims against the agency are backed up by sufficient appropriation.
Correspondingly, replenish immediately the amount utilized for unrelated expenses to ensure the implementation of the PPAs in the current year and submit a written justification on the aforementioned deficiency for us to be able to make a decision in the audit.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to implement the following:
Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability; and
Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations:
Secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan through a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement to transfer the above-mentioned PPE items; and
The Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize these PPE items from the books of the agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
Strictly adhere to the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for CF specifically on the complete documentary requirements and submission of the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within seven (7) days after release of the check.
Ensure that the disbursing officer is adequately bonded. An increase in accountability shall result in an increase in the amount of bond and subject to payment of additional premium.
Stop the practice of granting cash advance to accountable officers with unliquidated balances such that the total cash advance in his/her custody at a given period does not exceed his/her maximum accountability.

Due to the inclusion of non-peace and order activities as defined under Section 5.1.3.1 of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015, the total confidential fund released in CY 2023 amounting to P1,800,000.00 exceeded the allowed confidential fund by P1,215,000.00.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that qualify under peace and order.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations:
Conduct a thorough verification on the nature/ purpose of these funds to determine the status of the projects covered by such funds;
Determine if the remaining amounts pertain to an unutilized portion of the funds received after project completion and cause the remittance/ return of such to the source agencies, or direct the personnel in-charge of the implementation of the above projects to prepare a letter request allowing the transfer of the unutilized amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund for the LGU’s utilization subject to the approval of the donor agency; and
For ongoing projects with remaining balances in the books, coordinate with the source agencies to reconcile the balances with their records and prepare the necessary correcting entries (if any) to arrive at the accurate balances of the Due to NGAs account as at the reporting date.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Health Officer to provide justifications including the supporting documents, for failure to secure copies of the DTR, logbook, its equivalent, or an HRMO certification in lieu of the DTRs attesting to the number of hours physically reported for work.

Recommendation:

We recommended the following:
For the agency officials to exercise prudence in the use of government funds and to ensure that measures and strategies as outlined in the approved plans are cost-effective in order to attain maximum results notwithstanding budgetary constraints; and
Provide the justifications necessary to substantiate the expenses for the audit team to evaluate and come up with an audit decision.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management, through the MDRRMO, revisit its planning process and consider other areas/barangays as highly vulnerable to flooding and rain-induced landslides, as identified in the geo-hazard maps of the MGB-DENR RO XI, in the identification and allocation of resources for preparedness and mitigation-related PPAs under the LDRRM Fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to implement following courses of action:
1. Require the department heads/designated supervisors to prepare and/or revise the statement of duties and responsibilities. This document should outline the specific tasks to be completed within a month, ensuring that actual accomplishments align with the office's requirements; and
2. Require job orders to prepare and submit accomplishment reports that are specific in terms of actual duties performed such that proper evaluation may be made on the necessity of hiring/re-hiring.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing R.A. No. 11313, and ensure compliance with the posting requirements to ensure proper dissemination of the ordinance. Further, ensure the inclusion of the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to implement the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to review the provisions outlined in R.A. 9679 and secure a written agreement and policy regarding upgrading monthly Pag-IBIG contributions;
2. For the Human Resource Management Officer to secure a “Request to Update Membership Savings” form for those employees who want to contribute more than the mandatory contributions; and
3. For the Municipal Accounting Office to apply the latest provisions for Pag-IBIG contributions for CY 2024 onwards since there is a mandatory upgrade of monthly contributions from P100.00 to P200.00 starting February 2024.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the computation of income tax due and to make the necessary adjustments in the amount of taxes withheld, as warranted. Further, enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office to be updated with the latest BIR issuances to ensure accuracy in the computation of taxes.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management submit immediately the lacking documents, otherwise, the penal provision under Section 122 of PD 1445 shall be applied. Furthermore, adopt measures to ensure the timely submission of transaction documents to avoid the issuance of a Notice of Suspension on agency transactions.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to:
Initiate the needed coordination, monitoring and evaluation on the implementation of all priority development projects within the budget year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF by proper planning to address the conditions/limitations that hinder the timely implementation thereof;
Ensure that projects included in the 20% DF have a long-term effect/benefit in the socio-economic conditions of the constituents; and
Prioritize the allocation of funds for the implementation of proposed development projects in the LDIP.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Management the following:
For the Municipal Engineering Office to prioritize the preparation and submission of the DED and other requirements for the implementation of the NTF ELCAC projects;
Follow-up with the DILG on the result of the review and assessment of the POW, detailed engineering design and other documents such that the BAC may proceed to the conduct of procurement activities for these projects; and,
Direct the MPDO and MEO to exercise diligently their monitoring function such that issues and concerns that cause a delay in the project implementation such as lack of necessary tools for DED preparation, could be immediately identified and addressed.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Managementto instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare timelythe BRS taking all the noted deficiencies to anise ataccurate Cash in Bank Statement regularly balances. Likewise, observe the prescribeddeadlines for the submission of the said BRS.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Accounting Department to conduct verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances for those who are not connected with the Municipality to determine the extent of its collectability. In case the collectability is not feasible, file an appropriate request for the approval of COA Memorandum No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended the following Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to file an appropriate request for the approval of COA to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant accountspursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2023-008 datedAugust 17, 2023; and the Municipal Accountant toconduct regular and periodic verification, analysis andvalidation of the existence of all fund transfers.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct GSO to coordinate with the respective offices to submit the liquidation/utilization reports together with the Relief Distribution Sheet (RDS) on any proof of distribution to the bonafide beneficiaries to enable theMunicipal Accountant to prepare an appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended to the Management to strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations on the preparation andsubmission of the required RPCPPE. Likewise, ensurethe full implementation of the "One-lime cleansing ofPPE accounts" and undertake all other procedures asprovided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come upwith reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Management the following: • Prioritize and carefully plan the project implementation to ensure it will meet both immediate community needs and long-term development goals. Issue demand letters to the barangays for any unliquidated fund transfers and implement measures to prevent future occurrences of unliquidated fund transfers; and • Conduct a comprehensive review of the unimplementable procurement specifications and adjust the ABC to align with prevailing market rates. This may involve revisiting the budgeting process and considering factors such as inflation and fluctuating market conditions to ensure a more realistic budget estimate.
We recommended that management prioritize and fast-track the implementation of the project funded by NTF-ELCAC in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to immediately commence construction.

We recommended and management agreed to the following courses of action: • The Municipal Engineer to implement an internal control system for the usage of fuel. Effectively control/monitor the usage of fuel through the proper observance of the rules and regulations pertaining to the usage of fuel. Regularly check and verify the correctness of the DTT prepared by the drivers. Ensure that the GSO regularly submits to COA a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. • The General Services Office (GSO) to submit a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the COA Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the succeeding month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of the Procuring Entity, direct the MEO and BAC to provide a written justification for the deficiencies noted in the conduct of procurement of fuel, for further evaluation by the audit team.

• The Municipal Mayor being the head of the agency shall ensure that all report and documents required under the law, rules and regulations must be properly complied with or observed by the personel concerned; Submit RDS as proof of distribution of relief goods. • The OIC-GSO in coordination with the OIC-MDDRMO, submit the required SSMI together with the required documents such as the Reliefs Distribution Sheet or distribution list as proof of distribution to the Auditor. • The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submitthe Journal Entry Voucher to the office of the Auditor.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management through its responsible offices like the MDRRM Office, and Bids and awards Committee (BAC) hasten the implementation or procurement of the above mentioned project and any Programs/ Project/Activities (ppAs) related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, and prevention.

We recommended and management agreed that the MDRRMO shall prepare the monthly Report of Source and Utilization of MDRRMF to be certified by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC andLocal Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

We reiterated and Management agreed to our recommendation to submit immediately the reported lacking vouchers to preclude the issuance of the Notices of Suspension. Also, personnel concerned to strictly observe and submit the required reports within the prescribed deadlines to effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their validity, propriety, completeness and accuracy.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following courses of action: • To formulate a uniform policy and procedures in the engagement of JO and Contract of Services workers so that the necessity and propriety of hiring job order and contract of services employees are justified. Also, JOs under the Legislative Department who are contracted to gather data under research activity, specific target date and population in a certain barangay should be provided in the Activity Design, for us to determine the propriety of the accomplishments reported; • To establish standardized contract templates for JOs and COs that clearly outline their duties, responsibilities, working hours, compensation, and any other relevant terms and conditions; and • Require the department heads/designated supervisors to strictly monitor attendance especially the arrival and departure time of the job order and contract of service personnel to ensure that the required number of working hours per day is strictly adhered.

We recommended the Management instruct Municipal Accountant to prepare timely the BRS, taking all the noted deficiencies to arrive at accurate Cash-in-Bank balances to be presented on the Financial Statements. Likewise, observe the prescribed deadlines for the submission of the said BRS.

We recommended that the management reconstruct the records of the above-unliquidated cash advances and other receivables. Maintain a General Ledger and a subsidiary ledger for each of the accountable officers or debtors for easy examination and enforcement of the account. This may also facilitate the approval of the accounts already requested for written-off. Also, conduct verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability.

Moreover, we recommended that the Management instruct the MDRRM and Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to fast-track the procurement of the subject rescue vehicle andexpedite the procurement and delivery ofthe same.

We recommended that the Management: • Instruct the Municipal Accountant and other offices concerned to facilitate the return of the unutilized balances of the funds pertaining to the completed projects to the SA, unless otherwise it is provided in the Memorandum of Agreement the IA can utilize the remaining funds after the completion of the project; • Instruct the offices in charge of the implementation of PPAs and determine the status of implementation. Likewise, traced back who are the grantors of the funds so that the accounting office can prepare the appropriate liquidation report to the identified SAs, otherwise, remittance to the Bureau of Treasury (BTr) will be made; and • Fast-track the implementation of the ongoing PPAs and returned immediately any excess funds to the SA.

We recommended that management shall submit immediately the reported lacking vouchers to preclude the issuance of Notices of Suspension. Also, personnel concerned shall strictly observe and submit the required reports within the prescribed deadlines to effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their validity, propriety, completeness and accuracy.

We recommended that management prioritize the implementation of projects funded by NGAs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines, and judiciously plan its implementation such that prompt action can be made to address the issues/constraints causing the non-implementation.

We recommended that the LGU shall take advantage of the latest technology by adopting the system of payroll payment through an automated teller machine of Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) - Mati City, Davao Oriental, its depository bank, in order to strengthen control of cash management.

1. For the Municipal Accountant, prepare and maintain the property ledger cards of the purchase property and equipment in CY 2021 and ultimately all the corresponding PPE ledger cards for all recorded PPE in the books. 2. For the GSO, prepare and maintain the property cards of the identified property and equipment that were acquired in CY 2021 and all PPE Cards of the PPE owned by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1. Direct the Chairperson of the said task force to exertall possible efforts in locating and collecting themissing or lacking documents necessary to transfer thetitle of the aforresaid parcels of land in the name of theLGU in order not to incur a further delay inestablishing the ownership of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor to ensure the full implementation of the One time Cleansing of PPE accounts and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Accounting Office shall maintain the special accounts in the General Fund in accordance with the abovementioned guidelines.

We recommended that management shall adhere stricly to the time frame in which the ESWMP be implemented and facilitate the establishment of MRFs in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required under Section 32 of RA No. 9003 for the welfare of the entire populace of the municipality.

We recommended that management shall initiate collection of the overpaid amount to the contractor in the total amount of P7,574,368.44 Also taken to initials collection submit justification on other deficiencies noted by COA TASmentioned in the attached Technical Evaluation Reports.

Expedite the implementation of intended PPAs for which the funds were received and return to source agencies the residual amounts of completed projects. Otherwise, revert the same to the General Fund of the municipality.
The treasurer shall reconcile his cashbook balance with the collection daily and also with that of the ledger balance maintained by the Accountant at least once in every quarter.

We require immediate submission of the aforementioned reports and documents to allow timelyevaluation, analysis, and audit of accounts andtransactions. Also management is reminded pursuant toSec. 39 (3) of PD 1445, no-submission of pertinent reports without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action as well as for disallowing permanently a claim under examination.

Direct the Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts to make possible the verification of the propriety and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank balances on the financial statements during the year and to effect on the financial statements during the year and to effect the necessary correction adjustments found in the course of reconciliation.

Replenish immediately the amount of P1,200,000.00 to the trust fund so as not to cause further delay in the implementation of the intended programs/project. Likewise file appropriate charges against the formerLocal Chief Executive Mayor Dumaan and other personel found liable on technical malversation of public fund.

The treasurer shall reconcile his cashbook balance with the collection daily and also with that of the ledger balance maintained by the Accountant at least once in every quarter. The Accounting unit who it tasked to perfom the internal audit function of the municipality shall ascertain the accuracy of the general ledgeraccount and shall the following procedures as follows: Trace entries in the general ledger to the MunicipalTreasurer and verify footings and balance.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Management the following:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to send notices to the payees concerned on the existence of these checks that had become stale;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting journal entries to restore the Cash in Bank account and the liabilities of the LGU to these creditors; and
Comply with the requirements outlined in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual on the cancellation of the stale checks.

Recommendations:
We reiterated the following recommendations for Management to:
Secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan through a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement to transfer the above-mentioned PPAs; and
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize these PPE items from the books of the agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management expedite the transfer of ownership of the aforesaid parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the government. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a personnel who shall be primarily responsible for the retrieval of necessary documents and expediting the titling process of these parcels of land.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Management the following courses of action:
Direct the BAC to carefully review its procurement process, including all the supporting documents for every transaction, to ensure that succeeding procurement activities are in accordance with rules and regulations and in order to promote integrity in the procurement process of the agency;
Enjoin the BAC, Technical Working Group and Secretariat to attend relevant training on RA 9184, its IRR and other issuances of the GPPB; and
Submit a written justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies for further evaluation by the audit team.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed:
For the MPDC and MDC to properly identify and itemize the programs and projects for inclusion in the AIP and to provide specific project description, location and such other details;
For the OIC-MBO to review the budget allocation for proposed programs and projects in the AIP and to ensure that the funding requirements are classified under the proper expenditure categories (i.e. PS, MOOE, Capital Outlay); and
For the OIC-MBO to discontinue the practice of including in the continuing appropriations the unexpended balance of MOOE.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Development Council to promptly prepare and submit the necessary AIP or supplemental investment program for the purpose to be approved by the local sanggunian.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the HEA Focal Person to provide justifications for the excessive claims of the Health Emergency Allowance of the healthcare workers and to ensure verification of DTRs as to the number of hours physically present at work before making the CREC report. Additionally, we recommend that those in charge of health facilities and the head of office should check and validate the propriety of the DTRs of the healthcare workers under their supervision.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management provide necessary justifications for the unauthorized transfer and use of the trust fund without prior approval from source agencies and to ensure strict compliance with the above-mentioned circular for succeeding transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that qualify under peace and order.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Management the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to review the provisions outlined in R.A. 9679 and secure a written agreement and policy regarding upgrading monthly Pag-IBIG contributions;
2. For the Human Resource Management Officer to secure a “Request to Update Membership Savings” form for those employees who want to contribute more than the mandatory contributions; and
3. For the Municipal Accounting Office to apply the latest provisions for Pag-IBIG contributions for CY 2024 onwards since there is a mandatory upgrade of monthly contributions from P100.00 to P200.00 starting February 2024.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Require the department heads/designated supervisors to prepare and/or revise the statement of duties and responsibilities. This document should outline the specific tasks to 	be completed within a month, ensuring that actual accomplishments align with the office's requirements; and
2. Require job orders to duly prepare and submit accomplishment reports that are specific in terms of actual duties performed such that proper evaluation may be made on the necessity of hiring/re-hiring.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the computation of income tax due and to make the necessary adjustments in the amount of taxes withheld, as warranted. Further, enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office to be updated with the latest BIR issuances to ensure accuracy in the computation of taxes.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Management the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to conduct a comprehensive review of the agency's payroll and implement any necessary adjustments to rectify the identified deficiency; and
2. For the Municipal Accounting Office to undertake a thorough review of the provisions outlined in R.A. 10963, particularly concerning the computation of income tax liabilities for employees. Furthermore, it is imperative for the personnel of the Accounting Office to remain consistently updated on the latest and relevant rules and regulations related to payroll and taxation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing R.A. No. 11313, and ensure compliance with the posting requirements to ensure proper dissemination of the ordinance. Further, ensure the inclusion of the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the offices concerned to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days after perfection thereof including all the supporting documents for the timely conduct of review on the reasonableness of cost, conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures, or designate a personnel responsible for submission thereof.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management follow-up with the DILG on the result of the review and assessment of the POW, detailed engineering design and other documents such that the BAC can proceed to the conduct of procurement activities for these projects. Moreover, direct the MPDO and MEO to exercise diligently their monitoring function such that issues and concerns that cause a delay in the project implementation such as lack of manpower, could be immediately identified and addressed.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries and submit the corresponding Journal Voucher/s to the COA Office. Also, refrain from recording undeposited collections under CIB-LCCA just to close the Cash–Local Treasury at the end of the year.
We also recommended to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to record all financial transactions appropriately and timely to come up with a reconciled balance before closing of books for a particular period.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following to the Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize from the books all duly transferred lands to residents and equipment to PNP.
Direct the Inventory Committee to include all counted PPE items in the RCPPE, whether acquired through purchase or donation.
Require all the department heads to furnish immediately to the Accounting Offices document/s relative to any receipt of PPE item from other government or private offices for proper and immediate recording thereof.
Immediately dispose of 6 identified unserviceable properties and regularly conduct the disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the asset and its value.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that qualify under peace and order.
Also, submit a written justification for the above-mentioned deficiencies for further evaluation by the audit team.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management enforce appropriate action against accountable officers with long outstanding cash advances, especially those who are still connected with the agency, to immediately liquidate or settle their accounts following guidelines under COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, exert more effort to demand the settlement of these cash advances and reinforce the importance of accountability for cash advance management through clear policies and procedures outlining consequences for inaction and non-compliance.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify items that qualify for write-off and expedite the request, supported with the required documents as outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Management to direct responsible officials to:
Ensure that the payroll-In charge of the withholding taxes and preparation of payroll is updated with the appropriate computation of applicable withholding taxes;
Monitor and ensure appropriate withholding of taxes from employees and strict compliance with the BIR regulations related thereto.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Development Council promptly prepare and submit the supplemental AIP and supplemental investment program approved by the local sanggunian.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management submit immediately a written justification for the audit deficiency noted to preclude the issuance of an audit disallowance. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure close monitoring of the utilization of all funds and avoid the re-occurrence of the same deficiency in the succeeding year/s.
We also recommended that the agency officials concerned restore the amount of P1,478,674.25 in the 20% Development Fund which was disbursed for loan payment of the projects under General Fund Proper.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the MDC submit a written explanation of the said deficiency and prepare the required revised/supplemental AIP if there are changes in the PPAs of the original AIP.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management facilitates the immediate deposit of the DRRM Funds to the Trust Fund bank account to ensure that the unexpended LDRRMF as at December 31, 2023 is back-up with cash and the same will be solely spent for disaster risk reduction and management activities.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the MDRRMO ensure that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are serially numbered, and must be properly and completely accomplished. Require also the driver to prepare and submit a Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and a Monthly Report of Official Travel which summarize all the Driver’s trip Tickets during the month (Appendix G and F of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles).
We also recommended that the MSWDO, in cooperation with the MDRRMO, set a clear-cut guidelines or policies in the implementation of Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) and/or Emergency Shelter Assistance (ESA) in conformity with the guidelines provided by the DSWD.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all supporting documents are complete and appropriate before making certification of the DV.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the paid DVs on a daily or weekly basis, whichever is appropriate, to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely processing thereof and submission to the COA Office.
Also, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the mentioned lacking DVs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management direct the following:
1. The HRMO to furnish a copy of the contracts and DTRs of the JOs to the personnel/office who is processing the payroll for payment of their salary; and
2.  The Municipal Accountant to: a) submit the required supporting documents, and b) make sure that all supporting documents for all disbursements made are complete to preclude the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
GFPS officials and other offices concerned to formulate more responsive PPAs to address GAD issues and concerns of the Municipality. The cost of PPAs that partly address gender issues will be included in the GPB by way of attribution through the use of the HGDG tool; and
Municipal Planning Office, GAD Focal person and other offices concerned to continue updating, completing and maintaining a functional GAD Database which shall aid in gender analysis for the identification of gender issues. and the cause/causes, to be addressed by the agency; the agency’s GAD database.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Accounting Department to conduct verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances for those who are not connected with the Municipality to determine the extent of its collectability. In case the collectability is not feasible, file an appropriate request for the approval of COA to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant account/s pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

The designated GSO shall conduct a complete physical count on all the properties of the municipality, reconcile the same with the records of the accounting office and submit the RPCPPE using the prescribed forms thereafter; and
Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive spearhead the establishment of strong internal controls in the handling of cash particularly the collections and deposits made by the Treasurer to the bank to prevent further incurrence of unrecorded collections and/or over-deposits of collections. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Accounting Office determine the cause of the negative balance so that appropriate adjustments or actions can be made.

Further, we recommend that the ordinance include provisions on a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officers concerned to apply the guidelines and procedures explicitly provided in COA Circular 2020-006 by adopting the following measures: · In coordination with the Property Division/Unit, the Inventory Committee shall plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least, the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory-taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; · Reinforce the Inventory Committee which shall be composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less; · The members of the Inventory Committee shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed; and · Property records shall be updated based on the results of the physical inventory and reconciled with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE.

We recommended the management to: 1. Review all the projects undertaken and to be undertaken by the municipality including the projects noted in our observation and come up with a concrete action to address the deficiencies noted;

We recommended that the Management shall ensure optimal utilization of the available MDRRMF for disaster risk management activities to ensure readiness in mitigating the effect of any disaster. Moreover, we reiterate that MDRRMC shall incorporate and program in the LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of previous years in the current LDRRMFIP.
