Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description of the road, including Road ID/PIN, Road Name, Components, Description, and Cost, and supply the information to other responsible departments concerned, including the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant, who in turn will facilitate the preparation and maintenance of the necessary Local Road Network (LRN) Property and LRN Ledger Cards, respectively;
Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator to assist the Municipal Engineer in the proper classification of road lots in accordance with Executive Order No. 113, s. 1955 as a preliminary activity to be performed prior to actual inventory taking of local roads within the Municipality; and
Municipal Human Resource Officer to inquire about schedules of training courses offered by DILG Regional Office No. XI on Local Road Asset Management (LRAM) Manual and enjoin responsible department heads concerned to attend.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Inventory Committee to  conduct  an investigation to determine the last known location/office where the missing PPE items were issued/installed, the person accountable, circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for the loss, among others;
If accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE was pinpointed after investigation, demand shall be made from the accountable personnel to produce the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost thereof; and
If accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE could not be pinpointed after investigation, authority for derecognition thereof from the books of accounts may be requested from the COA in accordance with Paragraph 8 of COA Circular 2020-006, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without an available record of accountability.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the  Management agreed for the:
General Service Officer  to submit justification on its failure to ensure ownership by securing TCT for lot purchases;
General Service Officer to exhaust all possible legal remedies to prioritize the processing of title of the said lot in the name of the Municipality and, where appropriate, create a Land Titling Committee to expedite the titling of lots  in order to assure its protection from possible third party claims in the future; and
Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure of information on the recorded lot acquired pending the release of transfer of title to the land in favor of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to set realistic estimates for appropriate time and schedule based on allotted funds in the implementation of viable disaster response and management-related undertakings.

Recommendations:
We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on the reported 25% Barangay Share in RPT proceeds in the periods with unremitted balances, and to submit the required reports as basis of the Municipal Accountant in processing the amount that should have been remitted/distributed as share of each component barangay in the RPT proceeds, in accordance with Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure that the barangay can avail of the immediate use of the fund for its viable programs and projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed for the MHRMO to issue an internal written policy/memorandum for proper delineation of the functions and the recruitment of job order personnel and other considerations, including the basis for their entitlement to a higher and lawful rate with due consideration on the minimum wage rate as provided under Wage Order No. RB XI-2I.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description of the road, including Road ID/PIN, Road Name, Components, Description, and Cost, and supply the information to other responsible departments concerned, including the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant who in turn will facilitate the preparation and maintenance of the necessary Local Road Network (LRN) Property and LRN Ledger Cards, respectively;
Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator to assist the Municipal Engineer in the proper classification of road lots in accordance with Executive Order No. 113, s. 1955 as a preliminary activity to be performed prior to actual inventory taking of local roads within the Municipality; and
Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory taking of local roads once the required documents and other required information are provided.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal General Services Officer to undertake the procedural guidelines to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee where the members shall be temporarily relieved from their regular duties to complete the physical inventory in three months or less, and which shall prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Officer-In-Charge of responsible implementing offices concerned to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for prompt submission of the required documents to ensure compliance with the requirements of Section 27 of IPSAS 21 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, as the basis in the proper recording of financial transactions affecting Funds Received from National Government Agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:
Municipal personnel with issued mobile phones and its usage charges against the Municipality to process all billing statements for payments when incurred during the year; and
Municipal Accountant to avoid charging the prior year’s expenditure to the current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against the appropriation for the period should be followed pursuant to the above-cited regulation.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:
1) Focal Persons assigned in respective programs of the source government agency to submit a progress report on the number of projects sourced from the remaining balance of funds received from National Government Agencies in the CYs 2018 until 2023 and their action plan to ensure full implementation of the remaining projects;
2) Municipal Engineer to harmonize their plans with Program Coordinators/Focal Persons in terms of accelerating programs and projects that are yet to be implemented without compromising the quality of work; and
3) Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible departments concerned, including the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of any unutilized balances to the source agencies once the projects are completed for settlement of liabilities owed to NGAs.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile its records on the reported 25% Barangay Share in RPT proceeds in the periods with unremitted balances; and submit the required reports as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in processing the amount that should have been remitted/distributed as share of each component barangay in the RPT proceeds in accordance with Section 271 of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure that the barangay can avail of the immediate use of the fund for its viable programs and projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the creation and personnel complement of the Internal Audit Services (IAS) Office and consider converting existing vacant positions and/or parallel positions of incumbents in the agency for the installation and strengthening of the IAS, if warranted; and
Municipal Human Resource Officer to include in the plantilla of personnel, the establishment of IAS in compliance with AO No. 70 s. of 2003, and Section 124 of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to set realistic estimates for appropriate time and schedule based on allotted funds in the implementation of viable disaster response and management-related undertakings.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to ensure that LDRRMF should be utilized only for disaster-related activities and programs to maximize the intended benefits among constituents in the event any disaster might occur.

We recommended and Management agreed for the BAC Chairman, Members, Technical Working Group, and Secretariat to:
scrutinize the sufficiency of the contractor’s key personnel and equipment to ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of contract implementation; and
strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in the evaluation of bids submitted by the bidders to ensure fair and competitive bidding; and ensure that all approved contracts for projects implemented are supported with a DOLE-approved Construction Safety and Health Program pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the IRR.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and enforce loan repayment; and to file a request for write-off for receivables where settlement/recovery of funds can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed for the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 90 under Chapter 3 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to prepare two separate cashbooks for the Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts under the Trust Fund- SALINTUBIG and Trust Fund- DA BUB.
We recommended and the Management agreed to expedite the processing of requirements in availing the ATM Payroll System in releasing payment to all personnel salaries, benefits, and incentives.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer to:
exercise prudence in expediting the process of the selection, recruitment, and promotion from the pool of applicants to deserving individuals whose competence and skills are commensurate to the minimum qualification standards, subject to the requirements of the CSC laws and regulations; and
consider an in-depth evaluation of the existing organizational structure and staffing requirements based on the current work demands in individual offices needing significant improvement, subject to the availability of funds in the next budget period to achieve optimal benefits in terms of fostering job opportunities and set of qualified personnel as a channel in attaining envisioned programs and policies designed to pave way for improved quality of life among the constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality are properly labeled with “For Official Use Only,” under which should be written the corresponding name of the Agency operating or using the same. The mark should appear on each side of the motor vehicle. When there is no sufficient space on each side, the same should appear at the back and on the front just below the windshield of the motor vehicle.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant a complete description of road components for road projects as the basis for its recognition in the books of accounts.
General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components and secure a copy of the Road Map from the MPDO.
Inventory Committee to comply with the prescribed template on the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as illustrated in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008.
Municipal Accountant to prepare the ledger card of PPE accounts, including road networks, and observe the proper accounting and reporting of road networks and the appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the total road network system in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.

Recommendations:
We recommended, and Management agreed to instruct the:
Property Officer to update the records based on the results of the physical inventory and reconcile with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE, and;
MACCO and GSO (i) coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership, and condition of the properties, and (ii) prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and management is agreeable to our audit recommendation through the Municipal Accountant who committed to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statement of MDOC account to facilitate its review.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine and reclassify receivables that belong to non-current assets.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management committed to:
carefully review the information provided in the financial statements, including its schedules, and, if necessary, make adjustments to ensure the reconciliation of the amounts and accounts presented in the report. Failure to do so could result in misrepresentation of their financial statements, which is not in conformity with Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards; and
disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide relevant, reliable, and timely information about the LGU’s financial performance, position, and cash flows to assist management in making proper decisions.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to promptly establish comprehensive guidelines and implementing rules and procedures for the storage, reporting, and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies to ensure transparency, accountability, and optimal utilization of donated resources, thereby enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of healthcare services provided to the public.

Recommendations:

Establish an appropriate Allowance for Impairment for long outstanding receivables; and
Request for write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant Receivables that remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the MDRRMO to submit the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the OCD and the DILG – LGOO in compliance with Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular 2012-002.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and management agreed to implement our audit recommendation, through the MDRRMO, to fast-track the accomplishments of the plan and utilization of the funds to effectively prevent and mitigate the onslaught on lives and properties of the people of Maco in times of natural disaster.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the filing of the request write-off, together with the required supporting documents for receivables where settlement/collectability can no longer be ascertained in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendations:
We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the officials concerned to:
strictly enforce the period of liquidation as prescribed above and require the immediate refund/return of any excess amount thereof, and;
for unliquidated cash advances of P185,332.12, where settlement/recovery of funds can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, to file a request for authority to write off.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to set realistic estimates for appropriate time and schedule based on allotted funds in the implementation of viable disaster response and management-related undertakings.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the BAC Chairman, Members, Technical Working Group, and Secretariat to scrutinize the veracity of the documents submitted, particularly a copy of the Construction Safety and Health Program (CoSH)Program duly approved by the DOLE, forming an integral part of the contract for infrastructure projects, which must be secured from the contractor prior to issuance of Notice to proceed or where feasible, upon evaluation of bids or during the post –qualification stage pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer to:
exercise prudence in expediting the process of the selection, recruitment, and promotion from the pool of applicants to deserving individuals whose competence and skills commensurate to the minimum qualification standards, subject to the requirements of the CSC laws and regulations; and
consider an in-depth evaluation of the existing organizational structure and staffing requirements based on the current work demands in individual offices needing significant improvement, subject to the availability of funds in the next budget period to achieve optimal benefits in terms of fostering job opportunities and set of qualified personnel as a channel in attaining envisioned programs and policies designed to pave the way for improved quality of life among the constituents.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal Social Welfare Development Office to ensure that a well-defined policies and guidelines should be established in writing on the grant of financial assistance containing but not limited to the following:
Types of financial assistance (medical, burial, etc.) together with the prescribed rate for a specific case, including the frequency and mode of release of similar grants to an individual,
Documentary requirements to be used in screening the eligibility of the recipient for a specific type of financial assistance, and
Duties and responsibilities of personnel to be assigned in conducting a thorough evaluation or a case study prior to the grant of financial aid.
OIC- MSWDO to furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader a copy of the promulgated policies and guidelines intended for the approval, granting and mode of release of financial assistance.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed for the MPDC to prepare a supplemental Local Development   Investment Program and Annual Investment Program to include the ten projects costing P46,248,175.19 funded from the LGSF-SBDP NTF-ELCAC, pursuant to Item 4.2.1 of the Department of Budget and Management Local Budget Circular No. 151 dated March 30, 2023.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Human Resource Officer, and the management concurred to submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies and expedite the hiring of individuals from a pool of applicants who are qualified for the position in accordance with the requirements of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile its records on the reported 25% Barangay Share in RPT proceeds in the periods with unremitted balance and to submit the required reports as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in processing the share of each component barangay in the RPT proceeds that should have been remitted/distributed on a quarterly basis in accordance with Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure that the barangay can avail of the immediate use of the fund for its viable programs and projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the  MHRMO to issue an internal written policy/memorandum for proper delineation of the functions and the recruitment of job order personnel and other considerations, including the basis for their entitlement to higher and lawful rates with due consideration on the minimum wage rate, specifically under applicable Wage Order No. RB XI-2I effective January 1, 2023, or as may be prescribed by the DOLE- RTWPB-Region XI in succeeding wage order.

Recommendations:
We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accounting Office to:
a. provide the necessary supporting documents upon submission of Bank
Reconciliation Statements in order to facilitate verification of the amounts
presented in the report; and
b. ensure that necessary adjustments are made before the closing of books such as
reverting to the cash in bank account the amount corresponding to the unreleased
checks.

Recommendations:
We recommended, and Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to conduct
regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables
as prescribed under Section 7.1 (a) of Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, and to
recognize impairment loss on Other Receivables account.
Furthermore, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a request for authority
to write off the dormant Other Receivables account to COA duly supported by the
documents prescribed under the same circular.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed for the Inventory Committee to enhance its
strategy for the timely completion of the physical count of inventories, and submit the RPCI
to the Office of the Audit Team Leader within the reglementary period, and reconcile with
the reported account balances in the LGU’s books of accounts. We also recommended the
Municipal GSO to regularly submit the SSMI together with the RIS to the Municipal
Accountant to record in the books all issuances of inventories during the year.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed for the:
a. General Services Officer to undertake the physical inventory and disposal of
unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 162 of COA Circular No. 92-
386; and
b. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the
derecognition of the unserviceable properties to ensure the fair presentation of the
PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services
Office and thoroughly review the composition of the Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE)
accounts, and draw a journal entry to reclassify the properties identified as semi-
expendable and falling below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 in accordance with
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004. The proforma adjusting entry is as follows:
Dr. Prior Period Adjustment xxx
Accumulated Depreciation xxx
Cr. Property, Plant and Equipment xxx

Recommendations:
We reiterated our prior year's recommendation, and the Management agreed for the
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the concerned implementing offices, to:
a. facilitate the return to the SAs or the National Treasury of the unused funds and the
reversion to the General Fund the unused local counterparts for PPAs which are
already completed or when the intended purpose stipulated in the Memorandum of
Agreement has been fulfilled and draw the proper adjusting journal entry for the
same; and
b. conduct immediate reconciliation with the SAs on the unutilized balance of fund
transfers, which remained dormant for several years in the depository bank, to
determine the accurate amount to be returned.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to record
Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as at year-end in accordance with
Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001 and to present fairly the true financial
obligations of the municipality.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year's recommendation and the Management agreed for the
Municipal Accountant to immediately take necessary measures to reconcile the
discrepancy between these reciprocal accounts and make the appropriate adjustments to
fully eliminate the variance.

Recommendations:
We recommended, and Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to:
a. carefully review the information provided in the financial statements, including its
schedules, and, if necessary, make adjustments to ensure the reconciliation of the
amounts and accounts presented in the report. Failure to do so could result in
misrepresentation of their financial statements, which is not in conformity with
Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards; and
b. disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide
relevant, reliable, and timely information about the LGU’s financial performance,
position, and cash flows to assist management in making proper decisions.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts held under the
name of the LGU;
b. provide the necessary supporting documents upon submission of Bank
Reconciliation Statements in order to facilitate verification of the reliability and
accuracy of the amounts presented in the report;
c. draw journal entries to record the debit/credit memos; and
d. for subsequent years, ensure that necessary adjustments are made before closing of
books to revert to the cash in bank account the amount corresponding to the
unreleased checks to fairly present the account in the financial statement as at
yearend.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our prior recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor file the request for authority to write off the dormant Other Receivables account to
COA following the procedures as prescribed under COA Circular 2023-008 dated August
17, 2023, duly supported by the necessary documents.

Furthermore, the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the
impairment loss of the remaining dormant other receivable accounts to ensure its fair
presentation in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year recommendation and the Management agreed that the
Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office to implement the Moving
Average Method in costing the Supplies and Material Inventories and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock recorded under the account as provided under
COA rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to record Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as
at year-end in accordance with Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to:
a. carefully review the information provided in the financial statements including its
schedules and, if necessary, make adjustments to ensure the reconciliation of the
amounts and accounts presented in the report. Failure to do so could result in
misrepresentation of their financial statements, which is not in conformity with
Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards; and
b. disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide
relevant, reliable, and timely information about the LGU’s financial performance,
position, and cash flows to assist management make proper decisions.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our prior recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a) withhold the salary or any money due to the Municipal officers and employees for
the settlement of due and demandable receivables recorded under the Due from
Officers and Employees account; and
b) exhaust all efforts for the settlement of outstanding cash advances of AOs who were
no longer connected with the LGU. Thereafter, consider the filing of a request for
authority to write off accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2023-008 dated
August 17, 2023, if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to facilitate the posting on the LGU’s website and in three (3) conspicuous
places the quarterly reports on the fund utilization and status of implementation of the
projects funded under the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC in accordance with the DBM
Local Budget Circular No. 151 for the purpose of enhancing transparency and promoting
public accountability.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Human Resource
Management Officer to develop a plan to strategize and consider potential wage
adjustments for job order personnel, aligning them with the compensation levels of
equivalent positions in the government.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management, through the Human Resource and Management
Officer, instruct the newly hired and promoted employees for immediate compliance with
the essential documentary requirements to facilitate the timely processing and updating of
their GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC records. Consequently, the Agency Remittance Advice
(ARA) will be promptly updated, ensuring the timely and accurate monthly remittance of
premiums, loan repayments, and contributions to GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, through the
GAD Focal Person, establish a feasible timeline for the submission of GAD ARs by the
implementing offices concerned to facilitate the timely consolidation and prompt
submission to oversight agencies in accordance with the requirements of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001. Accordingly, posting
requirements should be observed in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2019-149.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office to:
a. prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts held under the
name of the LGU;
b. provide the necessary supporting documents upon submission of Bank
Reconciliation Statements in order to facilitate verification of the reliability and
accuracy of the amounts presented in the report; and
c. for subsequent years, ensure that necessary adjustments are made before closing of
books to revert to the cash in bank account the amount corresponding to the
unreleased checks to fairly present the account in the financial statement as at
yearend.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting and General Services Offices:
a. Closely coordinate and complete the ongoing reconciliation to come up with
reconciled balances of PPE accounts; and
b. Prepare the RPCPPE with supporting schedules to facilitate the immediate
adjustments of the discrepancies noted in the PPE accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the General Services Officer to undertake the disposal of unserviceable
properties in accordance with Section 162 of COA Circular No. 92-386; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the
derecognition of the unserviceable properties to ensure fair presentation of the PPE
accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record
Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as at year-end in accordance with
Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our prior recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the concerned implementing offices to:
a. facilitate the return to the SAs or the National Treasury of the unused funds and the
reversion to the General Fund of the unused local counterparts for PPAs that are
already completed or when the intended purpose stipulated in the Memorandum of
Agreement has been fulfilled and draw the proper adjusting journal entry for the
same; and
b. conduct immediate reconciliation with the SAs on the unutilized balance of fund
transfers, which remained dormant for several years in the depository bank, to
determine the accurate amount to be returned.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require the General Services Officer to undertake the disposal of unserviceable
properties in accordance with Section 162 of COA Circular No. 92-386; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the
derecognition of the unserviceable properties to ensure fair presentation of the PPE
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to file the request for authority to write off the
dormant unliquidated cash advances account to COA duly supported by the documents as
prescribed under COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the impairment loss of
dormant unliquidated cash advances to ensure fair presentation of receivable accounts in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record
Due to BIR account upon setting up Accounts Payable as at year-end in accordance with
Section 4 of Revenue Regulations No. 12-001 and to present fairly the true financial
obligations of the municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. carefully review the information provided in the financial statements including its
schedules, and, if necessary, make adjustments to ensure the reconciliation of the
amounts and accounts presented in the report. Failure to do so could result in
misrepresentation of their financial statements, which is not in conformity with
Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards; and
b. disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide
relevant, reliable, and timely information about the LGU’s financial performance,
position, and cash flows to assist management in making proper decisions.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the:
Municipal General Services Officer to undertake the procedural guidelines for one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Inventory Team to prepare and submit a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) once approved to the Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities.

We recommended the Management for the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the needed supporting documentary requirements to expedite the preparation and submission of their request for authority to write-off for receivable where settlement/recovery of funds can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to:
Include only viable undertakings to be sourced from LDRRM Fund-Continuing Appropriations amounting to P875,953.85 that are deemed most appropriate under the circumstances for prompt implementation after thorough evaluation, subject to approval of the Municipal DRRM Council; and
Enhance monitoring efforts and strategies to ensure the optimal utilization and implementation of PPAs under the LDRRM fund, particularly the unimplemented continuing appropriations.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer, and the management agreed to submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies and expedite the hiring of individuals from a pool of applicants who are qualified for the position in accordance with the requirements of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile its records on the reported 25% Barangay Share in RPT proceeds in the periods with unremitted balances to submit the required reports as basis of the Municipal Accountant in processing the amount that should have been remitted/distributed as share of each component barangay in the RPT proceeds in accordance with Section 271 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that the barangay can avail of the immediate use of the fund for its viable programs and projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the BAC Chairman, Members, Technical Working Group, and Secretariat to scrutinize the veracity of the documents submitted, particularly a copy of the Construction Safety and Health Program (CoSH)Program duly approved by the DOLE, forming an integral part of the contract for infrastructure projects, pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 prior to issuance of Notice to proceed or where feasible, upon evaluation of bids or during the post-qualification stage.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Human Resource Officer to:
exercise prudence in expediting the process of the selection, recruitment, and promotion from the pool of applicants to deserving individuals whose competence and skills commensurate to the minimum qualification standards, subject to the requirements of the CSC laws and regulations; and
consider an in-depth evaluation of the existing organizational structure and staffing requirements based on the current work demands in individual offices needing significant improvement, subject to availability of funds in the next budget period to achieve optimal benefits in terms of fostering job opportunities and set of qualified personnel as channel in attaining envisioned programs and policies designed to pave the way for improved quality of life among the constituents.

We recommended, and the Management agreed for the  MHRMO to issue an internal written policy/memorandum for proper delineation of the functions and the recruitment of job order personnel and other considerations, including the basis for their entitlement to a higher and lawful rate with due consideration on the minimum wage rate as provided under Wage Order No. RB XI-2I.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Instruct the OIC-Accountant to make full disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements the composition of the Property, Plant, and Equipment, highlighting the inclusion of unserviceable properties and items destroyed by fire; and
Inventory and Disposal Committee and the General Services Officer to immediately cause the disposal of unserviceable properties to prevent their further deterioration and for management to potentially obtain a higher return through sale or negotiation.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine and reclassify receivables that belong to non-current assets.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed to instruct the MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan the priority programs/projects/activities to be charged against the Prior Years’ LDRRM Fund unexpended balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers of unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MDRRMO to ensure the submission of the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the OCD and the DILG – LGOO in compliance with Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular 2012-002.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
direct the MDRRM Council and MDRRM Office to monitor and evaluate the implementation of all LDRRMF PPAs, to include in the evaluation process the reasons/causes why the projects were not realized as planned, and to come up with the suited strategies to address the problems encountered to avoid its recurrence in the future;
require the MDRRM Council and MDRRM Office to re-evaluate the PPAs under continuing appropriations and subsequently initiate the reversion or realignment of the appropriated funds for PPAs no longer needed or necessary so that the same may be used effectively and efficiently for more appropriate priority projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the MEO and MPDC to ensure that the 20% DF is well-planned and duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan as prescribed in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a)  exert extra effort on the collection of Due from Officers & Employees and Other Receivables, and;
b) request for write-off for receivables where settlement/collectability can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct:
the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prepare and submit an Updated CY 2023 LDIP-AIP pursuant to Local Budget Circular No. 151 and;
secure at all times SB Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract with concerned contractors on behalf of the Municipality before contract perfection for those projects under the lump-sum appropriation and fund transfers from various government agencies; and

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Expedite the transfer of the property, plant and equipment to the intended recipients/ beneficiaries since control of the asset is not with the Municipality by executing Deed of Donation or any other means for the transfer of PPE;
Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the records by derecognizing the PPEs to which the Municipality has no control in the financial records; and,
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and Municipal GSO to trace in their records the composition of the line item that was recorded with generic description in order to, subsequently, record such appropriately in the financial records of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer the details of the amount recorded in the accounting records and to subsequently derecognize the same in the financial statements; and,
Formulate communication procedures in the reporting of delinquent Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax between the MTO and MAccO in order for the latter to disclose the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Regularly update the personnel of the BAC about the deadlines set by the GPPB and submit the documents needed within the deadline set; and,
Facilitate trainings of personnel by accredited providers pertaining to the updates on procurement laws and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the GAD Focal Point Personnel to observe the usage of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tools in GAD planning and to determine the HGDG scores in each activity as basis in the attribution of expenses for GAD-related activities; and
Provide corroborative details on reported amounts in the GAD Accomplishment Report to enhance the reliability of reported accomplishments.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the GAD Focal Point Personnel to devise a systematic and holistic approach in the identification of GAD-related PPAs to be undertaken in the period through following the guidelines set forth in the PCW– DILG- NEDA- DBM Joint Circular 2013-01 or through other means that may be deemed appropriate; and,
Instruct the offices in-charge of the implementation of GAD-related PPAs to devise a periodic monitoring and review mechanism that would facilitate in the determination of the status of implementation of each PPA and to provide a feedback mechanism by which constraints found in the implementation could be addressed expeditiously.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Establish a database that will provide interlocking information between the Municipal Assessor’s Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to provide easy access, management and updating of information pertaining to the basic taxes due; and
Evaluate the existing procedures in the assessment and billing of basic taxes and penalties and improve breakdowns in recording through adoption of work-automation processes or any other means that Management may find appropriate.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the other offices in the Municipality, to conduct initial re-assessment as to the viability of the planned projects prior years projects under the 20% Development Fund and provide recommendation to the Municipal Development Office if whether it is feasible to continue the project;
Subsequently, coordinate with the Municipal Development Council to determine the appropriate action to be undertaken in relation to the recommendation of the MPDO as to the viability of the prior years; projects, particularly as to whether it is feasible to continue the projects or identify new programs, projects and activities to be implemented; and
Consider the opening of additional plantilla for the Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the implementation of development projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to expedite the institution of administrative and/ or judicial action against delinquent taxpayers to avoid prescription of the right of the Municipality to collect such taxes; and,
Evaluate the procedures in the collection of delinquent taxes and introduce improvements in the process to improve collection efficiency on delinquent taxes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the City Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the donated assets and its corresponding depreciation expense so that balances of the affected PPE accounts be accurately presented in the financial statements as at December 31, 2022.

Instruct the CGSO to coordinate with all offices and conduct re-orientation on the proper procedure and action to be done upon receipt of any properties/inventories that were delivered directly to them by the suppliers/donors.



Communicate and/or make representation with DENR-EMB for further necessary actions in order to immediately settle the issues and concerns in the booking up of the donated properties in the books of the donor and the City Government.

The properties donate by DENR-EMB were already recorded / included in the City Government’s books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accounting Office provide an adequate disclosure in the Notes to FS the significant and material information as to the actual status of the RPT Receivable from Ekran Service Inc in order to present fairly the said account in the city’s financial statements as at December 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: Appropriately disclosed as recommended

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

3.Instruct the offices concerned to hasten the preparation of the request for re-alignment with the City Development Council (CDC) in order that funds for non-doable projects and those balances of completed projects could be used to finance the other lined-up priority projects of the city.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

1. Direct the Internal Audit Service Division (Management Audit Section) to review the established process flow on the receipts/issuances and recording of assistance in kind received from national government, from other LGUs and agencies, in order to strengthen the system, thereby avoid subsequent breakdowns and ensure complete recording of transactions in the city’s books of accounts.  

2. Require the CHO to continue using the Distribution and Utilization Reports (DURs) to document the receipts, issuance/consumption and balances of the vaccines and other medical supplies received, in which should be duly reviewed and signed by the preparer’s immediate supervisor/vaccination program coordinator and also to avoid making alterations thereon to ensure reliability on correctness of data as indicated. - Ethel Lawas 



3. Direct the CGSO to conduct an inspection and inventory of the CHO’s drugs and medicines including COVID-19 vaccines received from all sources in order to fully establish accountabilities of these donated vaccines and ancillary supplies. - Sarmena Pasaol
observations.managementAction:
The CADO-IAS has started the facilitation of the review of the inventory process of the City government.



Per recommendation, the CHO is now using the Distribution and Utilization Reports (DURs) to document the receipts, issuances/ consumption and balances of the vaccines and other medical supplies received from DOH and Provincial Health Office in which is duly reviewed and signed by the CHO, properly coordinated with CASSO and CACCO with no alternations thereon to ensure reliability and correctness of data.

On-going preparations of inventory reports based on property transfer report given by DOH.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

1. Require the concerned offices (CHO and CAccO) to submit a written explanation/justification why several deficiencies were noted on the recording and documentations of the city’s implementation of COVID-19 Vaccination program, for our further evaluation and next appropriate action.

2. Instruct the CAccO and the CHO to closely coordinate with each other and to conduct a thorough reconciliation of their records as to receipts and disposal of the vaccines, to ensure accuracy of the balances of the affected accounts in the city’s financial statements as of year-end of 2022.
observations.managementAction:
An explanation was submitted to COA.



The office together with the City Accounting Office is now closely coordinating with each other and conduct regular reconciliation of our records as to receipts and disposal of the vaccine, to ensure accuracy of the balances of the affected accounts in the city’s financial statements per recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Budget Office and the Local Finance Committee to include in the City’s annual budget the repayment of the non-interest-bearing loan with the NHA.  

We also recommended to the City Planning and Development Office to enforce strict collection policies to the individual housing beneficiaries in order to hasten the recovery of the amount paid out of the City’s funds, thus eliminate delinquency in the repayments of their NHA Loan.
observations.managementAction: The Management has allotted P1M in the supplemental last Feb. 16 and was paid as per check #122387 dated March 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

1. Inform the source agency of the unutilized funds totaling P860,257.17 and prepare a letter request duly approved by them to utilize the unspent funds to augment other priority projects of the City. If request is not granted, expedite the return of these amounts to the concerned source national agencies.

2. Direct the CEO and other concerned implementing offices to ensure that adequate planning and coordination with the project’s stakeholders are being made prior to its identification and implementation, to avoid possible delay or project cancellation causing possible return of funds to source agencies.
observations.managementAction: All the projects enumerated in the AOM were completely implemented and the related unutilized funds were duly returned to the fund source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

1. Instruct the City Assessor to revisit the existing Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMVs) and conduct a general revision of property assessment every three (3) years, as required under Section 219 of the Local Government Code to ensure that taxes imposed are based on updated market values.



2. Direct the concerned officials to regularly review the List of Real Property Tax Delinquency and apply all available remedies provided in the LGC to intensify real property tax collection.

3. Ensure to provide additional manpower to assist in the updating of RPUs records particularly the latest addresses of the landowners with delinquent accounts in order to easily locate and serve to them their individual demand letters.
observations.managementAction:
The 2023 General Revision of Real Property Assessment has been conducted and approved by the Sangguniang Panlungsod under City Ordinance No. 422, s. 2023. Such ordinance shall be forwarded to Sangguniang Panlalawigan for approval and publication will commence afterwards.



The CTO conducts the following activities:
• Prepares list of delinquent taxpayers to be published in the local newspaper for two (2) consecutive weeks
• Sends Notice of Delinquencies / demand letters as well as bills through mails.
• Conducts information dissemination about RPTs during barangay assemblies
• Posts list of delinquent taxpayers near the entrance of the City Hall
• Use of e-banking through G-Cash and LBP iAccess.

The CTO has:
• hired personnel to work on these deliverables

Coordinated between CTO and CASSO was done on the full implementation of ETRACS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CHRMO and the department heads concerned submit a satisfactory justification for the non-appearance of the personnel assigned at their respective offices, for our further validation and next appropriate audit action.
observations.managementAction: Disseminated communication letters dated March 2, 2023 to the concerned Job Order workers to submit satisfactory justification to COA. As per record, all workers complied the required justification to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the CHRMO and other concerned offices to formulate policies and procedures to strengthen its internal control over the administration and monitoring of JO’s attendance specifically those assigned in the field so that accurate and reliable personnel records are maintained.
observations.managementAction: Issued Memorandum Order 365 providing for the policy of the issuance of reassignment order for Job Order workers that are assigned to other offices, which has different designation / function from its mother office job designation/ title or fund charged.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the CHRMO and other concerned offices to include/indicate in the JO contracts/hiring plan the specific duties and responsibilities of the JO workers in order to easily evaluate and established that the hiring of JOs is necessary and justified.
observations.managementAction:
Conducted and orientation last April 12, 2023 on the enhanced plan template.

Starting July 2023, all offices will be using the enhanced template for hiring plan that indicates the specific duties and responsibilities of the job order workers to be hired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the CDRRMC to strictly adhere to the guidelines on the utilization of the CDRRMF as provided in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular 2012-002 and henceforth refrain from charging to the CDRRMF all non-disaster risk related expenditures to preclude issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: All procurements charged against CDRRM Funds are approved by the CDRRM Council.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

1. Direct the City Engineering Office to hasten the submission to the City Accounting Office of the progress/final billings with complete documentation, for all their completed projects, by also instructing the concerned contractors to expedite any project works to be rectified so that recoupment of their advances will be recovered the soonest possible time;

2. Instruct the City Accounting Office to issue a demand for the refund of the unrecouped advances or forfeit the corresponding irrevocable standby letters of credit or performance/surety bonds posted by the defaulting contractors, hence a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

3. Henceforth, strictly comply with paragraph 4.3 of Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and impose administrative sanctions against erring personnel who failed to prioritize the deduction/withholding of advances to contractor’s repayment, as warranted.
observations.managementAction:
The CEO and the CAccO immediately communicated with the concerned contractors regarding unrecouped advance.

In fact, deduction of recoupment in the final billing was done for the following projects:

1. Development of Kaputian Beach Park (Waste Water Facility)
2. Concreting of Road going to Bandera Nature’s Park

However, the unrecouped balance for the Construction of Public Market at Sitio Maag will be deducted from the amount that can be claimed by the Hexamindz Corporation in the project re: Construction of New City Building at Sitio Maag.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the CAccO to strictly adhere with COA Circular No. 2021-008 by creating and integrating in their present accounting system a separate RC for GFPS and assign RC codes in order to properly segregate and monitor all GAD related expenses/transactions and be able to expedite the generation of GAD related financial reports at any given period/time.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Focal Point System (CGFPS) convened to attend the CGFPS Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) Training and GAD Planning 2024 on March 22 – 24, 2023 where the concern on establishing the Responsibility Center for the GAD CGFPS was discussed.

On April 12, 2023, the GAD Focal Point System (CGFPS) approved the standard process flow to harmonize the planning, implementation and evaluation phases as presented by the Technical Working Group (TWG). Further, the GAD Secretariat and TWG shall continue to work on the establishment of Responsibility Center to be utilized in 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:

1. Direct the City Accountant to require the concerned suppliers and service providers to submit their TIN prior to payment of their claims with the City Government.



2. Require the CAccO remittance in-charge to always reconcile on a monthly basis the balances shown in the subsidiary ledger with that of the Schedules of Remittances, which are due for payment to the BIR, GSIS and PAG-IBIG. Moreover, to ensure that those rejected names in the GSIS system during the remittance of a specific month be included in the next billing period, to prevent under and/or non-remittance thereof.

3. Instruct the CHRMO to expedite the submission of all documentary requirements to CAccO in order to support the preparation of monthly remittances, and to direct these two offices to work in close coordination so that no amounts withheld remain unremitted during the billing period.
observations.managementAction:
Several follow-ups had been made by the CAccO to suppliers and service providers.
Conduct of monthly reconciliation of all mandatory deductions is already being observed as well as coordination with CHRMO for the necessary documents needed.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the CAccO exert effort to serve demand letters to the persons liable and enforce collection of the same so that amounts recovered could be used as additional funds for the city’s other operating expenses.

observations.recommendation:
•	Conduct regular monitoring of the solid waste diversion operations; determine annually the approximate volume of wastes diverted from waste disposal facilities; and




•	Direct every Barangay Solid Waste Management Committee to support CENRO in its monitoring of solid waste diversion operations through its submission of SWM monthly reports.
observations.managementAction:
Conducted continued monitoring of waste volumes being disposed at the landfill to determine quarterly waste diversion.





Conducted survey of waste diversion from Junkshops and recyclers who are applying for solid waste management certificates. A total of 62 junkshops already surveyed. From the survey, an estimated 429 tons per month of recyclables is diverted through junkshops.  


An estimated 34% diversion was achieved from January to October 2023 based on Project waste generation versus Disposal data from Sanitary Landfill. 



Reminded all barangays to submit this office their Solid waste diversion reports. A total of 100 barangays have submitted quarterly Diversion reports as of June 2023.


CENRO continues to monitor the submission of SWM monthly reports of barangays.
observations.reason: This recommendation was partially implemented due to non-compliance of some barangays. This will be included in the audit of Solid Waste (Citizen’s Participatory Audit).

observations.recommendation:
1. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a list of cash in bank accounts that remained dormant for so many years with the request to close and transfer these to the General Fund; and
2. Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances from the Trust Fund to the General Fund bank accounts; and
3. Municipal Budget Officer to consider the balances as source in the preparation of the supplemental or annual budget in the current or ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: 1. Prepare endorsement for the closing and transfer of the dormant accounts to the Sangguniang Bayan; 2. Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances from the Trust Fund to the General Fund bank accounts; 3. Municipal Budget Officer to consider the balances as source in the preparation of the supplemental or annual budget in the current or ensuing year.
observations.reason: Endorsed to Sangguniang Bayan for closing and transfer of the dormant ten (10) years; Closed the dormant account under TF and transferred to General Fund for appropriation; Recorded in the books of the General Fund and available for Supplemental;

observations.recommendation:
1.Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to submit explanation on the incurrence of delays in completing the projects and subsequently determine the appropriate action to be taken whether to issue a Warning Letter or to directly employ punitive actions prescribed by existing guidelines; and
2.Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the balances of those fully implemented projects to the respective National Government Agencies where the funds came from.
observations.managementAction: 1. Direct the MEO to require the contractors concerned to submit explanation on the incurrence of delays in completing the projects and subsequently determine the appropriate action to be taken whether to issue a Warning Letter or to directly employ punitive actions prescribed by existing guidelines; 2. Direct the MTO/MACCO Municipal Accountant to submit to COA downloaded funds in the LGU, and give a copy of the Certificate of Liquidations and Disbursement and return all excess fund to respective agencies;
observations.reason: Issued a warning letter to contractors and require them to submit catch-up plan and set timeline for project completion.; Prepare liquidations in all downloaded funds in the LGU, and give a copy of the Certificate of Liquidations and Disbursement and return all excess fund to respective agencies;

Require the Project/Program proponent to submit earlier all needed and required MPDO documents to include Perth and Gantt Chart; Improve project implementation feedback mechanism; Maintain, Improved projects/programs communication to address directly issues and concerns of project implementation; Issue an executive order for the Project Monitoring Team by the Chief Local Executive to closely monitor the implementation of the projects;

observations.recommendation:
1. Conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects/activities can be identified accordingly using the LDRRM funds- current and prior years and implemented the same thus, optimum utilization of the available funds be achieved; and
2. Re-program/re-appropriate in the ensuing year the unexpended balance of MOOE from 70% mitigation fund of the current year and the planned PPAs be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and revised AIP.

1. Direct the LDRRMC to conduct adequate planning of all program/activities to effectively implement all PPAs using the LDRRM fund intended for beneficiaries/groups/constituents to mitigate, prepare to, respond to and recover from, the effects of disasters.; 
2. Reconcile the undisbursed mounts of the available from the previous years of 70% of LDRRMF and program it for projects/activities of LDRRM.
observations.reason: Monitor the utilization of 70% and the excess of 70% to be accounted separately;

Subsequently, the Inventory Committee is preparing the RPCPPE and submit to COA.

observations.recommendation: 8. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all PPE to establish existence thereof and to come up with a Physical Inventory Plan and establish policy guidelines in the conduct of the count to be able to complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period.

The Report shall likewise be submitted to the Accounting Office for subsequent reconciliation with the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: 16. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the monthly schedule of shares prepared by the MTO and prepare quarterly the Disbursement Voucher for remittance of shares to the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Per validation by the Audit Team, the Accountant prepares the DVs every quarter. Per validation by the Audit Team, the Municipal Treasurer prepares the disbursement voucher and remits quarterly the shares within five (5) days from the end of each quarter.

observations.recommendation: 18. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 for the timely submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements, i.e. within the reglementary period of ten (10) days after the end of each month; 19. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to hasten the enrollment in the Banks’ online facility and exert effort to secure in advance copies of the bank statements through bank snapshots and electronic mail every end of the month which can already provide sufficient data needed for bank reconciliation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The registration in the AGDB’s We-access facility helped the MACCO in the preparation and submission of the BRS on a monthly basis. Already registered in the bank’s We-access facility.

observations.recommendation: 20. observations.managementAction: Already registered in the bank’s We-access facility. Submission of accounts Trial Balances and Journals with schedules are submitted at the same time.

The reuse of ECPAC and/or a more suitable system and RPTAS database installation are also considered. The appointment of a duly-licensed MASSO Head is being considered who will meet the standard qualification of the CSC.

observations.recommendation: 26. observations.managementAction: Closed. Updating of the GSO’s database was finished. Last January 18, 2024, the OIC-MGSO together with the personnel from MEO conducted an inventory of road lots, pavements, drainage, canal and others. However, the cost of some road lots, drainage and pavements were not recognized since the Engineering Office cannot provide the POW of such projects because the Municipality was not the implementing agency.

We recommended to the BAC the following:

Disqualify the said bidder and initiate the post-qualification process on the bidder with the second LCB.

Require the posting of performance security upon contract signing.

Submit the following documents for the above-mentioned goods and infrastructure projects procured through public bidding:
Complete bidding documents 
Eligibility documents and eligibility requirements 
BAC Security (if any)
Minutes of meeting, BAC attendance sheet and invitation to three (3) observers for every procurement activity 
Certification from the BAC Secretariat on the posting of the ITB
BAC Resolution on the preliminary examination of bids
Minutes of BAC meeting on the detailed bid evaluation
TWG Report on the Detailed Bid Evaluation for determination of the LCB, as reviewed by the BAC
Post-qualification Evaluation Report by the TWG
Notice of Post-qualification
Performance Security

observations.recommendation:

Likewise, in the budget formulation, ensure that every project will be sufficiently funded to avoid prolonging project completion due to budget insufficiency. While it is already a given fact that a municipality has no capacity to implement multiple highly budgeted projects, it is recommended that the agency will prepare a project prioritization analysis vis-a-vis its budget capacity.

We recommended for Local Chief Executive (LCE) to:

1.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to expedite the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending demand letters to delinquent taxpayers giving priority to delinquent taxpayers having large balances, and;

2.	Create a committee that is responsible for the conduct of an information dissemination drive regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment thereof. 

3.	Avail of the remedies provided for by law to enforce the strict and prompt collection of delinquent real property taxes.

1.	Direct the HRMO to create an employee manual that will define the job description, rights, and responsibilities, among others, of all the employees within the Municipality; 

2.	Issue a uniform and clear-cut policy on the hiring of Job Order workers to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto; and

3.	Evaluate and determine the appropriate personnel complement for each office

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Require the Head of the Department concerned, which is the Municipal Health Officer in this case or the authorized representative occupying a regular plantilla position, if any, to render a report stating the volume of donated items utilized or the number of issuances to end-user units as the basis for the Accounting Office to record the transactions relative to the utilization/distribution of the donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies that shall be treated as expenses for a given reporting date.

2. Unless the personnel-in-charge is able to provide a report on the receipt and utilization of donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies from DOH, he/she will be held liable for whatever pilferages or wastages incurred from non-compliance with the applicable laws, rules, and regulations for property and supply management and that the related transactions shall likewise be suspended in audit.

3. Establish accounting requirements/guidelines or implementing rules and regulations that are deemed applicable in the storage, reporting, and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies from DOH and/or other government agencies.
observations.managementAction:
In a letter dated Jan. 10, 2024, the Municipal Health Office certified that the Drugs, Medicines, and Medical Supplies donated by the Department of Health (DOH) amounting to P1,352,489.81 have been fully utilized and disposed of.

Thus, the Municipal Health Office promised that if ever there are drugs, medicines, and medical supplies to be donated by the Department of Health and other agencies will not be accepted unless it is inspected by the Municipal General Services Office and Office of the Municipal Accountant.

Management has not yet established the relevant guidelines for the storage, reporting, and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies from DOH and/or other government agencies.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | Reiterated through AOM No. 24-003 dated January 15, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive collection strategies to improve collection by consistent preparation and issuance of Notices of Delinquency for all delinquent RPUs; b) fast track the ETRACS implementation to support more efficient assessment, collection and reporting system for RPT/SET; c) conduct dialogue with the owners of the RPUs under CARP to address concerns on the collection of taxes. If warranted, request the presence of the Provincial Treasurer and Provincial Legal Officer to assist in such undertaking; and d) for the delinquent real property taxes with NDs already served, recommend to the Provincial Treasurer the imposition of the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or judicial action, following the procedures authorized in the LGC, to enforce collection and to undertake the same expeditiously on a yearly basis to reduce amounts accumulated as receivables for delinquent taxes.
