observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: 1. the MEO to submit to the MACCO the Project Completion Report (PCR) and its supporting documents relative to the above-mentioned completed projects; 2. the MACCO to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset accounts upon receipt of the necessary documents, and provide the appropriate depreciation expenses; and 3. the offices concerned to revisit and evaluate the current system on the preparation and submission of the PCR and its supporting documents to MACCO.
observations.managementAction:
The MEO had already submitted to the Accounting Office the required documents. A copy of the duly received transmittal letter was furnished to the audit team.
The MACCO had already prepared the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account.
The offices concerned had revisited and evaluated the current system of preparation and submission of PCR and its supporting documents to MACCO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the Municipal Accountant to record all construction materials and supplies in the appropriate Construction Materials and Supplies Inventory account based on the AIR and Delivery Receipts. Credit said inventory account and debit the appropriate expense or CIP accounts upon receipt of the RIS and SSMI from the Municipal GSO; and b) instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to regularly submit the SSMI and RIS to the Municipal Accountant for the immediate recording of issuances of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction:
Necessary documents were submitted to the MACCO for the recording of construction materials and supplies in its appropriate account.
The Municipal GSO had regularly submitted the SSMI and RIS to the Municipal Accountant for the immediate recording of issuances of supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the information presented in the different financial statements and make adjustments, when necessary, to reconcile the amounts and accounts presented in the financial statements, failing which would render their financial statements as misrepresented which is not in conformance with Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards.
We further recommended disclosing the relevant information as stated in letters “a” to “b” above, to help the end-user understand the Municipality’s financial position and performance.
observations.managementAction:
The management submitted a revised CY 2022 FS with adjustments and/or re-computations. However, discrepancies were still noted upon evaluation, especially on the “Non-cash Expenses” account with no revisions made, thus affecting the fair presentation of financial statements as at December 31, 2022.
We further recommended disclosing the relevant information as stated in letters “a” to “b” above, to help the end-user understand the Municipality’s financial position and performance.
observations.reason:
A similar audit observation was noted and reiterated in Finding No. 5, Part II of this report.
The recommended information was still not disclosed in the Year-end Financial Statements for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) refrain from granting cash advances to the Disbursing Officer beyond the allowed maximum cash accountability, otherwise, increase the amount of the fidelity bond coverage of the AO in accordance with the fidelity bond requirements as per Treasury Circular No. 02-2019, and b) utilize the ATM Payroll System in releasing all personnel salaries, benefits, and incentives.
observations.managementAction:
The management had refrained from granting cash advances beyond the allowed maximum cash accountability of the AO.
ATM Payroll system was implemented by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to enhance its monitoring in the implementation of infrastructure projects and ensure the accuracy of the information posted on the project signboards in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had directed the Municipal Engineering Office to enhance monitoring and ensure the accuracy of the information in the project signboards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to: a) submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly approved/granted Time Extension, otherwise, liquidated damages shall be imposed to be deducted from any money due or which may have become due the contractor; b) submit the approved Variation Order, if there’s any, or render a satisfactory explanation on the discrepancy in the power and lighting layout; and c) furnish the COA Technical Audit Specialist through the Audit Team the copy of the required Mezzanine Drawing Details of the project Expansion of Two-Storey Library and Study Center, as required in the Technical Inspection Report for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction:
The management had already submitted the required documents.
The management had already submitted the required documents.
The management had already submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the concerned implementing offices to: a) facilitate the return to the SAs or the National Treasury of the unused funds for PPAs that are already complete or when the intended purpose stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement has been fulfilled; b) conduct immediate reconciliation with the SAs on the unutilized balance of fund transfers which remained dormant for several years in the depository bank in order to determine the accurate amount to be returned; and c) fast-track the implementation of unimplemented PPAs in order to maximize the utilization of the funds in accordance with its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction:
Unused funds were returned to the source agencies or to the National Treasury.
Reconciliation with concerned offices was conducted.
Implementation of PPAs was expedited to maximize utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve the collection by consistent preparation and issuance of Notices of Delinquency for all delinquent RPUs; b) conduct dialogue with the owners of the RPUs under CARP to address concerns on the collection of taxes. If warranted, request the presence of the Provincial Treasurer and Provincial Legal Officer to assist in such undertaking; c) direct the Municipal Assessor to submit the list of RPUs with boundary conflict; and d) for the delinquent real property taxes with NDs already served, impose the legal remedies either by administrative auction through a levy on real property or judicial action, following the procedures authorized in the LGC, to enforce collection and to undertake the same process to enforce judicial action, which is under the authority of the Provincial Treasurer’s Office.
observations.managementAction:
The MTO had sent Notices of Delinquencies and had conducted more aggressive tax collection strategies.
The Municipal Assessor had already submitted the list of RPUs with boundary conflict.
The LGU had collaborated with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to impose administrative remedies on delinquent taxpayers. The next step is the enforcement of judicial action, which is under the authority of the Provincial Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: The effect of the improved and more aggressive tax collection strategies shall be further evaluated once the Financial Statements and other financial records are submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the management enhance its planning process and identification of disaster risk reduction and management related-PPAs by including only those PPAs that are realizable and implementation ready in order to ensure linkage between the annual plan and budget as prescribed under Item 2.3 of the Local Budget Operations Manual.
observations.managementAction: Management enhanced its planning process to ensure linkage between the AIP and the executive budget as prescribed in the local Budget Operation Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management incorporate in the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan the programs/projects/activities to be charged against the Prior Years’ unexpended LDRRM Fund balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Prior year’s unexpended LDRRM Fund are included in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the Guidelines on Electronic Payment for Government Transactions and henceforth, prepare and attach the duly approved Disbursement Vouchers on all government remittances of mandatory contributions/loan repayments made through Direct e-Payment scheme pursuant to COA Circular No. 2021-004.
observations.managementAction: Duly approved DVs were already attached in the remittance of BIR, GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC paid through electronic payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Facilitate the gathering of the necessary documents required in the derecognition of the properties already under the control of the Barangays or other entities and secure an authority by way of a resolution to authorize the Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation (DOD) or a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); b. Draw a Journal Entry Voucher derecognizing the assets from the books of the Municipality and furnish the respective beneficiaries a copy of the JEV and MOA/DOD to facilitate the recording in the latter’s respective books; and c. Provide the Office of the Auditor copies of the JEV together with the supporting documents taking up the adjustment.
observations.managementAction:
Necessary documents are being gathered for the derecognition of these assets. A duly approved SB Resolution was secured to authorize the execution of a Deed of Donation and/or Memorandum of Agreement.
JEV was drawn for the significant number of assets to be derecognized in the books.
Copies of JEV and its supporting documents were provided to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Health Office, and other concerned offices to submit the following documents: a) The list of public health workers, including Barangay Health Workers, determined and approved by the Local Health Board; b) Specific authority from the head of the agency to the claimants to physically report and respond to the public health emergency indicating therein the assigned work stations/health facilities for COVID-19, the prescribed official working hours, and their duties and responsibilities; c) Daily Time Records showing the actual date and time of the services rendered by the claimants as certified; and d) Certification attesting to the truthfulness of the contents of the report and adequate consent for sharing of data obtained from the personnel involved. The certification must be signed by the head of human resource or personnel division and the head of the health facility, clearly indicating his/her designation or position; and Accomplishment Reports showing that the claimants have provided direct treatment and support care to COVID-19 patients.
observations.managementAction: All documents required in audit were submitted by the management including lists, authorizations, DTRs, certifications and accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) together with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and other concerned offices to address the root cause of the non-implementation of the programmed developmental projects and come up with a concrete plan of avoiding the same lapses. We likewise recommended that the Mayor instruct the MEO to fast-track the implementation of the development projects that are unimplemented as of December 31, 2021, and see to it that the same are completed in CY 2022. We also recommended that the Mayor instruct the MPDO to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development projects in accordance with the approved development plans as provided in Section 476 of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Developmental projects were implemented in CY2023. MPDO monitored the implementation of these developmental projects in accordance with the approved plans. The MEO and MPDO took actions to implement and monitor the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other concerned offices to submit the Program Framework/Activity Design for COVID-19 operations showing therein the specific objectives, project, and activities, target period of accomplishment, responsible department/office, and the budgetary requirements.
observations.managementAction: Pertinent documents were already submitted by the MPDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accounting Office in coordination with the Bid and Awards Committee to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Pertinent documents were already submitted to the MACCO for the evaluation and recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the MDRRM Officer to submit copies of the legal basis for realignment and augmentation of all COVID-19-related funds sourced from the Current/Continuing appropriation of the LDRRM Fund and the use of unspent LDDRM Fund in Special Trust Fund for COVID-19-related PPAs and expenses.
observations.managementAction: The documents required were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Office (GSO) in the preparation of the RPCPPE, ensure: (a) The adaption of the classification of PPE accounts used in the accounting records; (b) That each the condition of each property item during the physical count are indicated in the report to determine its serviceability and if disposal thereof is proper; and, (c) the inclusion of the Other Infrastructure Assets, Military, Police and Security Equipment, Other Machinery and Equipment, and Books. Further, we recommended to the GSO to use the format prescribed by the Government Accounting Manual in the preparation of RCPPE and to ensure that the report is duly certified by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency or its authorized representative. Also, we recommended to ensure that an invitation to witness the conduct of the physical count is sent to the COA Audit Team. Finally, we recommended directing the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to establish an effective or practical system that would ensure the expeditious and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the municipality's Property, Plant, and Equipment taking into consideration the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The GSO ensured the adaptation of the proper classification of PPE accounts used in accounting records. The prescribed format was used by the GSO in the preparation of RPCPPE. The MAACO and GSO worked effectively in the initial reconciliation of records between offices. An invitation letter and the PIP for the one-time cleansing of PPE dated 11/8/2023 was sent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC- Municipal Accountant impose a deadline on the submission of documents on all claims for accrual in order to ensure timely charging and recording of expenses in the year these are incurred pursuant to Section 119 of PD 1445 and IPSAS 19.
observations.managementAction: The management had imposed deadlines for all claims to ensure timely recording of transactions in accordance with the accrual basis of accounting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the AAR Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the concerned implementing offices to:

observations.recommendation:

b. Henceforth, instruct the heads of municipal offices to require the personnel hired under job order contracts from their respective offices to prepare and submit duly reviewed and approved accomplishment reports to support their claims for salaries and wages.
observations.managementAction: The agency had submitted the justification letter dated Feb. 23, 2023, and was acknowledged by the audit team. The JOs submitted their Accomplishment Reports, which were duly approved by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the AAR management sees to it that contracts of job order personnel of the Municipality are entered into and signed by the Municipal Mayor regardless of the source of funds and whether or not they report or are assigned to the Sangguniang Bayan or to the Office of the Vice Mayor unless the latter is duly authorized to enter into and sign contracts concerning job order personnel charged against the funds of the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: As per verification, the job order contracts in CY2023 were properly signed by the Municipal Mayor except for those assigned under the Sangguniang Bayan and the Vice Mayor’s Office, wherein the Vice Mayor was authorized to sign as per General Provisions of the Municipality’s CY2023 Appropriation Ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management incorporate in the Investment Plan the programs/projects/ activities to be charged against the Prior Years’ LDRRM Fund unexpended balances and also recommended the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund on a monthly basis in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted a monthly Utilization Report of the LDRRMF and included in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs under the Special Trust Fund.

The LGU had collaborated with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to impose administrative remedies on delinquent taxpayers. The next step is the process to enforce judicial action, which is under the authority of the Provincial Treasurer’s Office.

c. issue policy on the maximum allowable amount of reimbursable RATA to be claimed by the designated OICs and appropriate funds in the MOOE budget of the Municipality for the payment of reimbursable RATA.
observations.managementAction: Management has provided the accountable officers with the option of a salary deduction. Management has set the policy for the maximum allowable amount of reimbursable RATA. The management has provided the accountable officers with the option of a salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee automatically disqualify the bidders/suppliers who are not eligible to participate in the procurement activity of the Municipality due to conflict of interest in accordance with Section 1 (a), Rule IX of RA 6713 and Section 47 of RIRR on RA 9184. We also recommended to the management to require the former Bids and Awards Committee to submit to the Office of the Auditor a justification letter on the procurement of goods to MMCOW despite an apparent conflict of interest.

The management had implemented the recommendation; however, collection/liquidation for these accounts was still minimal. Management has issued demand letters to recipient LGUs. The management submitted a status report as per AAPSI dated August 10, 2023.

observations.managementAction: The management is committed to discontinuing the practice of releasing additional funds to barangays that already have unliquidated fund transfers. For future fund releases, management will ensure that Memoranda of Agreement (MOAs) are established.

Basalo issued an Executive Order Reconstituting the Disposal Committee to follow disposal procedures based on Sec. 267 of LGAM & Sec. 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: The Municipal Accounting Office to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the fund transfers as prescribed under Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005. observations.recommendation: a) refrain from granting cash advances to the Disbursing Officer beyond the allowed maximum cash accountability; otherwise, increase the amount of the fidelity bond coverage of the AO in accordance with the fidelity bond requirements as per Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 and b) utilize the ATM Payroll System in releasing all personnel salaries, benefits and incentives.

observations.recommendation: a) instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to prepare and deliver tax bills to property owners; undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive tax collection strategies including consistent preparation and issuance of Notices of Delinquency for all delinquent RPUs; conduct dialogue with the said owners of the RPUs under CARP to address concerns on the collection of taxes and, if warranted, request the presence of the Provincial Treasurer and Provincial Legal Officer to assist in such undertaking; b) direct the Municipal Assessor to review the assessment rolls specifically on the double assessment of properties and submit the list of RPUs with boundary conflict; and c) for the delinquent real property taxes with NDs already served, recommend to the Provincial Treasurer on the imposition of the legal remedies either by administrative auction through a levy on real property or judicial action, following the procedures authorized in the LGC, to enforce collection and to undertake the same expeditiously on a yearly basis to reduce amounts accumulated as receivables for delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: 1) Advised the concerned office to continuously prepare tax bills to property owners. 2) Delivery of tax bills was made daily. 3) A series of assemblies and meetings were attended by MTO. 4) Disseminate information regarding tax amnesty as per Provincial Ordinance No. 105-2023. The Municipal Assessor has reviewed the assessment rolls and determined that the cause of double assessment of properties was due to the double claimants whose properties were under court litigation. As per verbal inquiry dated 12/7/2023, the MTO continually serves NDs until October 2023 to the delinquent RPUs. The MTO submitted a schedule of a list of Real Property Tax Delinquency on 12/27/2023 with the status of issuance of Notices. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) together with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and other concerned offices to address the root cause of the non-implementation of the programmed development projects and come up with a concrete plan of avoiding the same lapses. observations.recommendation:
We recommended directing the PTO to recommend the adoption of electronic technology in the payment of its obligations.
Examples of transactions are as follows.
For Suppliers and Other Creditors– enter into an agreement for a bank-to-bank payment, but it should ensure timely issuance of Official Receipt.
For Personal Claims – Deposit direct to their respective bank account with deposit slip as evidence of payment claimed.
Also, document communication made with creditors to properly establish evidence that they were informed that their respective check is available. Otherwise, informed in writing the concerned Department Head who had supervision over the transaction. If no reply is received after two (2) duly received communications, recommend for the cancellation of the transaction.
observations.managementAction:
Adoption of Automatic Debit Account (ADA) / List of Due and Demandable Accounts Payable (LDDAP) scheme in paying its obligation instead of check issuance with LBP
Examples of transactions are as follows.
For Suppliers and Other Creditors– enter into an agreement for a bank-to-bank payment, but it should ensure timely issuance of Official Receipt.
For Personal Claims – Deposit direct to their respective bank account with deposit slip as evidence of payment claimed.
Notified the Department Heads and posting the list of employees Personal Claims at the PTO.

observations.recommendation: 47.Cause the return or remittance of the balances of transferred funds to the Source Agency for completed projects; and 48.If warranted, subject the dormant Due to NGAs for write-off.
observations.reason: Initially recognized with Allowance for Impairment.

observations.recommendation: 49.The PGSO and the OIC-Provincial Accountant to evaluate the existing processes and identify those which are non-compliant with the NGAS; to reconcile inventory records of both; and perform their inventory function in accordance with NGAS so that inventory records will be in order and regular disposition of inventories will be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Team had already conducted physical count on PPEs on the last quarter of 2021.
observations.reason: However, inventories on supplies was not yet done.

observations.recommendation: 52. Management should strengthen their manifesting commitment in protecting the assets of the Province, particularly the land, by obtaining Copy of the Transfer Certificate of Titles (TCT’s) in the name of the Provincial Government of Davao del Sur within a reasonable time frame and the monitoring closely this activity, thus establishing ownership.
observations.managementAction: Based on the records of the Provincial Assessor, as of December 31, 2021, there were sixty-eight (68) properties/landholdings located in the different municipalities under the name of the Provincial Government of Davao del Sur. However, the Certificate of Titles are still being processed.
observations.reason: Compliance and processing of the documentary requirements needed from the different agencies take time due to some issues and availability of signatories.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the officers concerned to conduct inventory taking of the subject inventories and submit the report to the PACCO for reconciliation. 

We also recommended that Management require the PACCO to carefully examine/trace the source documents for any discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at an accurate balance of Inventories.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate and reconcile balances with the PGSO and PACCO records
observations.reason: The PACCO is still on the process of reconciling balances with PGSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor direct the officers concerned to apply the guidelines and procedures explicitly provided in COA Circular 2020-006 through adopting the following measures:

1) Reinforce the Inventory Committee which shall be –

• composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less;

• temporarily relieved of all their regular duties during the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;

• responsible for the actual count to ascertain the existence, completeness and condition of all PPEs owned by the Province; and

• plan/strategize, in coordination with the Property Division/Unit, on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least, the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; and

2) Property records shall be updated based on the results of the physical inventory and reconciled with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE.

Likewise, for CY 2021 conduct physical count, ensure henceforth that all PPE items will be included and all required reports will be submitted to COA within the prescribed timeline.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate and reconcile balances with the PGSO and PACCO records
observations.reason: The PACCO is still on the process of reconciling balances with PGSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the:

1. Concerned contractors to immediately rectify or re-block the defects noted and implement or install the lacking component of the projects revealed during the inspection to keep off from disallowance in audit. Vigilantly monitor the condition of the above projects since these were newly completed and yet latent defects were already observed.  Defects that occurred, especially within the warranty period, should be on the account of the contractor; and

2. PEO to reevaluate its system of monitoring and supervision of the projects to ensure that incurrence of the deficiencies or discrepancies noted would be avoided.

We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct: 

1. The department concerned to observe the provision embodied in section 68 of RA 9184 pertaining to the imposition of appropriate liquidated damages. 
2. The office concerned to properly accomplish the PO so that the liability of both parties will be clearly defined and easy to enforce in case of breach of the agreements. 
3. The requisitioning or implementing offices to fully observe the provision of 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 04-2016 pertaining to documentation and posting requirement. 
4. The office concerned to submit a justification letter indicating the reason/s why the noted liquidated damages were not imposed, for us to further evaluate and recommend other possible actions.

We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct:

The contractors to immediately rectify or re-block the defects noted and implement or install the lacking component of the projects revealed during the inspection to prevent issuance of Notice/s of Disallowance. Vigilantly monitor the condition of the above projects since these were newly completed and yet latent defects were already observed. Defects occurred, especially within the warranty period, should be on the account of the contractor.

The Provincial Engineer Office (PEO) to reevaluate its system of   monitoring and supervision of the projects to ensure that the noted deficiencies or discrepancies will be avoided.
observations.managementAction: The contractors made some rectification on the noted defects of the projects.
observations.reason: Per TAS report three (3) projects were subject to liquidated damages namely; Road Upgrading of Dagohoy Daligdigon, Anahaw Maligaya, Maligaya New Camiling and one (1) project found to have major defects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor directs:

The contractors to immediately rectify or re-block the defects noted and implement or install the lacking component of the projects revealed during the inspection to prevent the issuance of Notice/s of Disallowance. Vigilantly monitor the condition of the above projects since these were newly completed and yet major defects were already observed. Defects occurred, especially within the warranty period, should be on the account of the contractor.

The Provincial Engineer Office (PEO) to reevaluate its system of   monitoring and supervision of the projects to ensure that the noted deficiencies or discrepancies will be avoided in future undertakings.
observations.managementAction: Immediate rectification and re-blocking were done. The activities were undertaken by PEO charged against the 10% retention of the contractor. Sometime in September 2020, the area experienced heavy rains that resulted to the collapsed of the road and damaged the existing PCCP. Large quantity of embankment was displaced and eroded. The area is being cordoned for safety while the POW for the repair is being prepared.
observations.reason: Supporting documents on the management action were not attached to AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to:

Direct the contractors concerned to immediately rectify the defects noted and implement or install the lacking component of the projects found during the inspection to prevent the issuance of Notice of Disallowance. Monitor the condition of the above projects as these were newly completed projects and yet latent defects were already observed. Defects occurred, especially within the warranty period, should be on the account of the contractor. 

Likewise, direct the Provincial Engineer Office (PEO) to reevaluate its system of   monitoring and supervision of the projects to ensure that the noted deficiencies or discrepancies will be avoided.
observations.managementAction: Rectification was done by the contractor.
observations.reason: Complete rectification not yet

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to immediately reconstitute the TWG of the PAMANA program. Assign and /or designate a Project Management Team (PMT) to closely administer the project implementation in coordination with the OPAPP. Make a partnership with other local agencies like the municipality and barangays in the implementation of the project.

Also, reevaluate the existing status of the project and determine the other causes of the delayed operation of the project.
observations.reason: Supporting documents on the management action were not attached to AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct:
The Provincial Engineer to implement internal control measures on the utilization of fuel. Effectively control/monitor the usage of fuel through the full observance of the rules and regulations pertaining to fuel consumption. Regularly check and verify the correctness of the DTT prepared by the drivers. The DTT should be prepared in two copies and serially-numbered.

The General Services Office (GSO) to submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the COA Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month.   

The appropriate office to repair all constructions and heavy equipment with defective odometers.
observations.managementAction: Adherence to audit recommendations
observations.reason: Ongoing installation of internal control measures

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor directs the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) and Information Technology Office (ITO) to coordinate with the PEEDO-Blood Bank for the installation/application of the ETRACS, and train the designated PEEDO-Blood Bank Revenue Collector on how to use the E-TRACS.
observations.managementAction: The PTO and ITO coordinated with PEEDO Bloodbank and conducted daily monitoring on their ETRACS utilization.
observations.reason: Validation of collection pertaining to the PEEDO-Blood Bank found out that there are still collections were issued in manual.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to give utmost priority the provision of adequate budget as counterpart for the establishment of Sanitary Landfill project in line with the 10-Year Provincial Solid Waste Management Plan (PSWMP). 

Likewise, require the PENRO to fast-track the documentary and other requirements for its immediate implementation in line with the PSWM Plan, and pursuant to Section 37 of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
The PSWMB held meeting on February 3, 2021 and resolved the following to provide immediate solution concerning waste disposal management of LGUs:

Resolution enjoining LGUs in Davao del Norte to establish and improve respective containment areas as short-term solution in the Phase pf Closure Order of the DENR-EMB RXI;
Resolution requesting Gov. Jubahib to endorse the intent of LGUs in Davao del Norte to dispose solid waste to Panabo City SLF subject to the terms and conditions provided by the Panabo City LGU; and
Resolution enjoining LGUs in Davao del Norte for a clustered approach integrated solid waste disposal facility
observations.reason: Engineering works cannot proceed to the proposed site for Sanitary Landfill at Brgy. San Jose, Sto. Tomas. Permitting and land feasibility constraints delay land acquisition process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Provincial Governor to enjoin the Drug Consignment Management Committee (DCMC), through the Provincial Economic Enterprise Development Office (PEEDO), to use the mandated Drug Price Reference Index (DPRI) issued by the DOH. The ceiling prices contain therein shall not be exceeded by the contract price of the Consignment Agreement that will be entered into by the Province, to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The province differed the payment of drugs and medicines to the consignors.
observations.reason: The answer to appeal by the management was already forwarded to the COA RO XI – Legal Division for review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct the Provincial Engineering Office (PEO) and other concerned Office/Departments to closely monitor and ensure economical and efficient implementation of all projects under PAMANA Program of the national government (DA and DILG).

Likewise, the management shall develop, adopt and implement systems and procedures necessary to sustain the life of the project or facility being the responsible party as the implementing agency (IA), as stipulated in the MOA executed for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: Several trainings and seminar implemented by province to the recipient to ensure the proper implemented of the said projects.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its CAO and CGSO to –
Provide the supporting documents as basis for dropping the PPE items from the books/records;
Make the necessary adjustments to correct the entries made for dropped PPE items that were not authorized; and
Carry out the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices –
CGSO
Property Unit – Promptly list down all obsolete and unserviceable PPE with complete data in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), duly signed by the City Accountant, Inspector and Appraisal Committee, and approved by the Head of the Agency or authorized representative and submit to the Disposal Committee for disposal as early as possible; and
Disposal Committee – Immediately dispose of all unserviceable/obsolete PPEs using the appropriate mode of disposal as determined and recommended by the Disposal Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from its sale, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items.
CAO – Require the CGSO to submit the necessary documents together with the Official Receipt after disposal, as the basis to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts to come up with the accurate balance of the PPE account in accordance with the accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices –
CGSO
Conduct a physical count of all properties received by the City through donation and furnish the City Accountant with the inventory list and pertinent donation papers as basis of the latter in the recording of the donated assets in the books of accounts; and
Coordinate with the City Assessor’s Office to conduct an appraisal of the donated properties to reflect its fair value measurement in compliance with IPSAS 23.
CAO
Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record the donated vehicles in the books for proper presentation in the financial statements; and
Ensure that donated assets in the ensuing periods, if any, with the City as Donee are properly recognized in the books of the LGU.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices –
CGSO/Inventory Committee/Property Officer – Perform test for impairment of  PPE at least annually, as required in IPSAS 21, and submit the impairment loss, if any, to the City Accountant for purposes of recognizing impairment loss; and
CAO – Recognize impairment loss, if any, in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the CAO to make the appropriate adjusting entries to correct the balances of Due to NGAs and Government Equity accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the offices concerned to henceforth:
Refrain from disbursing the funds when the validity of such subsidies had expired;
Terminate those projects that have been awarded after the prescriptive period as detailed in the applicable LBCs and revert the funds immediately to the National Treasury; and
BAC and BAC Secretariat - verify whether applicable prescriptive periods for various LGSF subsidies have expired before giving notices of award to the winning bidders of projects funded by LGSF.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its CTO to –
Secure adequate fidelity bond of the accountable officers to safeguard the government assets in case of loss through theft, misappropriation, and other losses due to fortuitous events;
Monitor properly the change in accountability of each accountable officer and see to it that the amount of their bond premiums conforms with the amount of their accountabilities; and
Appropriate yearly a budget to cover the payment of premiums on the renewal of the fidelity bonds both for public funds and property.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its CGSO to –
Retrieve the records of the properties found to have no tags and facilitate the placement/attachment of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and
Attach Property Tags to the newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management remind its responsible offices:
CAO –Record in the books of accounts all purchases of supplies, whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, as Inventory while its issuances are recorded as expenses, with RIS and SSMI as support documents. Further, a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, issuance and disposal pursuant to the Manual of NGAS; and
CGSO – Consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a weekly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the City Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the Manual on NGAS.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the CGD Management direct the LSB to ensure that the funds allocated for SEF be budgeted and utilized in accordance with Section 100(c), and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the offices concerned to:
Set specific deadlines on the submission of ownership documents from recipient barangays and if they fail to submit within the timelines, consider reverting the funds to more feasible projects;
Revert the funds of projects which overlapped with DPWH and other government offices’ projects; and
Regularly monitor the status of 20% DF to ensure that priority projects in the annual investment plan are carried out on time.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices –
DCDRRMO
Enhance monitoring of LDRRMF utilization focusing on PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of DRRM; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that the goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities are achieved.
BAC & CGSO – Expedite the procurement process to guarantee the full implementation of the PPAs incorporated in the LDRRMFIP within the set period.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices –
CGSO
Conduct physical count of all the remaining donated items and issue ICS on semi-expendable donations in-kind;
Prepare an inventory list of the remaining donated items for DRRM every month or every quarter if the City is under quarantine for more than three months and determine the condition of these items; and
Henceforth, prepare and maintain all the reports and documents necessary to account and monitor the receipt and issuances of all donations in kind and submit a copy of the required reports to the Audit Team.
CMO & CSWDO – Henceforth, coordinate with the CGSO as to the receipt of the in-kind donations for proper recording and to establish accountability over the donated goods.
CAO
Ensure that all Acknowledgement Receipts for the In-Kind donations are submitted for recording and disclosures in the financial statements are made of the donations in kind received; and
Submit the monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) within the prescribed period in compliance with existing Circulars.
All Concerned Offices – Endeavor to update with all the guidelines that are necessary for operations, especially during the emergency phase.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices –
CGSO – For similar transactions in the future, furnish the City Accountant with duplicate copies of all Acknowledgement Receipts of in-kind donations, invoice receipts, and inventory list of donated items for DRRM; and
CAO – Summarize the data from the documents submitted according to the required disclosure, compare records with the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM maintained by the CGSO for check and balance, and disclose the necessary information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
CPDO – Submit the Amended/Updated Annual Investment Program incorporating the CY 2021 LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP to avoid suspension in audit;
City Planning and Development Coordinator – Coordinate with the concerned offices and ensure the inclusion of the LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP PPAs in the AIP of the City in compliance with the abovementioned provisions; and
DCDRRMO & CAO – strictly observe the policies and procedures in the utilization of the LDRRMF.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Issue an Agency Memorandum/Order duly signed by the Head of the Agency designating focal person(s)/ officer(s) who shall be responsible for the upload of reports and coordination relative to the use of the COA Portal; and
Prepare and submit the applicable reports relevant to CGD’s receipts, transfers and utilization of Bayanihan 2 funds using the templates prescribed as required under COA Circular No. 2021-002.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its Department/Unit Heads –
Submit justification on the use of the government vehicles on weekends, holidays, or out of the regular office hours or outside the route of the officials or employees authorized to use them to indicate the necessity of such travels;
Instruct the vehicle drivers to properly accomplish the GASTA and other supporting documents necessary for the payment of fuel consumed for the period to avoid disallowance; and
Strictly implement and adhere to the pertinent provisions of the Administrative Order No. 239 and COA Circular No 77-61 dated September 26, 1977, prescribing the use of the “Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles”.

We recommended that the:

     1. PEO and PACCO regularly reconcile their reports and records on  the
        real status of implemented projects so that these will be  properly
        recorded in the books.

     2. PACCO  to  effect  the  necessary  accounting  journal  entries  to
        reclassify the completed projects to its  appropriate  PPE  account
        and recognize the correct depreciation expense for the period.

We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct:


      1. The Provincial  Engineer  to  prepare  the  RIS  for  the  utilized
         construction materials and submit the same to the PGSO;
      2. The PGSO to prepare the RSMI for submission to PACCO;
      3. The PACCO to prepare a JV to record  the  issuances  based  on  the
         RSMI; and
      4.  The Inventory Team to conduct a  physical  count  of  construction
         materials inventory to confirm its  existence  and  to  report  the
         accurate balance of the account.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the  Provincial  Engineer
to:

     1.  Identify,  recommend  and  enforce  immediate  solutions  to   the
        obstacles affecting the fast implementation of projects;


     2. Conduct regular evaluation on the status of implementation of major
        infrastructure projects and report to the  Honorable  Governor  the
        results thereof

We recommended the Provincial Governor to:

     1. Direct the Implementing Office of each project to conduct  strategic
        planning for the fast implementation thereof.

     2. Determine if the projects are still implementable or not.


     3. Direct the Provincial Treasurer to revert to the  National  Treasury
        those funds with unimplementable project.

We recommended that :

     1. The PBO and PTO must regularly reconcile their records to arrive at
        the correct information pertaining to the loans availed of  by  the
        Province.


     2. The PBO must report  in  the  SAAOB  the  total  loan  releases  as
        Allotment which indicates that the appropriation is backed up  with
        cash. The obligations must be deducted from the Allotment to ensure
        payment of these obligations.

We  recommended  that  the  Provincial  Governor  direct  the  Head  of  the
Implementing Office of the Livelihood Projects too:

      1. Prepare a strategic plan on how to hasten the  fast  implementation
        of livelihood projects;


      2. Conduct regular evaluation on the status of implementation  of  the
        identified projects and report to the Honorable Governor the results
        thereof.

We recommended the Provincial Governor to:

     1. Direct the PDRRM Officer to identify the causes/problems for the non-
        utilization  of  the  appropriation  for  capital  outlay  and  find
        solutions  to  fast  track  the  accomplishment  of  the  plan   and
        utilization of the budget.

We recommended that the Provincial  Governor  direct  the  GAD  Focal  Point
Person to:

      1. Identify, recommend and enforce immediate actions to fast track the
        implementation of programs.


     1. Determine early  on  if  there  are  unimplementable  programs  and
        request for a realignment to more meaningful and beneficial ones.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) to make the necessary adjusting entries on the P76,600.00 net book reconciling items in the books of accounts and accordingly, provide copies of the relevant journal entry vouchers to the Audit Team.
We also recommended that the Management direct the PACCO to henceforth immediately prepare the necessary correcting/adjusting entries for any discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items noted.

We recommended that the Management create an inventory committee which shall henceforth conduct a semestral physical count of all inventories of the Provincial Government of Davao de Oro with the Audit Team or its duly authorized representative.
We likewise recommended that the Management direct the Provincial Accounting Office to reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records together with the Provincial General Services Office and thereafter submit a duly-reconciled Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Management direct the:
Provincial Engineering Office to submit the Certificates of Completion and Acceptance covering all completed projects to the Provincial Accounting Office as well as to the Provincial General Services Office for the recognition of the completed CIP projects in their respective records; and
Provincial Accounting Office to reclassify all completed Construction in Progress accounts to their appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment accounts; provide for the corresponding depreciation expenses; and thereafter submit to the Audit Team the relevant journal entry vouchers together with Certificates of Completion and Acceptance.

We recommended that the Management instruct the PACCO to:
Refrain from making corollary entries recognizing Subsidy from National Government for the utilized portion of the Due to National Government Agencies account; and
Henceforth, observe strictly the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-009 or the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in recording the transactions of the Provincial Government of Davao de Oro, particularly the use of the Subsidy from National Government account.

We recommended that the Management instruct the PACCO to henceforth require on a monthly basis the PGSO to submit the relevant RIS and SSMI on all supplies issued and to use the proper accounts in the recording of inventories.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor instruct the Provincial Health Office and the Provincial Accounting Office to submit the following:
List of public HWs and frontline HRHs duly determined and certified by the Provincial Health Office;
Report on the number of COVID-19 patients attended by said HWs and HRHs covering the period December 20, 2020 to June 30, 2021; and
List of public HWs with justification on their inclusion for the SRA claim pertaining to the DDOPH – Pantukan and Maragusan; and
List of frontline HRHs with justification on their inclusion for the AHDP claim pertaining to the four (4) provincial hospitals and the Davao de Oro EOC.
Likewise, we recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Health Office and the Provincial Accounting Office to ensure, at all times, that the requirement on completeness of supporting documents before any payment is made to HWs and/or frontline HRHs and other health personnel relative to SRA, AHDP, and similar personnel benefits is strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) to submit, at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing government programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year to the Audit Team.
We likewise recommended that the Management direct the implementing offices to inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboard and/or public notices were already posted.
We also recommended that the Management instruct the PPDO to consolidate the quarterly report on PPAs of all implementing offices replete with the project name; implementing unit, office or division if it is not the agency as a whole; a brief description of the PPA; contractor or supplier, if any; mode of procurement; funding source, cost or approved budget; project duration including start and completion dates and location and thereafter submit the same to the Audit Team on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Budget Office, Provincial Accounting Office, and the Provincial Engineering Office to submit a justification why the salaries, overtime pay, and leave benefits of regular and casual employees as well as the repairs of a service vehicle were charged against the 20% DF notwithstanding the prohibition set in DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1.
We likewise recommended that the Provincial Governor instruct the concerned offices to henceforth refrain from charging Personal Services expenditures such as salaries, wages, overtime pay, and other personnel benefits as well as other expenses considered not allowable under the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification/s for the non-inclusion of the Accomplishment Report in the checklist of documentary requirements for the wages of JO personnel;
Submit the corresponding Programs of Work where the services of these JOs were part of; and
Henceforth require the heads of the implementing offices such as the Provincial Engineering Office and other offices employing JOs to check on the Accomplishment Reports of the latter before approving their daily time records.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the BAC and the PDRRMO to submit a justification why the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR were not strictly followed in the procurement of the subject relief goods.
We likewise recommended that the Provincial Governor instruct the PDRRMO to submit an explanation why the payments to the winning suppliers were not deducted from the required withholding taxes amounting to P116,878.66.
We also recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the BAC, the PDDRMO, and the Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) to submit the following:
1.	Approved Annual Procurement Plan containing the Procurement Project subject to Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases;
2.	Authority from the HOPE allowing the PDRRMO to directly negotiate with a legally, technically, and financially capable supplier; and
Mayor’s or Business Permit; Annual Income Tax Return of CY 2020 or Value Added Tax or Percentage Tax Return covering the previous six months; and Omnibus Sworn Statement of the winning suppliers except for RY Plastic Center, Inc.; and
Acknowledgment Receipt for In-Kind Donations for DRRM in accordance with Annex D of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Finally, we recommended that the Provincial Governor instruct the PACCO and the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to make payments for procurement projects only by checks and not through a cash advance unless the instances allowing resort to the latter are present as provided for under COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Management submit a justification for the non-withholding of the 10% Creditable Withholding Tax on the allowances and/or honorarium paid to judges, clerks of courts, and public attorneys.
We likewise recommended that the Management instruct the Provincial Accounting Office to deduct the applicable withholding tax on allowances, honorarium, and similar payments subject to withholding tax as required under Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We recommended that the Management direct the Provincial Agriculture Office (PAGRO) and the Provincial Veterinary Office (PVO) to fast track the completion of the subprojects by focusing on the causes of delays so as to remedy the same and accordingly, complete the projects in CY 2022.
We likewise recommended that the Management direct the PAGRO and the PVO to include the projects being implemented by their offices in the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs replete with the project name; implementing unit, office or division if it is not the agency as a whole; a brief description of the PPA; contractor or supplier, if any; mode of procurement; funding source, cost or approved budget; project duration including start and completion dates and location and thereafter submit the same to the Audit Team on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that the Management direct the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) together with the Provincial Engineering Office (PEO) and other concerned offices to address the root cause of the delay/non-implementation of the programmed development projects and come up with a concrete plan to accomplished the programmed projects, copy furnished the Audit Team.
We likewise recommended that the Management instruct the PEO to fast track the completion and/or implementation of the development projects not completed and unimplemented as of December 31, 2021 and see to it that the same are completed in CY 2022.
We also recommended that the Management instruct the PPDO to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development projects in accordance with the approved development plans as provided in Section 476 of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended for the Provincial Accountant record  the  reconciling  items
requiring adjustment as disclosed in the monthly BRS with a  net  amount  of
P4,132,648.11 in the books immediately.


We also recommended for the Provincial Accountant  to  prepare  the  monthly
BRS and record the reconciling items disclosed therein promptly  to  present
fairly the balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account  on
any given period.

We recommended for the Provincial Accountant’s  Office  and  the  Provincial
General Services Office to:


     1. Initiate immediate reconciliation of both records, and establish an
        effective or systematic procedure that would ensure the proper  and
        timely recording of the properties purchased  or  received  by  the
        Province; and


     2. Take up the necessary adjusting entries  to  correct  the  recorded
        amount.


We also recommended for the Provincial  Governor  to  require  the  PEO  and
Accounting Office to submit regularly to  the  GSO  the  list  of  completed

We recommended for the Provincial Accountant make  the  necessary  adjusting
entries to correct the misstatement under the Trust  Fund  books.  Moreover,
record future capital expenditures regarding the implementation of  PPAs  by

We recommended the following:

     1. For the hospital officials concerned  to  meticulously  plan  their
        purchase requirements during the year and adopt regular procurement
        methods in the purchase of hospital supplies and other  needs,  and
        ensure that enough commonly used and needed supplies and  medicines
        are available on stock.


     2. For the  department  officials  involved  in  the  procurement  and
        disbursement procedure of the Province  to  review  the  activities
        undertaken and identify means or manner in hastening the processing
        of procurements for the hospitals  and  for  the  replenishment  of
        their funds to regularly provide their operating needs  and  ensure
        continuous delivery of hospital services.


     3. For the hospital officials and Local Finance  Committee  to  assess
        the urgent financial needs of the hospitals to  be  funded  through
        petty cash fund or cash advance and ensure the release of this fund
        at the start of the year.


     4.  For  the  Provincial  Treasurer  to  adopt  monitoring  activities
        ensuring that all collections are deposited intact and on time.

We recommended for the Provincial Governor to strictly enforce  the  control
procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances as  provided  in
COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.


We also recommended for the agency officials concerned to meticulously  plan
their purchase  requirements  and  regularly  coordinate  with  the  BAC  on
procurement  matters  to  immediately  address  any  problems   encountered,
likewise to establish a satisfactory procurement system  that  could  timely
provide the supplies requirement of every department or office.

We  recommended  for  the  Provincial  Accountant  compute   and   take   up
depreciation expense in the books of accounts on a monthly basis.

We recommended the following:


   1. For the agency officials concerned to  submit  justification  and  the

   2. For the Provincial Accountant to perform her duties  with  the  utmost
      standard in reviewing the completeness, propriety and  correctness  of
      the payroll and its supporting documents.

We recommended for the agency officials  concerned  to  submit  to  the  COA
office the data of the specific PPAs not included in  the  quarterly  status
report totaling P171,655,479.20,  which  shall  include  the  project  name;
implementing unit, office or division if it is not the agency as a whole;  a
brief description of the PPA;  contractor  or  supplier,  if  any;  mode  of
procurement; funding source;  cost  or  approved  budget;  project  duration
including start and completion dates; and location.

We recommended for the Provincial Governor  direct  the  implementing  units
involved in the meticulous  preparation  and  regular  submission  of  their
quarterly report of PPAs to the PPMC for consolidation.

Finally,  we  recommended  for  the  PPMC  to  review   their   outputs   in
coordination with the Budget Office that would render a complete  report  on
the status of PPAs implemented by the Province  under  the  20%  development
fund and facilitate proper monitoring thereof.

     1. For the  PDRRMO  Head  to  ensure  the  strict  compliance  of  the

        March 2013 on the  charging  of  expenses  against  the  Province’s
        current year and unexpended LDRRMF.


     2. For the Provincial Budget Officer and the Provincial Accountant  to
        ensure that only expenses related to disaster  risk  reduction  and
        management activities and in line with the programs,  projects  and
        activities (PPAs) under the LDRRMF are charged from this fund.

We recommended the following:

      the DVs and their supporting documents.

We recommended for the agency officials concerned to strictly adhere to  the
prescribed procedures as provided in  the  IRR  of  the  R.A.  No.  9184  in
addressing slippage and delay  of  work  accomplished  by  contractors  with

We also recommended for the Bids and Awards  Committee  (BAC)  officials  to
review existing  procurement  procedures  that  may  be  undertaken  by  the
Province with Joint Venture contractors and  adopt  policies  to  strengthen
said control  procedures  in  deterring  adverse  effects  as  a  result  of
unsatisfactory performance by contractors.

We recommended for the Provincial  Local  School  Board  (LSB)  to  identify
priority programs/projects that may  be  funded  from  the  previous  years’
unappropriated balance approximately amounting  to  P35  Million  to  ensure
efficient utilization of the SEF.

We recommended for the Provincial LSB Chairperson to  instruct  the  SDS  in
requiring the submission of the approved municipality/city SEF Budgets  from
the municipal/city LSB and prepare the consolidated approved  municipal/city
SEF Budgets and list of proposals not  funded  from  the  said  budgets  for
submission to the Provincial LSB.

Likewise, we recommended for the Provincial LSB to take  into  consideration
the consolidated approved municipality/city SEF  Budgets  and  the  list  of
proposals not funded  from  the  said  budgets  as  concrete  bases  in  the
preparation of the Provincial SEF Budget.

         to ensure that all assistance in kind received not  only  from  DOH
         but also from other National Government Agencies will  be  properly
         recorded in the books.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the:
PACCO (i) submit the BRS for the General Fund bank account in the Development Bank of the Philippines within a reasonable time, and prepare adjustments to the book of accounts if warranted; (ii) ensure the timely preparation and submission of BRS for all bank accounts to the Audit Team; (iii) conduct a detailed review and checking of bank records or reports and request for the necessary documents to support the recording of reconciling items; and
PTO to coordinate with the agencies or offices that maintained funds with the bank that were tagged as non-moving to determine if such accounts are still vital and essential to their purpose and make appropriate action thereto.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor:
Direct the PACCO to prepare an appropriate adjusting entry of the noted understatement of the Drugs and Medicine Inventory account;
Direct the PHO, upon receipt of the vaccines, to submit immediately a separate report of PTR to PACCO for the latter to record the items on time. For the utilization, prepare also a separate report on a monthly basis and submit it to the PACCO within a reasonable time; and
Instruct the PHO to create a system in coming up with timely report preparation and submission.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct:
The PEO to submit the necessary supporting documents of the above-mentioned completed projects to the accounting department through PGSO.
The offices concerned to evaluate the current system relating to the preparation and submission of the PCRs to PACCO to avoid the delayed closing of the Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts once the project is already completed.
The PACCO to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset account if the necessary documents are already at hand. Provide the appropriate depreciation expenses if the asset is subject to depreciation.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the PACCO to:
Conduct a thorough review and analysis of the Due to NGAs account under the GF and TF, and make the necessary adjustments, where appropriate;
Return the excess amount from the fully completed projects to the source agencies pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and
Coordinate with the implementing offices of the long outstanding fund transfers so that appropriate actions may be undertaken thereon for effective and efficient utilization of the fund, and ensure that the intended beneficiaries could benefit therefrom.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Administrator’s Office (PADO), Provincial Health Office (PHO) and Provincial Accountant's Office (PACCO) to submit a written explanation on the above-mentioned deficiencies to make an audit decision.  Likewise, the Province shall be reminded on ensuring that supporting documents are complete before granting benefits to personnel and at all times, monitor compliance and strict observance of the rules and regulations in the granting and release of SRA to facilitate the payment of the COVID-19 SRA only to the eligible public and private HWs.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the:
Provincial Accountant's Office (PACCO) to send out additional demand letters to the LGUs concerned with unliquidated fund transfer and require its immediate liquidation; strengthen the internal controls pertaining to fund transfers so that easy monitoring of its status can be had and timely demand will be made to the recipient LGUs;
Provincial Engineering Office (PEO) to strictly perform the monitoring and evaluation of the infrastructure projects by conducting ocular inspections or visits to validate the implementation of the projects and ensuring that these conform with the executed MOAs; and
Remind the recipient LGUs to fully adhere to the MOA, otherwise, if the condition noted continues to exist, additional measures in granting and releasing of funds will be incorporated in the said agreement, so that all parties will be compelled to perform their roles and obligations, thereby ensuring efficient implementation of the project and timely liquidation of the funds.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the PEO and the BAC to carefully review all the supporting documents of every transaction; organize documents, data and information to avoid the noted deficiency from recurring. Likewise, require them to submit written justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies, for further evaluation by the audit team.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Direct the Local Finance Committee together with the Internal Audit Division to draft a comprehensive written policy on the grant of financial assistance, incorporating among others the control measures to ensure efficient and effective disbursement of government funds.
Also, the LFC should properly use the appropriate account in the budget preparation (e.g., Subsidy to LGUs, Honoraria, Salary, Overtime, Welfare Goods, Other Supplies and Materials Expense)

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Budget Office, Provincial Accounting Office, and the Provincial Engineering Office to submit a justification why the salaries and overtime pay of regular and casual employees were charged against the 20% DF notwithstanding the prohibition set in DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1.

We recommended to the management to holistically observe the purpose and intent of the procurement law in putting a maximum period in conducting procurement processes, which is to fasten the project implementation. Likewise, the issuance of NTCW must be in accordance with the tenor of the NTC and PO.

We recommended that the Management direct the officials concerned to review all the projects undertaken and to be undertaken by the Province including the projects noted in our observation and come up with a concrete action to address the deficiencies noted.
We also enjoined the offices concerned to address the root cause of the delay/non-implementation- like the Provincial Engineering Office (PEO),  if it pertains to the preparation of POW and other technical matters,  fast track its preparation, the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) if it pertains to procurement matter, and other agencies tasked to implement the project in case of fund transfers.
Likewise, we recommended that the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) will closely monitor and evaluate the status of the implementation of projects.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct:
All the assigned PRDP personnel in charge work hand-in-hand for the completion of the projects for the ultimate benefit of the intended users.
The contractors to rectify the defects noted and install the lacking component of the projects observed during the inspection to prevent the issuance of Notice/s of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct:
The contractor to immediately rectify the defects noted and implement or install the lacking component of the projects revealed during the inspection to prevent the issuance of Notice/s of Disallowance. Vigilantly monitor the condition of the above projects since these were newly completed and yet latent defects were already observed. Defects that occurred, especially within the warranty period, should be on the account of the contractor.
The Provincial Engineer Office (PEO) to reevaluate its system of monitoring and supervision of the projects to ensure that the noted deficiencies or discrepancies will be avoided.

We recommended that the management direct:
The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to monitor and evaluate the implementation of all LDRRMF PPAs. Include in the evaluation process the reasons/causes why the projects were not realized as planned. Come up with the suited strategies to address the perennial problems encountered to avoid recurring in the future;
The PEO, being the implementing department, strategizes its whole year activities to address the noted deficiencies. Likewise, scrutinize the contractors' performance concerning project completion. The office should be cautious enough in giving contract extensions, particularly to those contractors who keep incurring a delay. Extension must be based only on valid grounds with appropriate documentation; and
The PDRRMO and other offices concerned to plan the procurement activities carefully regarding the LDRRMF PPAS so as not to jeopardize its implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor direct the officers concerned to apply the guidelines and procedures explicitly provided in COA Circular 2020-006 through adopting the following measures:

1) Reinforce the Inventory Committee which shall be –

• composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less;

• temporarily relieved of all their regular duties during the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;

• responsible for the actual count to ascertain the existence, completeness and condition of all PPEs owned by the Province; and

• plan/strategize, in coordination with the Property Division/Unit, on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least, the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; and

2) Property records shall be updated based on the results of the physical inventory and reconciled with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE.

Likewise, for CY 2021 conduct physical count, ensure henceforth that all PPE items will be included and all required reports will be submitted to COA within the prescribed timeline.
observations.managementAction: Conduct physical count on inventory accounts

observations.recommendation:
