a) Direct those concerned to facilitate immediate processing to officially transfer the above PPE to the respective users/beneficiaries, secure an authority from the Sangguniang Panlalawigan by way of resolution to authorize the Governor to execute a Deed of Donation (DOD) or a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and

b) Require the Provincial Accountant to draw a JEV derecognizing the assets from the books of the Province and furnish the respective beneficiaries a copy of the JEV and MOA/DOD to facilitate the recording in the latter’s respective books.

	We also recommended that management sees to it that other properties not mentioned in our AOM but have a similar condition with our observation may also be derecognized in the books of accounts of the province.
observations.managementAction: Already acted on the 18 buildings mentioned in AOM 2021-016. Waiting for the approval of Sangguniang Panlalawigan

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management of the Provincial Government of Davao del Norte at all times disburse government funds i.e., BGP funds, with strict adherence to the provisions of the law, specifically Section 305(a) of RA 7160 and Section 4(1) of PD 1445.

We also recommended that concerned officials submit a written explanation why the incurrence of such lapses and why the amount of P376,085.44 should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Strict adherence to the provisions mentioned in the AOM.

Justifications were already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management shall always adhere to law, rules and regulations when disbursing government funds, specifically AO No. 26 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01 on the grant of hazard pay during ECQ, to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We also recommended that concerned management officials submit a written explanation on the above-mentioned deficiencies, for further evaluation by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate refunds were already acted

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor:

1) Direct the implementing offices concerned to: carefully review all the supporting documents of every transaction; organize documents, data and information so that review procedure can be easily made; and maintain a checklist of documentary requirements by recent laws, rules and regulations to avoid being missed;

2) Enjoin the BAC and the Accounting Department to assist the implementing offices on the need to comply with the documentary requirements on all transactions; and

3) Direct the responsible offices to submit written justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies, for further evaluation by the audit team.

We recommended that management re-evaluate and strengthen its existing controls on relief goods distribution operations and determine the areas that need improvement, particularly on documentation.

We also recommended that management adopt preventive measures to avoid the recurrence of the deficiencies noted and define the roles and responsibilities of each personnel assigned in the operation so that proper accountability can be easily set up in case of failure.

We further recommended that management require the personnel involved to explain the erasures or alterations of the documents presented and the discrepancies between the AR and CL.
observations.managementAction:
PSWDO immediately abide by the recommendations in strengthening the relief operations. Systems of relief distribution are in-placed. The following controls are installed:

LGU shall submit disaster report and request of relief goods indicating the number of affected families duly signed by LCE or authorized representative
PSWDO will immediately coordinate the LGU concerned to validate the request
Concerned LGU shall receive the relief goods duly signed 3 copies of RIS
After the distribution, LGU concerned shall submit to PSWDO the filled-up RDS for liquidation attachment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the:

1) Requisitioning or implementing offices to fully observe and/or comply with the provisions in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, Section 53.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and item 4.1.6 of GPPB Circular 04-2016; and

2) Offices concerned to accomplish properly the forms and reports by providing complete, accurate, and consistent information.

We also recommended that the Governor enjoin the PACCO to carefully evaluate and examine the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct his PGSO and PACCO to fully observe and/or comply with the prescribed policies and procedures on the accounting treatment of inventories per GAM for LGUs, Volumes I and II.
observations.managementAction: PACCO had complied the necessary adjusting entries to appropriate inventory accounts and complied the ICS of small tangible items amounting to P201,200.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct:

1) The department concerned to observe the provision embodied in section 68 of RA 9184 pertaining to the imposition of appropriate liquidated damages.

2) The office concerned to properly accomplish the PO so that the liability of both parties will be clearly defined and easy to enforce in case of breach of the agreements.

3) The requisitioning or implementing offices to fully observe the provision of 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 04-2016 pertaining to documentation and posting requirement.

4) The office concerned to submit a justification letter indicating the reason/s why the noted liquidated damages were not imposed, for us to further evaluate and recommend other possible actions.
observations.managementAction:
The management strictly mandated the department concerned to follow the provisions of RA 9184

PACCO ensured the completeness of the supporting documents and that no succeeding payments will be made if no clear data cited on the NOA and PO agreements between parties

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the concerned offices to comply with the provisions outlined in Item 4.5 of DILG MC No. 2020-142 and Item 4 of DBM LBC 126 to ensure that mandated financial documents are completely and correctly posted in the Full Disclosure Policy Portal of the Provincial Government of Davao del Norte.

We recommended that the Governor direct the offices concerned to revisit the existing relief operations system and adopt measures to expedite the delivery of assistance to target beneficiaries, not only for this COVID-19 crisis but as part of the Province’s relief goods operation in times of calamities, 

We also recommended that management ensure that the delivery, repacking and distribution of goods must be synchronized so that response in an emergency will be done promptly without sacrificing the existing procedural controls.
observations.managementAction:
PSWDO as the lead department in disaster response in conducting relief operation in times of calamities are re-oriented on systems and protocol in distributing of relief goods, proper documentations and existing provisions to maintain/enhance our internal control. 

Hired additional personnel with defined roles and responsibilities to ensure proper recording, inventory and monitoring of disaster relief operation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the:

Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, in the preparation of LDRRM plan, ensure that only items authorized to be charged in the said fund are included in the budget; and

b) Provincial Budget Officer and the Provincial Accountant ensure that only expenses related to disasters and in line with the PPAs under the 5% LDRRMF are processed for payment.

We recommended that management fully adhere to the guidelines set in LBC 119 and its subsequent amendments, in the utilization of LGSF -FA. If due to necessity, utilization of this fund other than for the approved PPAs is warranted, secure first the approval of DBM.

Likewise, submit a justification why the proscribed expenditures were charged against the fund for us to make a further evaluation on the matter.

observations.managementAction: The province ceased the granting of cash to disbursing or accountable officer for the purpose other than those provided in COA Circular 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Provincial Governor to direct the officers concerned to conduct inventory taking of the subject inventories and submit the report to the PACCO for reconciliation. Likewise, any discrepancies noted shall be examined and traced to the source documents, and effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at an accurate balance of Inventories.
observations.reason: The province is on-going to conduct physical inventory count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct the PGSO to conduct a physical inventory and submit the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in accordance with the rules and regulations provided by COA. Reinforce the Inventory Committee and emphasize the meeting of the legal deadline.
observations.managementAction: The province submitted the RCPPE on January 28, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Governor direct:

The PEO to submit the necessary supporting documents of the above-mentioned completed projects to the accounting department through PGSO. 

The offices concerned to evaluate the current system relating to preparation and submission of the PCRs to PACCO to avoid the delayed closing of the Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts once the project is already completed.

The accounting department to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset account if the necessary documents are already at hand. Provide the appropriate depreciation expenses if the asset is subject to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The PACCO partially closed the other projects from CIP to its specific accounts and its corresponding depreciation.
observations.reason: Reiterated and updated in CY 2021

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management directs the officials concerned to review all the projects undertaken and to be undertaken by the Province including the projects noted in our observation and come up with a concrete action to address the deficiencies noted. 

We also enjoined the offices concerned to address the root cause of the delay/non-implementation- the Provincial Engineering Office (PEO) if it pertains to the preparation of POW and other technical matters, to fast track its preparation, the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) if it pertains to procurement matter, and other agencies tasked to implement the project in case of fund transfers. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) will closely monitor and evaluate the status of implementation of projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated and updated in CY 2021

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Governor to direct the offices concerned to hasten the implementation of the projects under the Special Education Fund. Strictly observe the mandatory provisions of the procurement law particularly pertaining to “failure of bidding” to avoid the occurrence of the same situation. Review as well the Program of Work (POW) to arrive fair costing for both the government and the contractor/s.

The PSB shall prioritize projects/programs in accordance with Section 100.c of RA 7160 by incorporating these projects in the Annual Budget instead of in the Supplemental Budget only.
observations.managementAction: Strict adherence and compliance to the enumerated audit recommendations
observations.reason: Completed construction of classrooms

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the PACCO to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001.   Submit the lacking documents noted to avoid suspension in the audit of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The PACCO immediately revised their checklist on granting and liquidation of cash advances to various NGOs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Provincial Governor to direct the concerned officers to adopt an effective monitoring scheme to track the status of the funds transferred to Implementing Agency (IA). Corollary to that, adopt a policy or enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) to oblige the IA to submit liquidation report upon completion of the project otherwise, similar grant will not be released if the IA has an outstanding balance. In the case of financial assistance given to various LGUS under a state of calamity, issue a demand letter to remind them of their obligation to submit a liquidation report so that accounting records are updated and accurate.
observations.managementAction:
LFC and PPDO conducted joint review of PPAs.

Copy of AIP CY 2019 is forwarded to COA Provincial Office per received copy dated February 1, 2019.


All PPAs funded from the 20% DF included in the ensuing AIPs are capital expenditure.
observations.reason: Reiterated and updated in CY 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Governor to enjoin the PSWMB members to fully accomplish their functions and responsibilities pursuant to the provisions of Section 11 of RA 9003. Emphasis is given to the conduct of regular meeting concerning the Province’s 10-Year Solid Waste Management objectives.
observations.reason: The PSWMB conducted series of meetings on 2018-2019 which resulted to a submission of Provincial ESWM Plan for 2020-2029.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to reconstitute the agency’s GPFS in accordance with the structure and composition provided for in the MCW-IRR. It shall lead in setting up appropriate systems and mechanisms to ensure the generation, processing, review, and updating of sex-disaggregated data or GAD database to serve as basis in performance-based and gender-responsive planning and budgeting. 

The Province, through their Local Planning and Development Offices (LPDO), shall spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD  database to serve as the basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. The GAD database, which can either be manually operated or developed through software, shall form part of the overall management information system (MIS) of the LGU. For guidance on how to set up and maintain the agency’s GAD database, please refer to Annex B of PWC/DBM/DILG/NEDA JMC 2013-01 or if warranted, cause the PDDO staff to undergo proper training about GAD database development and maintenance.
observations.managementAction: Compliance to audit recommendations
observations.reason: Reiterated and updated in CY 2021

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct:

The offices concerned to review and assess the overall performance of the existing LEEs that shall help the Province in determining the major causes of its losses. Develop a Business Plan that can guide the management in the conduct of LEEs operations heading towards the achievement of the goal “to make the operations of the LEE financially self-sufficient and eventually profitable.” A well-planned and efficiently run LEEs can contribute to good local governance since they: (a) ensure that local public utilities and services meet constituency needs; (b) publish annual financial performance reports that provide transparency and accountability; (c) provide opportunities for citizen involvement; and (d) contribute to LGU revenue and resource mobilization because they could even generate modest financial surpluses through judicious control and tracking of LEE revenues and expenditures if properly set up and operated.

The offices concerned, particularly the PEEDO, to Conduct Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review (SAFSSR) of each LEE and prepare SAFSSR Report. The SAFSSR is in compliance with Section 316(h) of the LGC which requires the LFC to conduct a semi-annual revie w and general examination of cost and accomplishments against performance standards. For detailed procedures and format of the report, please refer to the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises issued by DBM through Local Budget Circular No. 11.
observations.managementAction: The management completely implemented all enumerated audit recommendations
observations.reason: Per APPSI, several target to address the recommendation were not fully attained such as a. Availment of Malasakit/MAIP funds for excess billing of patients; b. Established DAVNOR Pharmacy as the sole procuring entity to supply the needs of the hospital; c. Establishe computerized system of inventory; d. Hospital equipment in relation to the UHC; d. improved availment of MALASAKIT, MAIP with the guidance and collaboration of PSWDO; e. Conduct Physical inventory of supplies and materials by the DVANOR Pharmacy staff.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct the Provincial Service Officer (PGSO)-Warehouse, through the PGSO   to update the Stock Cards, and ensure proper piling of construction materials in the warehouse. 

Likewise, the Information Technology Division, under the Office of the Provincial Administrator, shall be instructed to prioritize the development of an Inventory Control System on the receipts, keeping, and issuances considering the significant quantity and amount involved of construction materials used in the implementation of projects by ADMINISTRATION.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Control System is one of the components of the module develop by the ITO.
observations.reason:
Per validation the PGSO partially 
use the excel format to comply the audit observation.

Other PASIMS module to be prioritized and waiting for the PADO IT personnel to create the system.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct the PGSO-Warehouse, through Provincial Government Services Office (PGSO), to properly maintain and update the Stock Cards in order to determine the balance of construction materials inventory as at year-end.

Likewise, instruct the Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) to maintain a Subsidiary Ledgers for each item of construction materials which shall be reconciled with the Stock Cards maintained by the PGSO.

Finally, management shall install controls that safeguard the utilization of construction materials, including the monitoring, accounting and reporting of any excess construction materials of completed projects which could be used for another priority project of the Province.
observations.managementAction: Stock cards on Construction materials are kept updated

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Provincial Governor to require the Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) and Provincial Economic Enterprise Development Office (PEEDO) to develop control mechanisms and monitoring tool to ensure the strict compliance with the guidelines on the disposition of “PhilHealth Capitation Fund”, as provided under PhilHealth Circular No. 40, s. 2000, as amended by PhilHealth Circular No. 16, s. 2004 dated July 12, 2004.
observations.managementAction:
Adherence to the mandated administrative costs that shall not exceed 20% of the PCF;
No charges shall reflect under the Philhealth Capitation Fund and no Activity Design shall be routed for approval for the same purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall strengthen the inventory system controls at the designated Storage/Warehouse of the LDRRM prepositioned goods by: a) requiring the maintenance of an updated Stock Cards for each items, b) preparing and conducting periodic inventory of stocks, c) monitoring of the stocks condition, d) periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records, and e) other necessary controls.
observations.managementAction: The designated storage/warehouse of the LDRMM complied the maintenance of stock cards for each item and monitor the stocks condition.
observations.reason: The designated storage/warehouse conducted periodic inventory of stocks and periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Provincial Governor to direct the Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) and Provincial General Services Office (PGSO) to immediately reconcile their records in order to establish a correct balance of undelivered Cements. Accordingly, require the concerned supplier the immediate delivery of undelivered items.

Likewise, the PACCO shall be instructed to ensure the completeness of supporting documents as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payment is made to supplier of Cements.

Moreover, the PGSO shall be reminded to see to it that all procurement of Cements, especially those procured under cash basis, are properly accounted and recorded in the stock cards. 

Finally, management should stop the practice of advance payment to suppliers of “Cements” pursuant to Section 88 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Already complied with the audit recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor directs the Provincial General Services Office (PGSO) to properly accomplish the Purchase Order (PO) complete with the necessary data/information in accordance with Section B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated 15 August 1996.

Likewise, it shall be instructed to strengthen the controls on the preparation and approval of PO to improve process efficacy.
observations.managementAction: The province p complied the necessary data/information pertaining to POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor requires the Provincial Human Resources and Management Office (PHRMO) and the Provincial Accounting Office (PACO) to closely coordinate, and must perform a periodic reconciliation of their records/reports, in order to facilitate timely remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums and loan amortizations.  Accordingly, incurrence of unnecessary delays and possible interest/penalties which might be unjustly imposed upon the concerned HDMF members/employees on late remittance could be avoided.
observations.managementAction:
The reconciliation between PACCO and PHRMO was done.

One of the causes of the delay of the remittances was due to discrepancies of the personal data of the employee between the records of the PHRMO and the Pag-IBIG. To prevent this issue, PHRMO have required the submission of pertinent documents to ensure the accuracy on the personal data of the newly hired employees. Further, they have also implemented the “No MID No Payroll Policy”.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor directs management to adhere strictly the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. 

Likewise, require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to monitor  and evaluate the use and disbursement of the LDRRMF based on the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan, pursuant to Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121, otherwise known as “Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010”.  

Finally, the corresponding continuing appropriation balances of completed infrastructure projects and procured PPEs are considered savings, and shall be available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP, pursuant to Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated 12 September 2012.
observations.managementAction: The PDRRM have evaluated the said continuing appropriation and allotment balances and presented to the PDRRM Council for approval of re-appropriations. The Provincial Development Council included the changes in the Revised AIP. An amount of Php5,593,916.00 have been re-appropriated through Supplemental Budget No. 3.

The district hospitals have hired new personnel and re-oriented existing personnel to implement the controls recommended by the Audit Team: a) delegated authority from Chief of Hospital to Administrative Officer V; b) segregated the functions for processing, reviewing, recording, custody and approval by assigning one personnel per function; c) maintained stocks cards; d) liaison officer ensures that documentary requirements are correct and complete; e) transactions were verified by an Inspectorate Team; and, f) accounting and PEEDO Admin personnel were tasked reconciliation of records and data.
observations.reason: Validation and evaluation made by the Audit Team disclosed that the recommendations were already implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require its project team to conduct regular inspection and validation of projects implemented under the Performance Challenge Fund and require barangays concerned for feedbacks/comments regarding the utilization of existing and newly constructed materials recovery facilities.
observations.reason: The MRFs were still not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that CGD Management instruct its CENRO to conduct a Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) at source as a top priority in CY 2020 and ensure that it will be conducted every two (2) years as stated in the Ten-Year ESWMP.
observations.reason: WACS at source was placed on-hold due to the Pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that henceforth when the pandemic restrictions would loosen up, the Management conduct a regular physical inventory of the property of the City and eventually prepare and submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period as required by rules to allow periodic reconciliation of property records and assure the accuracy of reported PPE balances in the financial statements.

Management’s Comments:
Coordination with the CGSO will be undertaken by the CAO to reconcile the recorded PPE accounts and their corresponding amounts as presented in the financial statements.
On the other hand, the CGSO and the Inventory Committee will request from the City Mayor for issuance of a Memorandum directing all Property Supply Officers and Property Custodians to conduct physical inventory count of all PPEs of the City, including those that are being used by offices of national agencies. The inventory count will commence in March 2021 to enable them to submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period and to arrive in a more accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that, henceforth when the pandemic restrictions would loosen up, the Management:
Require the CGSO and other custodians of supplies to undertake the actual physical inventory every year and to submit inventory reports to the Auditor;
Instruct the Inventory Committee to formulate strategies or techniques in the conduct of physical count during a pandemic or similar situation; and
Enjoin all stock custodians and the CAO to perform the reconciliation of the inventories with the recorded balances in the books.

Management’s Comments:
The CAO will provide the List of Inventories to the CGSO and inventory custodians of the different offices to reconcile and verify the accounts and amounts as recorded in the books of accounts.
Moreover, thru a memorandum, the CGSO will inform all custodians of supplies to closely coordinate with them, together with CAO, on the proper and actual physical inventory and to submit the required reports every year.
The City Inventory Committee on its 1st regular meeting this CY 2021 will include the agenda on the formulation of strategies or techniques in the conduct of physical count during a pandemic.
The management will see to it that all property/stock custodians reconcile every end of the month with the CGSO and CAO their respective inventories on hand against the balances per books of accounts.

We recommended that the Management reminds its CAO to –
Strictly observe the rules and regulations pertaining to the recording of the receipt of cash donations in the Trust Fund books using the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF”; and
Make the necessary adjustments to correct the entries made for the donations recorded in the “Other Payables” account.

Management’s Comments:
The CAO will prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the entries according to the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that the CGD Management direct its:
BAC and BAC Secretariat to post the complete copies of the bidding documents on both CGD and PhilGEPS’ websites, specifically for:
goods and services, and infrastructure projects - from the time the invitation to bid is first advertised/posted;
consulting services - eligibility documents shall be made available from the time the request for expression of interest is first advertised/posted and the bidding documents, from the determination of the short list; and
the details where the complete bidding documents can be secured and downloaded.
CITC and the BAC Secretariat to make the posted copies of the bidding documents available for viewing and downloading for audit purposes;
BAC Secretariat to update on the CGD’s website the minutes of pre-bid conferences; and post the NTP and the related approved contract at CGD and the PhilGEPS’ websites within fifteen (15) calendar days from the issuance of the NTP;
BAC, BAC Secretariat and Head-BAC TWG for Civil Works to ensure that a PhilGEPS printout/screenshot copies of the posted complete bidding documents, NTP and approved contract to be attached to the disbursement voucher; and
CITC to include a tab or any information for bidding documents, NTP and approved contract in www.davaocityportal.com under BAC postings menu.

Management’s Comments:
The BAC for Civil Works including its TWG and Secretariat will undertake the following actions:
Posting of the complete set of tender documents for Batch 01-2021 and Batch 02-2021 on the PhilGEPS and the City Government Portals. Also, this will be done for the succeeding batches.
Uploading of the Minutes of the Meeting and Notices of Award (NOA) for CY 2020 onto the Government Portals. The secretariat will continue doing such for the information of concerned parties.
Currently, the secretariat is still uploading the NTPs for the year 2020, while the NTPs issued for January 2021 were already posted.
The screenshot printout will be attached to DVs to serve as proof of posting of the tender documents, NTPs, and contracts on the PhilGEPS portal.
There were times that uploading onto the PhilGEPS Portal took almost a day to complete due to slow posting facility and sometimes the portal is inaccessible. These were the common issues often encountered by the secretariat.
All uploaded documents in the City Government Portal are available even after the deadline of the submission of bids for audit purposes.
The BAC will request additional personnel to ensure full compliance with the AOM and comply with the provisions of the law in promoting transparency in the conduct of procurement.

We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
DCDRRMO – Strictly monitor the receipt and utilization of cash donations to attain the intended purposes of the donors within the prescribed period;
CSWDO – Expedite the utilization of the donated funds for emergency relief and rehabilitation assistance to provide the supposed benefits to the beneficiaries; and
CAO – Remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized funds if there are no plans to use such funds for the benefit of the disaster victims pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.

Management’s Comments:
The CAO sent communication letters to CSWDO and DCDRRMO requesting the action plans on the utilization of funds. Once the responses are received, necessary steps will be undertaken following the COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
DCDRRMO – Submit a Supplemental LDRRMFIP including the complete list of PPAs to be funded from the grants and donations; and
CAO – Henceforth, avoid utilization of cash donations without the necessary supporting documents, otherwise any charges against the fund could be subjected to suspension in audit.

Management’s Comments:
Concerning the disbursements made amounting to P17,065,000.00, the CAO requested from the City Mayor’s Office a copy of the Project Designs as support document.

We recommended that henceforth, an office order be issued by the head of office or her representative, authorizing the personnel required to report for work during ECQ to support their claims for hazard pay consistent with AO No. 26 and DBM BC No. 2020-01.

Management’s Comments:
During the virtual exit conference, the HRMO Head acknowledged the audit finding of the audit team. He commented that they have requested the employees concerned to report for work during ECQ even without the required prior authority due to the instant exigency of service. But assured that in cases where a similar event might happen, they will prepare the necessary prior authority to report for work.
In his written reply, the HRMO Head informed that a Memorandum dated 27 March 2020 was issued adopting a work from home scheme for all city government officials and employees.  Said Memorandum indicates the provisions that employees must be on-call from Monday to Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm and identified offices that perform vital functions shall still be on full operation.  Hence, no prior authority or office order to work was prepared by HRMO or respective departments/offices for said purpose.

We recommended that the CGD Management direct the offices concerned to abide by the documentation provided in the DILG MC 2020-079 on the use of DCPOC funds for COVID-19 related PPSAs.

Management’s Comments:
The team was informed by the City Planning and Development Coordinator, as the Chairperson of POPS TWG, that the office concerned will be compliant with the needed documentation as provided by the DILG MC. Also, they will closely coordinate with the City DILG Director, as the secretariat of DCPOC, on the proper guidance of the DILG MC. And more importantly, that all DCPOC funds used for COVID-19 related PPSAs shall be utilized only with the required processes and approvals as needed and warranted, taking into account the latest issuances.
The City Budget Officer also commented that they will faithfully observe and comply with the provisions on the policy content and guidelines on the use of POPS fund for COVID-19 related PPSAs. Thus, the documentary requirements for CY 2021 of annual and supplemental budgets will be provided and submitted to the audit team.

We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
CGSO – Submit the revised APP for validation by the Audit Team whether or not all procurements, undertaken in response to the Covid-19 outbreak, were based on the revised APP; and
CBO & CAO – Ensure that all disbursements pertaining to procurement have been duly supported with an updated and approved APP consistent with the budget and revised modes of procurement.

Management’s Comments:
The CAO together with the CBO will coordinate with the CGSO in the preparation and submission of the approved revised APP.
The management assured the Audit Team that henceforth before allowing any disbursement/procurement of goods and services, an APP or updated APP must be secured especially in the case of COVID-19 related expenditures.

We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
CGSO – Ensure that required supporting documents for all procurement of goods are complete following the existing rules and regulations, and submit copies of AIR of deliveries of goods within 24 hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct a timely review and validation;
BAC – Comply with the requirement on the submission to COA copy of perfected purchase orders and its supporting documents irrespective of amount within five working days from issuance thereof; and
CAO – Ensure that all claims are supported with complete and proper documents before processing for payment to avoid suspension in audit.

Management’s Comments:
The “no remarks” on the AIR signifies complete delivery of items. The CGSO only puts remarks on the AIR in the event of partial deliveries.
The heavy influx of papers submitted due to the temporary cessation of the Executive and Legislative offices’ operations caused the said Office to overlook the POs with no PR numbers and date of the “conforme”.
A more thorough review of the documents shall be undertaken in future procurement activities.
On the other hand, the CAO agreed to the recommendations given for the proper procurement of goods under Trust Fund – Donations/DRRMF and Crisis Intervention Program. Thorough checking of supporting documents will be observed and monitored that funds will be disbursed following the existing government budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The AIR shall be filled in with data whether complete or partial delivery in accordance with prescribed instructions on how to accomplish the form.

We recommended that henceforth, the management direct its concerned CMO staff/representative, who negotiated with suppliers and/or the BAC on the procured goods totaling P309,937,794.13, to obtain/submit the required appropriate delegation authority from the HoPE, in accordance with Item No. 3 of the GPPB Resolution 03-2020 dated 9 March 2020.

Management’s Comments:
The BAC explained that this happened during the height of the ECQ, wherein the operations of the executive and legislative offices of the City were temporarily put on hold. A verbal authorization from the HoPE was given to the CMO Chief-of-Staff, to directly negotiate with the technically and financially capable suppliers who can deliver the goods needed by the City. However, due to the given circumstances and considering that the City needs immediate action from its local government, the BAC may have overlooked the provisions under GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020. Moreover, the BAC commits to submit to the recommendation given by COA.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The audit team acknowledged the duty and responsibility of the BAC particularly at the time of the pandemic. We also understood the urgency required by the local government to attend to the needs of the City. However, we could not just overlook the laws, rules, and regulations in the procurement of various goods and services, especially in issuing, documenting, or communicating the delegation of authority to ascertain whether it was proper and for the timely and responsive conduct of procurement activities. The Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184), being a law, must always be observed. Likewise, relevant issuances from the GPPB should be firmly adhered to by the management.
Consequently, the audit team stood on its recommendation for the management to strictly comply in accordance with the GPPB Resolution on the matters of delegation of authority to award a contract and the authority to negotiate with suppliers.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and BAC Secretariat to:
Ensure that all procurement of highly needed supplies, materials, equipment, utilities, telecommunications, and other critical authorized services under RA 11469 adhere to the documentary requirements set forth under GPPB Circular 01-2020.
Observe strict compliance to Section 9 of GPPB Circular 01-2020 and GPPB Advisory 05-2020.

Management’s Comments:
The BAC Chairperson on Goods and Head of BAC Secretariat informed the audit team that they have enrolled in the GPPB website on 03 February 2021 for the posting of NOA and POs for the procurements made under Bayanihan Grant for Cities and Municipalities (BGCM).  However, as of 15 February 2021, the GPPB has not verified or confirmed the account of the BAC.
The BAC and the CGSO shall comply with the recommendations and shall ensure that all subsequent procurements under RA 11469 adhere to the reportorial and documentary requirements provided under GPPB Circular 01-2020.

We recommended that the Management reminds the DCDRRMO/CSWDO/CAO to:
Ensure the strict compliance with the provision of EO No. 51, whereby the NFA shall be the supplier of rice that the government entity needs in connection with its functions unless a waiver is issued by the NFA;
Submit to the Audit Team the required waiver for the procurement of rice which was not sourced from the NFA; and
Enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with NFA to facilitate the rice procurement arrangement.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant will submit a copy of the waiver from the NFA once received from the City Mayor’s Office.

We recommended that the Management remind its CGSO and BAC to strictly comply with the procurement process and conditions mandated by the RIRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2007 in resorting to Agency-to-Agency mode of procurement; and
We also recommended that the Management remind its CAO to ensure that relevant documents necessary for auditorial review are submitted to justify the use of Agency-to-Agency procurement.

Management’s Comments:
To ensure that the necessary supporting documents are complete, the CAO will coordinate with the CGSO and BAC before payments are made.
The Office will give special attention to additional requirements needed in the Agency-to-Agency mode of procurement in the succeeding transactions.
Also, the OIC-CGSO and Chairperson, BAC-Goods commented that during the months wherein the City of Davao was put under ECQ, the City procured grocery packs through Emergency Cases. However, the suppliers could not deliver the sacks of rice due to its non-availability. The BAC could no longer find a supplier capable of delivering the required quantity of sacks of rice which compelled the City to procure from the NFA.
To resort to procuring the rice from another government agency connotes that the mode of procurement to be undertaken should be Agency-to-Agency. But, due to the urgent need and taking into account the strict “stay home” policy and closure of establishments leading to unemployment of most of the citizens, the BAC unintentionally allowed the procurement thru such modality without acting in accordance with the provisions under RA9184, and thus coursed the said procurement through Emergency Cases.
Nevertheless, the BAC and CGSO will comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Management remind its responsible offices:
CGSO – Ensure that required supporting documents for all procurement of goods are complete following the existing rules and regulations, and submit copies of AIR of deliveries of goods within 24 hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct a timely review and validation;
BAC – Comply with the requirement on the submission to COA of copy of perfected purchase orders and its supporting documents irrespective of amount within five working days from issuance thereof, and adhere to documentary requirements stated in the GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020. Require the BAC Secretariat to post all procurement opportunities, results of bidding and related information in the PhilGEPS bulletin board, agency’s website and any conspicuous place in the premises of CGD; and
CAO – Ensure that all claims are supported with complete and proper documents prescribed in COA Circular Nos. 2009-002 and 2012-001 prior to processing of transactions for payment.

Management’s Comments:
The CAO explained in their letter reply to the COA regarding the attachments of other documents in lieu of required documents on the above transactions: (1) For the grocery packs, each household/beneficiary received three (3) packs of goods that will total to 300,000 packs for 100,000 households and the issuance of certification that the same beneficiaries were recipients of the 2nd and 3rd distribution of grocery packs from the CSWDO; (2) For the food meals, the acknowledgment receipts signed by the facility personnel were attached instead of distribution lists for precautionary measures or to have no contact with COVID patients as certified by CMO; (3) For the construction materials, the Accomplishment Report with photos was attached to the paid voucher transmitted to the audit team.
Consequently, the BAC explained that the AIRs were prepared after the delivery of goods and services as the acceptance of items is done during the delivery and the inspection is conducted after the delivery and upon request of the requisitioner. As for the NOA, they posted some of the NOA on the CGD’s website, albeit scanning of the remaining NOA is still on-going. And a copy of the revised APP for the 1st semester of 2020 has already been submitted. Also, the BAC and the CGSO have already coordinated the preparation and posting of the documents as provided by GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020 and they shall comply with the reportorial requirements in all future procurement activities.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The audit team appreciated the explanation cited by the CAO in their letter. However, another missing prerequisite under Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases) was to justify the necessity of the procurement. The approval by the HoPE of the revised APP should be sufficient to confirm the existence and veracity of the grounds relied upon in the adoption of the said alternative mode of procurement as stated in the GPPB Resolution 03-2020. Unfortunately, the revised APP was not prepared and available at the time when the transactions were still in the process to support the procurement.
As for the response of the BAC, there is no issue as to when should the AIR be prepared. The issue here is that no AIR was furnished to the COA after the acceptance of goods and services. It turned out that the delivery and acceptance were made on the same day, except for the groceries from LTS – Retail Specialist, Inc. that was accepted on April 20, 2020. It took several months from the time of acceptance of deliveries of goods and services before AIR was prepared, and therefore presumed that a copy of AIR was not submitted within the prescribed period.
For now, the audit team acknowledged the initial actions made by the management and shall monitor the compliance with the above-mentioned recommendations. Still, we maintained our stand on the recommendation to adhere to the documentary requirements stated in the COA Circular No. 2012-001 and GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020.

We recommended that the management:
Require the BAC or the End-user to henceforth, submit the resolutions recommending the award of the contracts to the suppliers indicating the approval of the HoPE; and
Strictly adhere to the procedures of the procurement process, especially on the issuance of BAC Resolution recommending the award of the contract and the issuance of NOA by the HoPE.

Management’s Comments:
The BAC Chairperson on Goods reiterated the justifications submitted to the audit team on related AOMs that these transactions transpired during the peak of the pandemic and both executive and legislative offices were temporarily closed and the whole City was clueless on what will happen next. Considering the temporary closure of both legislative and executive offices brought about by the pandemic and leaving the local government perplexed on where the crisis will lead to, the BAC impulsively issued a single resolution intended for all procurement related to COVID-19. Nonetheless, the BAC committed to submit to the recommendations and be more cautious and attentive to the guidelines of the issuances by the authorized bureau, committee, and/or agency in all their future procurement activities.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The audit team acknowledged the management’s justifications and explanations as to the cause of this issue. Even so, we maintained our stand that the BAC should strictly adhere to the above-mentioned recommendations and we intended to monitor the management’s compliance thereof in the succeeding procurement activities.

We recommended that Management reminds its responsible offices, as follows:
CSSDO to –
carefully profile and assess all target TUPAD beneficiaries,
conduct thorough evaluation on the eligibility of sampled beneficiaries who are deemed unqualified and take appropriate action, and
explain why payment to ineligible TUPAD Beneficiaries should not be disallowed in audit;
Barangay/CSSDO to –
issue Sworn Affidavit of qualified recipients/beneficiaries instead of Barangay/ CSSDO Certification in future operations;
Barangay to –
post in the barangay hall all qualified recipients/beneficiaries of all assistances/aids extended to its constituents to promote transparency and accountability;
City Accounting to –
require submission of certification of displacement from company/ establishment before processing payment for the displaced workers; and
City Accounting/CSSDO/Barangay to –
facilitate refund of the amount representing overpayment.

Management’s Comments:
The program is designed to provide economic activity for the displaced/ disadvantaged worker for a period of 30 days. However, the City of Davao noted that such a short period is not enough for them to recover from the impact of displacement. The CGD is on the belief that by extending the project to the relative/s of the worker, it will lessen the impact of displacement. During the application, it was discussed that only the worker and the worker alone can select a beneficiary of his/her own choice. Hence, the CSWDO did not bother to question the choice/s of the workers having in their minds the beneficiary/ies they identified could help the displaced workers.
The Accountant has already notified the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO) on the requirements of Certificate of Displacement from company/establishment in the next transactions and refund of overpayment for listed beneficiaries.
Persons who received double compensation were already informed to return the money but no reply was received as of AOM reply date (11/4/2021).
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The team will monitor the implementation of the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
Lingap Para sa Mahirap Program of the City – Come up with a MOA by and between CGD and the funeral service provider and/or crematorium specifying the cost of services to be given to COVID-19 related deaths, and establish specific guidelines on the grant of such assistance patterned after the prescribed rules and regulations for uniformity and fairness as well as to avoid possible misuse of government funds;
CSWDO – Strictly implement the specific set of criteria in the evaluation of individuals considered to be indigent and in crisis situation brought about by socio-economic difficulties; and
CAO – See to it that payments for funeral assistance are supported with complete documents and are based on rules and regulations set forth under the DILG DOH JMC No. 1 Series of 2020.

Management’s Comments:
There was a possibility that assessment of indigence was haphazardly done because the funeral, burial, and cremation of those who died as a suspect, probable, or confirmed COVID-19 case shall be facilitated within 12 hours to avoid contamination. With this, the CSWDO is asking for indulgence on this lapse.
Moreover, the Office is cognizant that Certificate of Indigency is not merely for compliance that can be issued hastily. It carries a big responsibility on the part of the social workers as it plays a big role in the disbursement of public funds.
A constant reminder to the social workers on the proper issuance of Certificate of Indigency was initiated.
Consequently, the CAO appreciated the recommendations to provide adequate funeral assistance to indigent beneficiaries and to lessen the exposure of funds to misuse. The Office will also comply with provisions stated in the JMC.

We recommended that Management require the City Agriculture Office and City Veterinarian Office to:
Submit original copy of distribution lists signed by recipients/beneficiaries with complete information to support the actual distribution of goods; and
Validate the distribution sheets submitted by the barangays.

Management’s Comments:
The CVO was not able to assure the complete entry of the necessary information required in the distribution lists because its completion was facilitated by the respective barangay officials which is beyond the supervision of the office.
As of September 2020, the original and complete distribution lists signed by the recipients were already submitted to the City Treasurer’s Office as attachments to the DVs for payment to the suppliers of the goods. Thus, the Office could not provide the original copies of the lists to the Audit Team. However, scanned copies of the lists and certifications/acknowledgement receipts from the barangay officials are available in the office for reference.
Also, the office assured that the recommendations shall be followed in similar undertakings in the future.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team maintained its recommendations.

We recommended that the Management reminds the CAO/CBO to:
Implement the use of the Cash Donation Utilization Request form for every utilization of cash donation received, and prepare a separate Subsidiary Ledger (SL) per donor and per purpose for cash donations received amounting to P100,000.00 and above while those below P100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as “Various Donors” per purpose; and
Prepare the records on cash donations pursuant to the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2014-002, and submit the required monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations.

Management’s Comments:
The CAO prepared the necessary Subsidiary Ledgers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.  Also, the Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations for the month of January 2021 as required by the Circular was already prepared and submitted. The succeeding utilization of the cash donations will be strictly in accordance with the reporting guidelines provided by the said Circular.

We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
CGSO – Prepare and submit to the Auditor the Inventory List of All Procured Items using the prescribed format for the purchase of relief supplies in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
CSWDO – Coordinate with the CGSO in the preparation of the required report for being the department assigned in the stockpiling and distribution of food and non-food relief items.

Management’s Comments:
The CSWDO is currently coordinating with the CGSO to submit the required Inventory List of All Procured Items.
Also, the CGSO submitted the Distribution List, RIS, and SSMI to support the procurements of the grocery packs and will abide by the recommendations provided.
Auditor’s  Rejoinder:
Agencies are required to prepare the Inventory List using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that Management reminds its responsible offices -
CEO – ensure compliance with documentary requirements prior to submission of request to DBM for LGSF funded projects; and
CAO - comply with reporting and posting requirements in accordance with LBC No. 116.

Management’s Comments:
The Accountant provided the reporting timeline of the said project with the fund received last 10 June 2019. It was informed also that the City Accounting strived to adhere with the reportorial requirements of DBM LBC No. 116-A and full disclosures policy with their continuous commitment to transparency and compliance to guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
Direct its CTO to immediately transmit all the required source documents and/or paid vouchers to CAO to facilitate the recording of transactions.

Direct its City Treasurer to always observe timeliness and promptly address the cause/s of any delay because delay in the of documents to the City Accountant also delays the recording process, hence, affecting the completeness and accuracy of the CDG financial reports, particularly the balances of cash in bank and related income and expense accounts.

Enjoin the City Accountant to constantly follow-up with the City Treasurer’s Office and the bank regarding unrecorded transactions.

Instruct its Offices concerned to immediately facilitate the filing of a request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts, including unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

Direct its CCDO Head and staff to conduct thorough evaluation and assessment of the cooperatives applying for loans before granting to ensure collectability within the agreed terms and condition and regularly monitor the operations of cooperative-borrowers.

We recommended that Management check on the amount of bond and whenever necessary, increase their coverage with the Fidelity Bond of the BTR especially the liquidating officers/district treasurers who are handling large sums of money, turned over to them by the collecting officers, pending deposits to the bank.

We recommended that Management direct its CENRO to regularly monitor and immediately cause/facilitate the organization of operational BSWM Committees in every barangay pursuant to Sections 6 and 7, Rule VI of the IRR of RA 9003 for the effective implementation and enforcement of solid waste avoidance and volume reduction strategies, as well as segregation of wastes at the barangay level towards ensuring protection of public health and the environment.

We also recommended that the CGD Management direct the LSB and the City Engineer to strengthen its coordination vis-a-vis timely plans and projects for implementation; and maximize its existing manpower and/or capacitate available personnel, if necessary.
