observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Require the CENRO to conduct an actual physical count of all mobile garbage bins, determine the number of units according to capacity for those deployed in the barangays, including those that are damaged/unserviceable and those stored in the Davao City Motor Pool and submit the RPCPPE to the CGSO and COA;
Instruct the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees to monitor and maintain the mobile garbage bins deployed in their respective barangays and to report directly to CENRO and CGSO any damage or loss thereof; and
Oblige both CENRO and CGSO to facilitate immediate processing of the disposal of unusable/damaged mobile garbage bins determined upon physical count and receipt of reports from Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management reminds its responsible offices:
CAO – to observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories, and require the monthly submission of the RIS, SSMI and distribution list for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories; and
CGSO – to conduct a periodic physical count of inventory on hand and reconcile with accounting records to reflect the necessary adjustments for proper valuation of the inventory as of a certain date.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management reminds the City Accounting Office to make a necessary reclassification of the mentioned accounts, and ensure recording of all transactions in the appropriate accounts in the books to avoid any overstatement/ understatement.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management reminds its responsible offices:
CAO – to revert the unexpended balance of P5,153,212.46 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund which shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local Sanggunian, in compliance with Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
DCDRRMO – to closely monitor the implementation of the Programs/ Projects/Activities (PPAs) listed in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to ensure that the PPAs are implemented before the lapse of five years.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management require the LSB and the Acting City Treasurer to consider re-evaluating the amount invested in HYSA to be made available in the current account and direct the LSB to maximize the use of the available funds, and deliver more effective services pursuant to Section 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160 instead of investing the funds in HYSAs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that CGD Management:
Direct the CTTMO and other offices concerned to effectively implement its well-planned city-wide road clearing operation;
Direct the CPDO to monitor the implementation and ensure that the road development projects submitted for appropriation be used by the general public; and
Enjoin the City Council, CTTMO and other offices concerned to revisit Appendix V of Comprehensive Transport and Traffic Code of Davao City as regards the imposition of fines and penalties and make necessary upward adjustments as deemed appropriate.

Recommendations:
We recommended that CGD Management:
Require the LSB to refrain from appropriating personnel-related expenses associated with the Late Afternoon and Modified Work-Study Program;
Direct the City Accountant to immediately discontinue charging payment of salaries of DepEd personnel handling extension classes under Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses, and henceforth ensure that only allowable and/or valid expenditures are charged to the Fund, in compliance with existing laws, rules, and regulations; and
Deploy the remaining LSB-hired teachers to handle extension classes and hire additional teachers only when there is no available personnel from DepEd to handle such classes in accordance with the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Recommendations:
We recommended that CGD Management direct the:
CEO and CPDO to closely coordinate with other offices concerned and strictly require ownership documents as a prerequisite in the implementation of road development projects in subdivisions and villages; and
City Accountant to certify supporting documents as complete only, in Box A of the Disbursement Voucher, when the required deed of donation or a deed of sale of subdivision/village road lot was made in favor of CGD.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management reminds its responsible offices:
DCDRRMO – to see to it that any utilization of the LDRRMF-QRF is supported by all the necessary supporting documents i.e. the Local Sanggunian Resolution and Declaration of State of Calamity and submit the duly accomplished distribution list to support the appropriate adjustment in the books of accounts, and to avoid suspension in the audit;
CAO - to record in the books of accounts all procured relief goods as Inventory account “Welfare Goods for Distribution” while the issuance of the goods to the intended beneficiaries be recorded as expense account, with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and RSMI as support documents; and
CGSO – to submit the Inventory List of Items for DRRM indicating the procured relief goods to support the recorded amount of the account “Welfare Goods for Distribution”.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the personnel concerned to fast track the procurement and delivery of the needed computer equipment to effectively utilize the school computer laboratory of the Emilio Ramos National High School.

Recommendations:
We recommended that CGD Management:
Direct the LSB to ensure that funds allocated for SEF be budgeted and utilized in accordance with Sections 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 to attain the purpose for which the fund was created; and
Direct concerned officers to discontinue payments of expenses that are not chargeable against SEF, otherwise, may not be allowed in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that CGD Management direct the offices concerned to abide by the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017 in charging appropriate expenditures to the 20% DF.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Supply Officer submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and ensure that the required reports are properly accomplished.

Recommendations:
We recommended that CGD require CENRO to:
Promptly replace damaged, unserviceable and missing mobile garbage bins and hasten its deployment to the collection points in barangays as well as in parks, public schools while retaining units for use in CGDs special events;
Ensure that in the deployment of mobile garbage bins to the barangays, an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment form shall be accomplished to  document the receipt of bins by the barangay official from the CENRO Officer; and
Identify the mobile garbage bins which may no longer be needed, not necessarily unserviceable, then dispose them utilizing public auction or any of the other modes of disposal deemed appropriate, in accordance with prescribed procedures on the supply and property management in local government units.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management reminds its responsible offices:
DCDRRMO – to prioritize the construction or upgrading of evacuation facilities using the 70% Mitigation Fund consistent with the minimum standards set by the NDRRM Council;
CSSDO – to coordinate with the LDRRM Council through LDRRM Office in complying with the requirements/requisite listed in the above-cited provisions to attain a disaster-resilient evacuation center, and include in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and LDRRM Investment Plan those facilities not yet existing in the Los Amigos Evacuation Center but prescribed by the standard; and
CEO – to conduct annual inspection of the City’s evacuation centers, as well as, every after the occurrence of a hazard or disaster event.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the City Agriculture Office personnel concerned to evaluate the project implementation, conduct periodic monitoring, as well as training for farmer-beneficiaries on the use of the fermentation facility.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require its project team to conduct regular inspection and validation of projects implemented under the Performance Challenge Fund and require barangays concerned for feedbacks/comments regarding the utilization of existing and newly constructed materials recovery facilities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management conduct regular monitoring of the project to evaluate its effectiveness and sustainability.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the officials concerned exert extra effort to expedite the delivery of the lacking items in order for the intended beneficiaries to achieve the maximum beneficial use of the projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended that CGD Management instruct its CENRO to:
Conduct regular monitoring of the solid waste diversion operations; determine annually the approximate volume of wastes diverted from waste disposal facilities; and
Direct every Barangay Solid Waste Management Committee to support CENRO in its monitoring of solid waste diversion operations through its submission of SWM monthly reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended that CGD Management instruct its CENRO to conduct a Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) at source as a top priority in CY 2020 and ensure that it will be conducted every two (2) years as stated in the Ten-Year ESWMP.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management require the FMOD and CEE to:
Intensify collection strategies such as sending out monthly notices and conducting dialogues with delinquent lessees for settlement of unpaid accounts under compromise and installment agreements, otherwise resort to possible revocation of lease contracts; and
Impose necessary surcharges to ignite prompt payments and/or lessen the amount of uncollected income, heeding pertinent provisions of the said City Ordinance.

Direct its CTO to immediately transmit all the required source documents and/or paid vouchers to CAO to facilitate the recording of transactions.

Direct its City Treasurer to always observe timeliness and promptly address the cause/s of any delay because delay in the of documents to the City Accountant also delays the recording process, hence, affecting the completeness and accuracy of the CDG financial reports, particularly the balances of cash in bank and related income and expense accounts.

Enjoin the City Accountant to constantly follow-up with the City Treasurer’s Office and the bank regarding unrecorded transactions.

Instruct its Offices concerned to immediately facilitate the filing of a request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts, including unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

Direct its CCDO Head and staff to conduct thorough evaluation and assessment of the cooperatives applying for loans before granting to ensure collectability within the agreed terms and condition and regularly monitor the operations of cooperative-borrowers.

We recommended that Management direct its DCDRRMO Chief Operating Officer to promptly submit the LDRRMFIP with complete details of funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness, including the required list of PPAs for the utilization of Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommended that Management direct its concerned key staff to always see to it that the utilization of LDRRM Funds is supported with necessary documents particularly the required inclusion in the Investment Plan of a separate caption for the list of projects and activities that are charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended that Management direct its City Budget Officer, City Accountant and, DCDRRMO Chief Operating Officer to observe due care in the use of LDRRMF to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related PAPs.

We also recommended that Management direct its three key officers concerned to ensure that only allowable and/or valid expenditures are charged to the Fund, in compliance with existing law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the CGD Management require the Local School Board to fully observe the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 and Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160, in planning activities funded by the SEF.

We also recommended that the CGD Management direct its City Budget Officer and City Accountant to ensure that only allowable and/or valid expenditures are charged to the Fund, in compliance with existing law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management check on the amount of bond and whenever necessary, increase their coverage with the Fidelity Bond of the BTR especially the liquidating officers/district treasurers who are handling large sums of money, turned over to them by the collecting officers, pending deposits to the bank.

We recommended that Management direct its CENRO to regularly monitor and immediately cause/facilitate the organization of operational BSWM Committees in every barangay pursuant to Sections 6 and 7, Rule VI of the IRR of RA 9003 for the effective implementation and enforcement of solid waste avoidance and volume reduction strategies, as well as segregation of wastes at the barangay level towards ensuring protection of public health and the environment.

We recommended that Management to direct the City Planning and Development Coordinator and the City Development Council to regularly submit monitoring report on the status of implementation of PPAs as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan, in coordination with the City Engineer’s Office,  categorizing the projects as to its nature, status of completion, cost and funding source.

Direct its Accountant to require the DCW in-Charge and/or DSWO to submit attendance lists of children - feeding recipients under the Supplemental Feeding Program conducted for SY 2017-2018, duly certified and attached to the liquidation report of the DSWO; and

Direct its CSSDO to fast track the full implementation of the program during the school year period, so as not to require the children from reporting on summer vacation just for the feeding program.

Immediately submit the lacking project reports in order for the team to facilitate submission of the Annual Project Audit Report as required by the World Bank and; 

Strictly observe the reglementary period in the submission thereof in the succeeding accounting period in compliance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the CGD Management enjoined the concerned key officers to consider returning the P900 Million in HYSA to the City’s current account maintained for its 20% Development Fund; and henceforth use it to hasten or fast track the implementation of intended projects and programs.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of its concerned offices to monitor and expedite the implementation of the planned activities and projects incorporated in its LDRRMFIP, attain a disaster-resilient community and/or provide its essential benefits to the public.  

We also recommended that Management direct the Chief Operating Officer, LDRRMO, to conduct an effective planning, consider identifying the projects/ activities/ programs that can be fully implemented within the year without incurring any delay, before submitting the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the CGD Management require the LSB and other concerned divisions to reassess its processes and procedures affecting project implementation and facilitate immediate best effort to address those with problems on land title or ownership documents, to fast-track the completion of current and future projects.

We also recommended that the CGD Management direct the LSB and the City Engineer to strengthen its coordination vis-a-vis timely plans and projects for implementation; and maximize its existing manpower and/or capacitate available personnel, if necessary.

We recommended that the CGD Management direct its CPDO to closely coordinate with the respective barangays and other government offices concerned, and regularly monitor status to ensure that priority projects in the annual development plan are carried out in a timely manner and its purpose be achieved to the benefit of the people.

Require the contractor to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted to conform to the approved program of works or forfeit the performance security and consider blacklisting if the contractor will refuse to effect the necessary corrections.

Direct the CGD Inspection Team to conduct regular and strict monitoring and supervision to ensure that all contractors undertake the projects in accordance with the approved program of works.

Direct its concerned department to expedite the implementation of various programs, projects, and activities to prevent delay of the intended benefits.

Direct its Accounting Office to make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the appropriate account in recording the awards and incentives for fair presentation in the financial statements, and reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the source agencies.

Cause the return of any unobligated balances to the source agencies or remit the same to the National Treasury, especially those fund transfers whose projects and programs had already been completed.

We recommended that the CGD Management instruct its CENRO to promptly implement planned activities and projects in consonance with its Ten-Year Integrated SWM Plan to prevent undue delay and to maximize its potential benefits.  

We also recommended that the CGD Management direct its CENRO to revisit the SWM Plan and evaluate those activities and projects which are deemed not viable and find alternative means to achieve the identified objectives.

To request for putting of tarpaulin outside the building showing the name of Central 911 that is visible to the constituents needing assistance to prevent confusion as to the site of the satellite offices, and

To appeal for additional necessary equipment and vehicles such as ambulance to be distributed to the four satellite offices, computer units and telephones for PSAP and K9 dogs to ensure the full attainment of the vision and mission and to avoid any delays in serving the public. Moreover, the inventory records/reports should be regularly maintained and properly filled-up pursuant to the provisions of the New Government Accounting Manual indicating the condition of each supplies and equipment to prevent the inadequacy of supplies and equipment of each unit.

We recommended that Management direct its City Accountant to provide financial information on revenues vis-a-vis related expenditures incurred by each LEE, as well as maintain individual subsidiary ledgers for the LEEs, pursuant to Section 107 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the CGD Management instruct the concerned economic enterprise offices to be guided by the Manual, being developed by the DBM with the European Union, on the setting up and operations of local economic enterprises, to ensure that each LEE is relevant and self-sustaining.
Conduct an appraisal of donated properties to reflect their fair value measurement in compliance with PPSAS 23.

Require the Property/Supply Unit to prepare and submit the inventory list of all procured and donated items (Annexes P, P-1 and P-2) to the auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

Maximize the use of LDRRMF and implement programmed projects in the LDRRMFIP to efficiently manage and address eventualities for risk or emergencies due to occurrence of man-made or natural calamities.

Require the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to closely monitor the undertakings out of the LDRRMF so that projects, programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the City’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management are fully implemented.

We recommend that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office to deny collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste and strictly implement the use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers for specific types of wastes. CENRO should also strive to ensure that only residual wastes shall be disposed in the Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office monitor and facilitate the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required under Section 32 of RA 9003 for an efficient and effective collection and disposal of solid waste that will provide as an additional measure to mitigate health and environmental risks.
Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM to prevent possible loss of life, damage to government and private property, and loss of livelihood.

Consider training and the development of personnel a priority program to build the capacity in order to improve preparedness and response at all levels before, during and after disasters; and

Focus on the improvement of technical skills of the participants and team management through conducting more training allowing the exchange of experience and knowledge to develop coordination of disaster response.

The City formulate hiring plan of casuals/ contractuals/ job orders to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto.  Department heads should be able to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of employees under contracts/job orders.

We strongly recommended the installation of an Internal Audit Services (IAS) that shall be responsible for putting in place processes for  identifying, analyzing and managing risks; initiating policies and procedures  to address the risks that may misstate the balances of the accounts in the financial statements and the amounts in the budgetary reports and performance reports and ensuring the conduct of a continuous monitoring of internal control system so  that internal control remains attuned to the changed objectives, environment, resources and risks of the agency.

Conduct a physical count of all properties received by the City Government that may be donated not only by DOH but by other institutions and furnish the City Accountant with the inventory list and pertinent donation papers as basis of the latter in recording in the books of accounts the donated assets.

The recommended increase in bond coverage must observe the following guidelines on the increase in accountability as provided in Item 7.3 of the said Treasury Circular, to wit:
“7.3 Increase in Accountability – Any increase in the amount of accountability, resulting to an increase in the amount of bond shall be subject to increase in premium.
7.3.1 The applicant shall accomplish and submit General form 57A to the concerned DO/PO together with the following:
7.3.1.1 Special Order increasing the amount of accountability; and
7.3.1.2 List of bonded public bonded officers certified by the agency official in charge of Administrative and/or Finance department, service, division or unit.
7.3.2 The amount of bond premium shall be computed based on the increased amount of bond. The bond shall take effect upon the time of payment of premium on the new bond application.
7.3.3 The CTOO II concerned shall cancel the previously assigned risk number to the applicant and issue a new risk number to the new bond application.”

Review and update all leave cards and ensure correctness of leave balances; and
Record the earned leave credits on a monthly basis.

Require the BAC Secretariat to avoid generic minutes of the meetings conducted and to include the discussions on the readiness of the procurement at hand consistent with the Government’s commitment to promote transparency of activities in the procurement process as provided by the procurement law.

Promptly submit its LDRRMFIP with complete details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated 12 September 2012.

Avoid utilization of LDRRMF without the necessary supporting documents particularly the LDRRMFIP, otherwise, any charges against the fund in the absence of such document may be suspended in audit.
Make the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the unexpended balance of the Trust Liability – DRRM to the General Fund as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated 12 September 2012.

Create an inventory committee in order to accelerate the conduct of physical inventory of its supplies and properties pursuant to section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

Require the Property/Supply Unit to prepare and submit the inventory list of all procured and donated items (Annexes P, P-1 and P-2) to the auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

Prepare and issue ARE to the person having custody and control of the property/equipment classified as PPE to safeguard government resources, determine accountability and facilitate the accounting thereof.

Direct its BAC to be prudent in conducting the post-qualification procedure on the supplier with the Lowest Calculated Bid, as required in Section 34 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 – Objectives and Process of Post-Qualification.  

Upon delivery of the medicines to the procuring entity, require the pharmacist’s certification on the expiry dates to be presented and submitted also to the General Services Office (GSO) personnel for a simultaneous inspection with the delivered items and the latter to exercise due care in validating the documents and items inspected as to correctness of product name, quantity, and expiry date.

Submit the required ARE duly signed by beneficiary schools or end-users, and any relevant documents to support its distribution to actual end-users, to avoid possible audit suspension/disallowance of the subject transactions; and

Fully comply with the new guidelines on the use of the Special Education Fund pursuant to DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 particularly on the submission of requirements needed in the preparation of the Annual SEF Budget, including a soft copy of the DepEd-approved SIPs.

Coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development projects of the City to ensure that the resources are managed in the most effective and efficient manner to meet the needs of its constituents.

Seek authority from EMB-DENR to extend the project implementation period and to be authorized to spend the funds beyond the period set forth in the MOA, otherwise there is a need to return the fund to the source agency or national treasury, whichever is appropriate.

Re-evaluate the feasibility of project and determine if project implementation is still practicable, bearing in mind the surrounding issues causing the delay while also considering that project completion is already long overdue. Otherwise, return the fund to the source agency or the national treasury, whichever is appropriate. It is prudent that the fund be returned rather than being kept dormant in the accounts of the City Government.
Hasten the implementation of PAMANA projects and the utilization of funds thereof in order to deliver the expected benefits, which are long overdue, to the intended beneficiaries.

Maximize the use of LDRRMF and implement programmed projects in the LDRRMFIP to efficiently manage and address eventualities for risk or emergencies due to occurrence of man-made or natural calamities.

Require the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to closely monitor the undertakings out of the LDRRMF so that projects, programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the City’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management are fully implemented.

Remit to the National Treasury all unnecessary balances of special and trust funds maintained whose nature and purpose cannot be determined pursuant to DOF-DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 4-2012 dated September 11, 2012.

The management should faithfully comply with the National Building Code (PD 1096) of the Philippines and DILG-DPWH Joint Memorandum Circular No. 001 dated July 4, 2013 as pertains the remittances of the 5% share of DPWH and 15% share of OBO.

Enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare Procurement Monitoring Report for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Sec 12.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.  Also, furnish a copy of said report to the audit team.

Comply with Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 as well as Paragraph 1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated July 28, 2011 and August 25, 2011, respectively, for the procurement of Common-use Supplies.

We recommend that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office to deny collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste and strictly implement the use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers for specific types of wastes. CENRO should also strive to ensure that only residual wastes shall be disposed in the Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office monitor and facilitate the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required under Section 32 of RA 9003 for an efficient and effective collection and disposal of solid waste that will provide as an additional measure to mitigate health and environmental risks.
Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM to prevent possible loss of life, damage to government and private property, and loss of livelihood.

Require the City Accountant to immediately prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund monthly in order for the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the concerned offices on the required dates, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 -002.

Consider training and the development of personnel a priority program to build the capacity in order to improve preparedness and response at all levels before, during and after disasters; and

Focus on the improvement of technical skills of the participants and team management through conducting more training allowing the exchange of experience and knowledge to develop coordination of disaster response.

We recommend that the local chief executive direct all department heads involved in development projects to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the proposed programs and projects and to ensure that the allocated funds are optimally utilized for projects identified in the approved AIP, in accordance with the guidelines/policies prescribed under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the concerned officials be enjoined to review the availability of unobligated appropriations in the prior years. Afterwards, revert the unobligated/unexpended appropriations and re-appropriate the same to other development projects.

We recommended that the local school board enhance its budgeting functions by observing proper budgeting process guided by Rule XXXIV of RA 7160 because it affects the efficiency in allocating and utilizing resources. It also determines the success or failure of the local government in delivering support to basic public education, particularly, in building schools and facilities as one of the major priorities of the Special Education Fund. More so, it could prevent incurrence of unnecessary pre-termination charges arising from withdrawing placements under SSD and HYSA.

The members of the local school board should strengthen its coordination to be able to come up with a timely and inclusive budget and maximize the use of the Special Education Fund, in view of its mandate under RA 5447 and RA 7160, to enhance implementation rate and help address the perennial problem on classroom shortage in Davao City public schools.

Management should uphold the provisions in Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and RA 5447 governing the law on Special Education Fund.

 While those qualified LSB-paid teachers are not yet absorbed in the DepEd national plantilla, they must have been deployed to handle identified extension classes where necessary or assigned to existing extension schools. However, it is still strongly encouraged that qualified LSB teachers handling main classes be prioritized in the DepEd plantilla.

The City formulate hiring plan of casuals/ contractuals/ job orders to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto.  Department heads should be able to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of employees under contracts/job orders.

accelerate the conduct of physical inventory of its supplies and properties pursuant to
section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
We also recommended that Management require the Property/Supply Unit to
prepare and submit the inventory list of all procured and donated items (Annexes P, P-1
and P-2) to the auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15,
2014.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Property Officer always prepare and issue ARE to the
person having custody and control of the property/equipment classified as PPE to
safeguard government resources, determine accountability and facilitate the accounting
thereof.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct its BAC to be prudent in conducting
the post-qualification procedure on the supplier with the Lowest Calculated Bid, as
required in Section 34 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 – Objectives and Process of Post-
Qualification.
We also recommended that Management, upon delivery of the medicines to the
procuring entity, require the pharmacist’s certification on the expiry dates to be presented
and submitted also to the General Services Office (GSO) personnel for a simultaneous
inspection with the delivered items and the latter to exercise due care in validating the
documents and items inspected as to correctness of product name, quantity, and expiry
date.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to:
a. submit the required ARE duly signed by beneficiary schools or end-users, and
any relevant documents to support its distribution to actual end-users, to avoid
possible audit suspension/disallowance of the subject transactions; and
b. fully comply with the new guidelines on the use of the Special Education Fund
pursuant to DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 particularly on
the submission of requirements needed in the preparation of the Annual SEF
Budget, including a soft copy of the DepEd-approved SIPs.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Planning and Development Coordinator, together
with the Local Development Council, coordinate, monitor and evaluate the
implementation of the different development projects of the City to ensure that the
resources are managed in the most effective and efficient manner to meet the needs of its
constituents.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the BAC – infrastructure to award a contract only after the detailed
engineering investigation, survey and design for the project have been
sufficiently carried out as provided in the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
b. Require the City Engineer’s Office to completely perform detailed
engineering and survey function, as enumerated under Section 477(b)(4) of
the Local Government Code;
c. Direct the City implementing offices to coordinate with DPWH in the
implementation of infrastructure projects to avoid overlapping with the
national government funded projects;
d. Direct concerned offices to facilitate settlement of the road right of way issues
to avoid delay in project implementation; and
e. Review all plans and timetables to ensure that what is planned and targeted
are achieved and feasible during the year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned officials seek authority from EMB-DENR to
extend the project implementation period and to be authorized to spend the funds beyond
the period set forth in the MOA, otherwise there is a need to return the fund to the source
agency or national treasury, whichever is appropriate.

Recommendation:
We recommended that concerned officials re-evaluate the feasibility of project
and determine if project implementation is still practicable, bearing in mind the
surrounding issues causing the delay while also considering that project completion is
already long overdue. Otherwise, return the fund to the source agency or the national
treasury, whichever is appropriate. It is prudent that the fund be returned rather than
being kept dormant in the accounts of the City Government.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the offices concerned hasten the implementation of
PAMANA projects and the utilization of funds thereof in order to deliver the expected
benefits, which are long overdue, to the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management maximize the use of LDRRMF and
implement programmed projects in the LDRRMFIP to efficiently manage and address
eventualities for risk or emergencies due to occurrence of man-made or natural
calamities.
We also recommended that Management require the Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Office to closely monitor the undertakings out of the LDRRMF so that
projects, programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the City’s capacity for disaster
risk reduction and management are fully implemented.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management require the CENRO to:
a. Impose compliance with the minimum operating requirements for sanitary
landfills pursuant to Section 42 of RA 9003;
b. Direct their efforts towards the maintenance of landfill facilities such as
access roads, signs, lamp posts, toilets, and perimeter barriers; and
c. Expedite upkeeps of landfill equipment particularly the landfill compactor.
Remit to the National Treasury all unnecessary balances of special and trust funds pursuant to DOF-DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 4-2012 dated September 11, 2012.

The City Accountant should follow-up the supporting documents from DILG so that the transactions be recorded in the books and reflect the correct balances of cash and related accounts in the financial statements.

The management should faithfully comply with the National Building Code (PD 1096) of the Philippines and DILG-DPWH Joint Memorandum Circular No. 001 dated July 4, 2013 as pertains the remittances of the 5% share of DPWH and 15% share of OBO.

Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements all funds received as donations since they are regarded as public funds rightfully under the controllership of the City Government of Davao, subject to PPSAS 23, Sec. 63 of PD 1445, Sec. 307 of RA 7160, and the usual accounting and auditing rules.

Ensure that all cash advances are liquidated on the prescribed period. Also, update the audit team of the action taken as regards the liquidation of cash advances of Ms. Belinda Q. Hementera as she is still currently employed with the City Government, and on Ms. Maria Felisa C. Marques’ cash advances since she is no longer an employee of the city government.

We recommend that the concerned officials from City Accounting Office and City Mayor’s Office be enjoined to coordinate and require the recipient NGOs to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them. If necessary, impose sanctions to the delinquent NGOs, as provided in Section 9.0 of COA Circular 2007-001.

a. Conduct the physical count of supplies and materials, semi-annually, in accordance with existing rules and regulations to verify the accuracy of reported balances and submit the required report thereon.
b. Prepare and submit, promptly, their SSMI to the City General Services Office for proper consolidation and submission to the accounting office.

Enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare a Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Sec 12.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184. Also, furnish a copy of said report to the audit team.

Comply with Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 as well as Paragraph 1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated July 28, 2011 and August 25, 2011 respectively, for the procurement of Common-use Supplies.

Enjoin the concerned officials to strictly adhere the advertising and posting requirement of RA 9184, and make certain that the printout copies and certifications from BAC Secretariat are appended to the disbursement vouchers as required under Section 9.1 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Enjoin the concerned officials to strictly observe the requirement on posting of warranty securities as provided in Section 62.1 of RA 9184.

We recommend that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office to deny collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste and strictly implement the use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers for specific types of wastes. CENRO should also strive to ensure that only residual wastes shall be disposed in the Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Office monitor and facilitate the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required under Section 32 of RA 9003 for an efficient and effective collection and disposal of solid waste that will provide as an additional measure to mitigate health and environmental risks.

a. Ensure that all PAPs funded from the LDRRMF will support disaster risk reduction and management activities during the pre- disaster preparedness and post- disaster activities; and
b. Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM to prevent possible loss of life, damage to government and private property, and loss of livelihood.

Require the City Accountant to immediately prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund monthly in order for the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the concerned offices on the required dates, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 -002.

a. Consider training and development of personnel a priority program to build the capacity in order to improve preparedness and response at all levels before, during and after disasters; and
b. Focus on the improvement of technical skills of the participants and team management through conducting more training allowing the exchange of experience and knowledge to develop coordination of disaster response.

We recommend that the local chief executive direct all department heads involved in development projects to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the proposed programs and projects to ensure that the allocated funds are optimally utilized for projects identified in the approved AIP, in accordance with the guidelines/policies prescribed under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the concerned officials be enjoined to review the availability of unobligated appropriations in the prior years. Afterwards, revert the unobligated/unexpended appropriations and re-appropriate the same to other development projects.

a. Submit pertinent documents/reports showing that implementation of BUB projects was started within ten (10) working days upon the receipt of fund from DA-RFO XI and BFAR XI as stipulated in the contract. Otherwise, provide explanation as to why the required start date of the project, as provided in the MOA, was not complied.
b. For the four (4) projects that have lapsed implementation periods, seek authority, from the source agencies, to spend the funds beyond the agreed period. Otherwise, return the fund to the source agency or national treasury, whichever is appropriate.
c. Make certain that the projects are implemented in accordance with the MOA between the City Government of Davao, DA-RFO XI and BFAR XI.

Enjoin the officials of implementing offices to expedite the implementation of BuB projects and the utilization of funds thereof in order to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time.

Enjoin the concerned officials to observe prudence in the disbursement of BuB funds and avoid incurring “unnecessary expenses” which are not essential to the attainment of BuB projects and explain why the subject amount was necessary to the projects and why it should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the local school board enhance its budgeting functions by observing proper budgeting process guided by Rule XXXIV of RA 7160 because it affects the efficiency in allocating and utilizing resources. It also determines the success or failure of the local government in delivering support to basic public education, particularly in building schools and facilities as one of the major priorities of the Special Education Fund.

a. The City Treasurer, as member of the Local School Board, advises the board about the actual status of funds available should actual versus targets vary during the budget year so that the LSB can program the funds for additional projects earlier in the year.
b. The City Accountant must present in the SEF Utilization Report a reconciliation of Actual receipts versus Budget to ensure that total payments do not exceed appropriated amounts.

The members of the local school board should strengthen its coordination to be able to come up with a timely and inclusive budget and maximize the use of the Special Education Fund, in view of its mandate under RA 5447 and RA 7160, to enhance implementation rate and help address the perennial problem on classroom shortage in Davao City public schools.

a. Management should uphold the provisions in Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and RA 5447 governing the law on Special Education Fund.
b. While those qualified LSB-paid teachers are not yet absorbed in the DepEd national plantilla, they must have been deployed to handle identified extension classes where necessary or assigned to existing extension schools. However, it is still strongly encouraged that qualified LSB teachers handling main classes be prioritized in the DepEd plantilla.

a. Uphold the law governing the Special Education Fund. Limit expenses only to the priorities of SEF in Sec. 100 and 272 of the Local Government Code.
b. Discontinue the charging of honoraria to ALS teachers, facilitators, coordinators, monitoring and support staff using SEF funds as they may be deemed irregular in nature, therefore, disallowable in audit.
We recommended that the Local School Board thru the DepEd Division of Davao City submit explanation on the alleged payment of unnecessary double compensation.

The City formulate hiring plan of casuals/ contractuals/ job orders to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto. Department heads should be able to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of employees under contracts/job orders.
